One tiny variation in choice results in a major change in outcome. Kind of like the Butterfly effect.

As for the Horror aspect of it, when you combine the story, sound, and imagery, I found it quite chilling at times and sufficiently scary.  I am admittedly a wuss tho.

Usually I don't really write serious reviews, but I love this game so much. I've been playing it on and off for many years now and it is by far my favourite visual novel ever. The plot is so nicely put together and, while of course there are certain areas that feel lackluster, overall the experience is so worth it. The voice acting and animations, the artwork in the backgrounds, cgs, etc., is so gorgeous yet gruesome at times— I love it. I feel as if this game does not get as much appreciation as it deserves because it is a visual novel that doesn't fall under the "anime esc" style, but that doesn't change the fact that it's such a wonderfully well made visual novel and I'll always appreciate it a lot. I've replayed the story countless times, seen every route through to the end and every branch as well (or at least on my old account, which I sadly lost). I'd say that if you are a fan of visuals novels and horror/grotesque themes in general, that this game is so worth playing through. I most definitely recommend it!

The Letter is one of the largest, longest visual novels I played on here which is both a good...and a bit of a bad thing. While I recommend this game, I would strongly advise that the game is best for people who enjoy visual novels. I can't quite put my finger on why I wouldn't rate this as one of the best visual novels on here, but here I go.

Graphics; the graphics are fairly nice and I do appreciate how it goes with the voice-acting (which is, imo, the standout in terms of quality...maybe a bit overacted sometimes though). However, it never really impressed me, no hate towards the developers who put a lot of work into it! It is certainly more alive than most visual novels out there.

Music; a bit dull if I'm being honest. It is most memorable during the moments with the ghost/demon thing but that's about it. But that's more of a personal opinion. 

Choices; there are a number of choices and endings that allow for a whole lot of replays. Every character you interact with could potentially die depending on the choices you make which definitely make them consequential. However, it is a bit obvious what each choice will lead to (i.e. whether a character will like you or not) and that takes me out of the game. I'll talk more about this in the gameplay section.

Story; as a horror game, I believe it delivers fairly well as much as a visual novel can. There are definitely moments where I jumped from a scare and got nervous as hell yelling at my computer screen during tense moments. But more than anything, the story is about the characters' relationships with each other. Some characters will be driven away from each other, get together, or stay good friends. The relationships you build with each character is the big driving point with the story...outside of the whole cursed letter and demon woman thing.

Gameplay; majority of the game is just listening to conversations and making choices. There are some moments of intensity where you have to click your way out of a bad situation...but you can skip those gameplays. 

This leads to my one complaint of the game. While I enjoyed doing different play-throughs, especially when changing the fate of the characters, it is just too big and there are soo many possible endings. The skip button is here but its a bit of a disadvantage. A whole lot of times you'll make a different choice BUT the dialogue is EXACTLY the same as another choice but because you're technically in a different storyline, you have to click through all that dialogue you already heard. It gets especially aggravating if you're trying to find all the different possible endings. I also had problems where I use the skip button but because I have already done so much of the game, and there is a whole lot of story to get through, it trips out and crashed once even. 

Overall, I only recommend this game for those craving for another choice-driven game with a bit of scares here and there. It is enjoyable and I especially love the "villain" character but its not worth trying to get through all the endings because there are just so many possibilities for a game that is already larger than most visual novels. But take that as you will.

[h1]This review will contain spoilers. Please read at your discretion.[/h1]

If I could rate this game "neutrally" I would. It's neither fantastic nor terrible. But I will lean towards the positive recommendation since I did complete the game.

If you've seen The Grudge or The Ring, then you basically have the plot of this VN and know what to expect. Take the chattering dead girl from The Grudge and throw her into the basic plot for The Ring and it's spot on. Instead of a creepy video, it's been replaced by a primitive form of a "chain-letter". (The early 2000s wants its inbox spam back.)

The Letter is labeled as a horror, but it felt like the "horror" side of it was added in at the last minute. I expected going into this to be frightened, or at least jump in my seat quite often, but this wasn't the case.

The character development was good, but some parts felt like they dragged on needlessly for filler. Often, I forgot I had been reading a "horror" VN. I wanted to see more jump scares, more frightening moments, more blood and gore and supernatural events! The artwork was definitely there and could have supported so much more trepidation in the reader, but in the end it was lacking.

[h1]Positives[/h1]
+ 95% of the characters have amazing voice acting. A few sound a little strange and out of place.
+ Artwork is fantastic. Love the dynamic backgrounds and the animated bits and pieces to make it feel more interesting and alive.
+ Different decisions to choose from which directly impact the story. Positive for replayability as you can see how different choices affect the story in another playthrough. The addition of achievements give you another reason to do so.
+ The audio outside of the voice acting is great. Helped to bring life to the fictional world.

[h1]Negatives[/h1]
- Some scenes just dragged on and on which made me feel like I was mindlessly clicking to get through the dialogue as quick as possible. Character development is one thing, but reading about instant noodles for what seemed like an eternity just felt out of place.
- The "horror" scenes were too short and I felt like there was not enough of them.

[h1]Needs Improvement[/h1]
~ Most notably towards the last half of the game, some of the speech did not reflect the subtitles. Words were missed, and sometimes whole sentences missed audio, or the wrong audio was played entirely. Also noticed a few misspelled words in the journal.

My review does point out some not so positive aspects, but they are all subjective. If you are into the VN genre and want to go for something on the "spooky-but-not-quite-horror" side, then definitely give this a try. It had its ups and downs, but I do not regret my purchase.

A wonderful piece of branching VN. 10/10. Buy even at full price. Here's pros and cons:

Pros:
-Choices matter 
-Multiple protagonists 
-All playable characters are mature adults, no schoolboys
-Writing is almost perfect
-Full English voiceover, and most characters sound just great
-Really long, longer than most other VN's
-Marianne is the best waifu ^^

So-so:
-Art is average (imo)
-Not really a horror, more like a thriller/detective
-Unusual storypresenting (you will either hate it, or love it)

Cons:
-Sometimes too much water/chitchat in dialogues, but that's the common sin for 99% of VN's.


Verdict: 10/10. Buy even at full price. It is not a dating sim. Its one of most deep and trought-provoking VN's on Steam so far, on par with Octave Higher. Not to mention almost full freedom of choice that shape your playtrough and more than 100 endings total.

A nice visual novel that's fun to play but incredibly painful to complete lmao. It has an interesting story and plenty of interaction, which is great but horrible at the same time if you want to complete the flowchart/get all achievements (I was actually crying when I had to repeatedly skip through the entire story from the very beginning til the last chapter only to complete every story branch that was often just a tiny difference in the dialogue). 
I guess it's important to mention that despite being a horror game, the VN still has lots of emphasis on the characters and their relationships (which I occasionally found boring tbh) but I think that it's actually refreshing how the game makes all characters have some sort of unlikable flaws that make them realistic, plus many of the characters are really dramatic which makes it entertaining (the voice actors truly gave their best there lol). The art is flawless and while I agree with the popular opinion that the character sprites seem somewhat odd at first and need to get used to, the CGs are still very beautiful and easily have some of the best detailed gore I've seen in VNs/games so far. 

The main antagonist is overall a quite stereotypical/cliche ghost that is often seen in classic horror movies (based on her design and "performance") which I honestly enjoyed for that nostalgic feeling, yet I also liked how there is still a decent (implied) explanation for the ghost's actions. However I wish that the story had more flashbacks/detailed background information, especially for the ghost (since it's the most important plot part which I wanted to find out more about) but also all characters in general, since the reader often only gets a vague idea of what their past was like. 

Overall I loved playing this, especially since you could experience the plot through different perspectives and also freely impact the story (it's fun to just, be able to [spoiler]murder the entire cast [/spoiler]if you want to lol). I just believe it would've been better if that major effort rather went into going deeper in the lore, instead of creating that huge amount of story branches that often don't change the plot at all XD.

This a very specific visual novel, a person will either love it or not like it. Personally, I was in the camp of not liking it too much. 

The biggest factor being, it is hardly a horror visual novel. There are scary moments and jump scares, but I would not classify this as a horror visual novel. This is more a slice of life/drama VN with a splash of a haunting and murder mystery. If that is what you're looking for, you'll love this game! Unfortunately, I wanted something more on the scarier or gothic side (I loved the House in Fata Morgana, for example). 

Pros:
[list]
    [*] It's long
    [*] Branching choices/multiple endings
    [*] A variety of characters and personalities
    [*] Nice art and BGs
    [*] Some of the jumpscares get you good!
[/list]

Cons:
[list]
    [*] It's long
    [*] "Horror" sections are few and far between
    [*] You have to [b]REPLAY[/b] the same story seven different times through the POV of different characters (this is for a single playthrough, not multiple). There are obviously some changes in the POVs, but some sections are just the same/similar.
[/list]

Got my Steam-Key from the Devs, bc I supported the Kickstarter.

Visual novels started to bore me but then I played the early Demo of The Letter and was hooked. Now that the finished product is out I am more than pleased to have backed this project.

Note: As of writing this I am in chapter 5 of 7

The characters move as you talk to them, even the backgrounds are animated.... everything draws you into the world of the game. Spoken lines are voiced over and the Voice actors are great! 

The spooky parts made me jump more than once... starting with clicking "New Game" There are parts where you have to manage QTE's to survive which gives a nice change to the good old Visual Novel click-reading.

I got attached to the characters and really want them all to be alive and well at the end! As for endings... the Game has many. Individual character endings (good and bad) as well as romance ones and an overall "true ending". So if you wanna see EVERYTHING buckle up! It'll be a long ride.

The Game also has a branching tree graphic for every chapter so you can follow your missed decisions if you wanna replay.

Overall I can say Good Job Yangyang Mobile! I haven't been spooked this hard in a long time and I love it!

My rating : 9/10

This game is amazing! Probably the longest visual novel ever, according to its description it contains 700k words which is crazy. So many different situations which trigger each other and different endings. Since we play all 7 characters, we get to see each character's perspective in different situations which made the whole story even better than it is. And on top of that the story itself is very fun, creepy, not so predictive and simply beautiful. I also liked the characters and the way they look and talk, the aesthetic of the game is also pretty, the soundtrack is amazing and fits the scenes and atmosphere. The only thing I would add to this game is ability of deleting saves since it can get very crowded after many replays and all. But overall the game is awesome and definitely worth playing.

I watched PatrckStatic's partial playthrough of this game when it first came out. It was intriguing and I wanted more. Now, I've finally picked it up and I was not disappointed in the least. Different paths based on your choices from the current and previous chapters, The Letter has an interesting story and creepy atmosphere. Subtle romance, creepy horror, and an interesting storyline that will keep you glued to your screen for hours, I highly suggest this game for anyone who wants a well designed horror story.

Edit: So, 30+ hours in, I'm trying to get all of the achievements. Which, of course, means maaaany playthroughs. At first glance, I thought it'd be a piece of cake but after delving in further, I realize that there is soooooo much replay value here. Different actions REALLY do cause different storyline. This game is fantastic. If you enjoy these types of games, you will LOVE The Letter. I know I sure do.

Good story
Well-written characters
Long
Non-linear (Choices DO matter)
Many endings
Nice art and music

Highly recommend.

while i DID enjoy playing and think the premise of the game is very interesting, i do have some points that hold me back from recommending this to someone looking for horror specific visual novels. mostly that i feel some subjects were taken more lightly than what seemed really necessary to the plot of the game, mostly involving the very theatrical style of dialogue used in game clashing with the very serious racial matters that were being tackled with certain characters. while i think overall the game is working to be sensitive and show a realistic take on several deep issues, some scenes did not hit the mark for me.
 but this is the first visual novel made by this dev team (?) so i think of them more as growing pains in development more than any actual slight against the writers. other than that i thought the art styles were unique and certainly stunning in many scenes, even if the proportions were off with some of the sprites, which i know can be difficult to master so really i don't find anything to actually complain about in that department. i'm certainly no artist of this level myself, i loved the expressive nature especially in the fully rendered scenes! personally i liked when it leaned more into the "anime" art style and wished to see a stronger commitment to that with all the character sprites, but that is just my own personal tastes. i certainly can see this being a good introductory visual novel for those who haven't played one before, since the production quality is great with fully voiced lines, a big cast of characters, wonderfully done scares, unique quick time events, and an easy to follow branching tree to see your story progression. the journal was a wonderful touch as well for summarising plot points that could have been forgotten with the long run time in a single play-through. 
for anyone reading this do not take it as a sign to stay away and think i am not a fan of this work, i loved riding along the dramatic, bumpy road of this charmingly scary game! the only thing i'd ask people to consider is to have a critical eye while playing and treat it with respect for the developers intentions.

Although I don't have much time logged in this game yet, I saw other people play through it with various outcomes, which is one of the reasons I decided to purchase it. There are times when I wish Steam had a sliding scale rather than just "positive" or "negative" reviews, since this one is mixed and I would only recommend it to certain people.

First, the positives: this game is definitely a labor of love, making it one of the best looking and most dynamic VNs I've ever played. A lot of attention and detail was put into crafting the story and world, as well as fleshing out the characters. I know some reviews have mentioned there's some racist and homophobic language in some of the routes, but they are in keeping with the characters they've established as the cast is actually very diverse racially and sexually, something not often seen in VNs. At the same time, they don't make race or sexuality the entirety of the characters' identity,  they just acknowledge that people's experiences are going to influence their lives and how they view the world.

The voice acting is top notch, and the developers are very open and friendly and responsive to feedback, looking to continually improve the game even after its release. One really gets a feel they are genuinely just as thrilled by each fan of the game they attract as the fans are for having found it. The could just take the "positive" rating they currently hold for the game on Steam and rest on their laurels, but they truly want to make the game as great an experience for everyone as possible, even opening a public Discord server (that is linked to their social media).

The story is engaging, even addictive at times. It's really like a soap opera, where no matter what happens, you find yourself wanting to know more. The fact that your choices have so much influence on the game, from beginning to end, makes repeated playthroughs feel almost like entirely different games. You can reveal dozens of paths, outcomes, and ending scenarios depending on how you choose to play right from the very first chapter on. There are lots of things to help you keep track of your progress, from branching trees to relationship charts to a journal that keeps a timeline of all the events as they happen and you view things from different characters' perspectives.

So now the not so great parts: as a "horror story", it sort of falls flat. If you're looking for a VN with non-stop tension and horror like Corpse Party, you'll be disappointed, as it seems to be more of a slice-of-life drama where a ghost story is looming vaguely in the background and once in a while, the ghost will pop up to inject unexpected horror. The scares are pretty effective when they happen, but they don't happen very often, and seem to happen less so as the story goes on.

Some of the story is extremely overwritten. The length could have easily been reduced if a lot of unnecessary dialogue and reptition were trimmed from the narrative portions, which could have offered more time to flesh out the ghost story. This seems to get worse over time, really getting bad around chapter five and lasting until the end of the game. A lot of time is spent meandering around the plot, whether it's the horror portion or the personal interactions, sacrificed in favor of discussions of topics like interior design and food (a lot of food shows up in this game, actually. I joke it excels more in food porn than horror).

In all, I'd say if you're already a fan of VNs and looking for one that mixes slice-of-life with a little bit of horror for Halloween, then you'll probably enjoy this a lot. However, if you don't have a lot of patience for overwritten narratives and character drama and just want something that goes straight into horror and focuses on it, you'll likely be very disappointed. Those who do get and enjoy it, though, won't be disappointed with the amount of content there is to explore and how many different ways there are to play (that high number of achievements isn't just for show, you really do have to try and steer the story around to get many of them).

Just finished my first playthrough, and having just recently gotten into visual novels, I'm worried that this might ruin all other visual novels for me! Honestly, a must play.

Pros:
+ Great and compelling story
+ A branching tree to show you the different possibilities (reminds me of the one for Detroit: Become Human, but less detailed in what those choices are)
+ Good visuals. A step above the usual 2D figures, with actual moving character figures.
+/- There are several mini games you end up having to play to survive during the game. And you have no clue how each wil work until its actually there. For me, it ended up adding to the atmosphere of the game, but I can see where it could be annoying to others. The game does have a difficulty option that probably affects this, though I didn't mess with it (yes, I dided a few times).
+ There are perma deaths in the game, which can change how the later parts of the game play out. Some of them seemingly obvious, some not as much.

The only thing I can think of to make this game better is adding a timer to some of the choices in the game, especially when in a more tense situation. Maybe in combination with a simple 2 (or more) button system for making choices rather then clicking, I could see that as adding to the game. But it was definitely a must play (again) game for me!

18+ hours I played this game for my first run {if you like to see my playthrough check out my stream on Facebook at Lozza's Emporium}, and I won't lie I quite enjoyed it. 

Was it creepy? Yes, at times
Did it drag? Yes, at times
Was it worth it? ABSOLUTELY, and I'd play it again

If I could recommend a horror visual novel for a first timer it would indeed be this game. It's simple, entertaining, and is well written and spoken. I can't wait for more projects from this company

Easily one of the most thrilling VNs I've played ! The suspense is awesome ! I love all the voice acting. The characters are all likeable in their own way. I've already restarted it to go back and try to find different routes. The sheer amount of achievements is satisfying ! Every choice means something & alters other routes as well. I strongly recommend it ! I got it on sale, but it's absolutely worth the full price. All the characters are voiced & animated ! Lots of gorgeous CG !

The Letter is all over the place but it's a labor of love and it shows. The branching story and character arcs are substantial. It's easy to dismiss and miss out on the story if you only play through it once. The strength is definitely playing out the different branches to understand what's going on in the story. Overall the game is good slow burner, but occasionally the writing shines through big time. It's far more sinister and bleak than the surface level shows. I thoroughly enjoyed it.
One of my fav vns! The writing is very immersive and the characters feel alive. What I love the most is that there's so many outcomes and your choices actually matter.

This game is fucking EVIL. AND ITS A VISUAL NOVEL. Guhhhh I've been reeling after this. Always finding one more way to give you the finger

I love these characters. I bonded with them. And seeing anything happen to them was soul crushing. Even though they had plenty of solace, I still feel dread the MOMENT the THING shows up. Oh lord

This game isn't full pedal to the metal with the horror, if that is what you are looking for. There is more than enough time to breath, with moments in between to only strength the horror further. Lots of mystery busting and bonding, and it was nice having a visual novel be FULLY voice acted. I was super shocked and enjoyed it

I can't sing enough praises about this, Yangyang Mobile. You guys did great and I'll be here crying inside for a while :(

6.5/10
I'm not super into VNs, but the demo did an effective job selling the horror of the story and I thought I would give it a go. This is a much more passive experience than the other VNs I've played like The Nonary Games and Danganronpa where you have to investigate and solve puzzles, most of the time you are going to be clicking just to move the dialogue along. There are a handful of choices that you make that will affect the storyline and a few QTEs that were quite effective if fairly simple, but that's about all you need to do.

I played through twice and felt that I had seen enough to consider it done.  There are a ton of branching paths, but there's a lot of repetition in each and so much of the dialogue and exposition is needlessly wordy and drawn out.  At several points I just wanted to take a red pen to the script and start cutting lines.  The characters are interesting and pretty well developed each with their own personality and trauma, the twists are surprising while being in line with the genre (think Ju-on or Ringu), and every choice felt it had weight.  I've seen some people put in ridiculous amounts of time to get every possible ending, but I didn't see a lot of variation when I went through it again making the opposite choices.  It you like the genre, this one is pretty well done, but if you want to be a more active player, there are other games that will work better.

Okay, so in a nutshell, this is one of those few visual novels where your choices actually have a real impact on the plot and the endings. There's tons of variations between routes, and there's a nice little diagram that lets you see what choices you haven't made before. It's excellent in terms of scope and design.

The CG's are gorgeous. The music and sound effects are professional-grade.
The art on character portaits are...hit and miss, at points.
The voice acting is -generally- above average, but there's points where characters aren't delivering their lines well and could have used a bit more direction on that front.

The script is solid, though there's definitely some rough spots. Nothing that's a deal-breaker, but definitely feels like it could have used a bit more polish. 

But this game is ABSOLUTELY worth whatever price you're paying for it. (And since it's several years old, you're probably buying it on sale.)

10/10 purely in the visual novel genre
7/10 as a game overall.

Hello Steam, welcome to my very first review.

[h1]DISCLAIMER[/h1]
This review features my personal opinion, thus you might encounter a concept called disagreement. But fear not, that does not mean that anything is wrong with you, it is a totally natural reaction. In cases of severe disagreement please refrain from flaming, instead downvote this review and state your own opinion in the comments, thank you very much.

[h1]Where I came from[/h1]
I read a few other VNs, including the Danganronpa and the Nonary Games series, which I enjoyed very much.
Regarding the horror genre I read some stories by Poe and Lovecraft, some of the finest wordsmiths of their respective time and watched a bunch of different horror movies.
Oh and don't get me wrong, I'm not loving said VN's for their gameplay elements but for their intricate story.

[h1]Where I went[/h1]
At first I was very intrigued, wondering how the story around the seven protagonists will evolve.
Driven by the urge to find out more about the otherworldly presence haunting each and every one of the vastly different characters I read hour after hour growing more excited by the minute.
It had me on the edge of my seat many times, building up the tension, increasing the psychological terror and then releasing it by the appearance of the ghost, masterfully presented in stunning CGs, freeezing the blood in my veins.

This tense atmosphere persisted for many hours, but somewhere along the way, about halfway through the story it began to loosen the tight grip in which it entangled me thus far.
The dialogues grew tedious, the looming horror started to lose its thrill, the occasional jump scare still managed to make my heart skip a beat, but they felt more and more familiar, to similar to their predecessors.

But I kept reading, still fascinated by the glorious presentation, which is, like many others stated, the best I've seen in a VN to this day. 
Graphically stunning, musically beguiling, this VN begged for my attention, this masterfully crafted piece of art.
I was longing for the next CG, the beautiful gore I was rewarded with for letting one of my precious characters die, wondering what sublime visual pleasures will await me at the end of this story.

And then it occured to me, this wasn't a novel to me anymore, it was just a glorified art gallery, a quite magnificient one without doubt, but the story lost me some time ago. 
I started to bargain, as one inevitably does experiencing grief, this story could still be saved, this journey is not over yet, the different endings will justify the arduous journey.

The journey through repeating scenes viewed from the perspective of different characters, showing but the tiniest nuance of variety. 
Through mind-bogglingly boring inner monologues. 
Through dialogues unparalleled in mundanity.

So I arrived at the end, with a tiny part of me still hoping that this is the moment to turn this whole thing around, to bring the story back to life, to reward my endurance. 
That, the first array of endings I encountered, did not, as failed the second to do.
But there was still a glimmer of hope, the true ending. 
Would it be able to finally quench my thirst for closure? Would it in fact prove to be a silver lining? 
Hope.
Hope turned into despair.

[h1]Where I ended up[/h1]
I expected this VN to have similar depth to Danganronpa or the Nonary Games, but I was deeply disappointed.
Maybe I was wrong to anticipate such complex storytelling from a work of fiction inspired by classic horror films, maybe this just isn't the right VN for me, but nonetheless I was so underwhelmed by this game, that I just had to share my experience with you. I hope (here I am, hoping again) my opinion is valued by some of you.

[h1]tl;dr:[/h1]
Top notch presentation of an overall unsatisfying piece of fiction.

[h1]Final Verdict: Good, but not a great VN. Spoiler Free Review.[/h1]

I did enjoy my time overall with The Letter. However, at 17 hours long (for one full playthrough), the narrative did falter at times, especially with the heavy exposition. At times it felt that not a lot happened with the actual plot, but the characters would go on and on in detail about what was happening or their feelings.

You never had to guess what a character was feeling or thinking about a particular event, as you were bound to hear about it in great detail. Sometimes it was just repetition of their previous thought, simply told in a slightly different way. Don't get me wrong, I enjoyed all the characters. They each had a background story and their own experiences which shaped their world views. It is nice to have three dimensional characters. However, it was taken a bit too far in this VN. Nothing was left to the imagination. Especially, with not a lot happening within the actual story. 

I honestly think this story would have been better told in 5 hours versus the 17 hours it took me to read. Having almost the same set set of events happen for each character may sound interesting at first, but it does get grating after the first few. There are some different tidbits here and there, but a lot remains the same. There's even certain sections where the events are exactly the same as before (same text even). 

I just think the whole story would have been better if there had been an editor to cut out all the unnecessary parts. The romantic interests of some the characters just didn't end up being necessary to the story. They were more like side quests with total dead ends. I'm not sure we needed to see any part of Marianne McCullough's story arc either. 

The story also had an unsatisfying ending. I was more relieved it was finally over than interested in the ending. That's a shame because there were several things I did enjoy. The art was very well-done. The VOs were good overall. The BGM was also good, although somewhat repetitive after 17 hours. The themes about friendship and family and struggles really resonated. It's a shame that the horror element was dragged on and on without a satisfying conclusion. 

I don't think the choices available were meaningful. There was ever hardly a moral conflict. I have no interest in playing the game again to see how different choices impact the story. I doubt they do. Finally, I did not like the QTEs. They weren't difficult, but they just weren't fun. 

I did get this on sale from a 3rd party site. I think I got my money's worth overall. While the writing wasn't great, everything else was pretty high quality for a VN.

[b]Rate 6.5/10. Good, but not great. It was a bit too long for its own good. Buy on sale.[/b]

(Note: This review is based on completing the game once.) 

I would highly recommend "The Letter" to people who enjoy games because:
+ They like an emphasis on characters and character relationships with each other
+ They like the whole butterfly effect/choice and branching paths mechanic and multiple endings 

Other [b]pros[/b] include: 
+ Voice acting is pretty good and fits the characters well
+ The CG Illustrations are really nice to look at 
+ Plenty of achievements to keep you busy (if you're into achievement hunting)
+ Decent length, even if you just play it once. I totalled up to about 25 hours.  

Overall I can see that a lot of love was given to make this game. However some [b]cons[/b] I would like to list: 

- The Ghost. I know it says it was inspired by certain Asian horror movies, but the [spoiler]long haired, pale skin Japanese ghost that makes the -same- sound[/spoiler] is really overdone. I wished they would have tried to make it stand out a bit more.  
- The plot is not this game's strong point. Not to say that it's bad, but it certainly isn't unique or something that will blow your mind...especially if you consider the plot of many Asian horror movies. 
- The writing can drag on sometimes and feel unecessary, especially when I was playing the second last chapter. Too much introspection. 
- Last one is more of a personal gripe. I was initially reluctant to buy the game because of: (yes, it's going to sound silly) the jiggle physics. It's not only unrealistic, it made my mind unintentionally create an association with some of the garbage lewd VNs that have littered steam. That being said, it isn't too bad. If you're someone that likes this sort of extra detail, I guess this would be a positive.

This game was in my wishlist for a long time before a friend got it me as a gift, but that doesn't affect my review of this game.

I was hooked the moment I started playing this game and the fact that every little choice can effect the storyline greatly, kept every play through fresh for me.

The artwork and music in this game are fantasic and I got really attached to the characters. I even found myself crying at some scenes.

The only issues I have are with how annoying Ashton's and Luke's routes are to 100% and I wanted to know more about ghost too.

9/10 game. I really loved it.

Holy story branches, batman. 

First thoughts:
This game is very, very complex if you're achievement hunting, and it's also TIME CONSUMING. It's a pretty typical interactive visual novel, except for the fact that this one is GORY, ACTUALLY SCARY AT SOME POINTS, and that it's got a ton of different interactions which do actually change the story arc.

It's seldom that a game in this genre is this fun or interesting to play, but - you play as a real estate agent who is trying to sell a haunted house. Depending on what happens, not only can YOU die but you can also play let a ghost/demon thing murder your friends accidentally with a single wrong option.

Honestly? I'd recommend this to anyone that enjoys quick time events, or to anyone that enjoys a casual yet complex horror story. This has so many interesting paths. I will say that [spoiler] ASHTON'S ROUTE IS RIDICULOUSLY HUGE in regards to how many branches it can spread out. Seriously. I can't even make an excel chart where it's actually readable on one page, because of how huge it gets [/spoiler] some of the routes are COMPLEX AF. Seriously, you need some specific events to trigger other ones, which can affect how other endings or things happen.

Achievements too. It's very rare that I don't work through and get EVERY ACHIEVEMENT EVER in all of my games, and I'm kind of sad to admit that this one probably won't end up getting "mastered" by me, because of this. There really are too many. And unlike in other games where it's crashing/glitching that prevents me from getting the achievements, in this one there is just way too many paths and my brain isn't that great at memory.

Onto more technical scores:
Graphics - 9/10 - this game is beautiful for a 2.5 dimensional game. Kudos to the game's designers. I love this art style. I'm not exactly sure how to define it, but it's a bit more of a painted/painterly type of style than your typical "anime" visual novel.

Sound - 10/10. This game knows exactly how to use music to chill you to the bone, and sound effects to really enforce what's going on. Lol. It sets the tone.

Gameplay - 9/10. I only had a few bugs (though this may be hardware related, as I did play this on my older pc) which messed with a few quick time events and lead to me re-doing them a bunch, but all of the minigame/quicktime events are interesting. They're not all the same, and you don't always (especially on a first playthrough) know when they're going to happen.

UI/HUD -10/10. This. Is. AWESOME. The game uses a graphical chart, which not only shows where you branched off on each character's story arc, but it also shows when you did not yet choose an option. This gets even more complicated when you consider that you're playing more than a single character. The menu allows you to check your friendship/romance levels with specific characters, and it also shows what path you're on in the save. This is awesome.

Honestly? If you're into spooky games, into gory games, or you're just into unique story in 2D games and you like reading, pick this game up. I'd recommend this for sure.

This game..there are no words to describe how abtolutely brilliant and beautiful the characters are, the story is magnificent and ive replayed it more times than i can count just because i love every single branch the story follows. And yes, your choices count much much more than you think, just in real life

Nice artwork, great voice acting and an interesting  story. A little slow at times, but it helps build the atmosphere.

If you like horror visual novels it's  a good addition to your library.

If you like Until Dawn, you'll like this
The CG's are beautiful (especially the gory ones)
The voice acting is very good. Luke's utter maniac performance is worth the price of admission

This game is way underrated knowing that it was made by a small developer. Like for real, i never seen so many buterfly effects in a game in my whole life. The artsyle is really nice and it has nice terror moments. All the dialogue is made by voice actors.
Also... what?, what else do you want? Go up and buy it!

The true horror of this game for me lies in spending many enjoyable hours getting to know each of these engaging characters and the suffocating fear that their deaths are a very real possibility before this game is done. Or worse that one of my decisions might have directly caused it to happen.  

The mark of a great story is its ability to suck you in and make you believe, which this game has done expertly. The SFX, voice work, and art style are all very good. And although I've only just finished the second chapter the story keeps pulling me back for more.

Although both games are at polar opposite sides of the spectrum, playing this I'm reminded that if the creators of Until Dawn had made me care about their characters as much as I do about the guys and girls in the Letter then I believe we would have entered the golden age of decision-based horror, and I would never get a good nights sleep again.

Great Stuff!

Headphones and the wee small hours of the night recommended for play.

This visual novel is great because it's long, the dialogues are fully voice acted in English, you play as different characters so you can understand the story in different perspectives (but that also leads to repetition), and it have different endings to offer which depends on the choices you made. There are mini animations and mini-games where you fought against the ghost, which makes the story more immersive.

Spooky mansion has a spooky letter in it, if you read it then a ghost will occasionally come and do bad things to your blood pressure until the bad thing is that you don't have any left.

That's the story in a nutshell.  Picked up for Halloween, looked cheap and cheerful, good for a few laughs and maybe some jumpscares.

Before I knew it I was deeply, DEEPLY invested.

Around 28 hours for a first playthrough (with all voice lines playing and reading out anything unvoiced, cut that way down if you're going at a solo reading pace) but I took around 60-70 hours total to explore all the routes and get the true ending, which is worth!

What takes time to show but shines brilliantly when it does is how much love and passion has been poured into this.  How deep the choice structure goes. How wildly different the story will be depending on your choices because they really do actually matter, far more than any other game of it's kind that I've played.  How human, flawed and complicated every single character is; at first they all appear tropey and shallow but never before have I now felt empathy this deep for a set of fictional characters. I have publicly shed tears over everyone, multiple times.

I want to dive deeply into all of the things I discovered as I played that opened up new layers to the writing and experience, but I'm very glad I knew none of them going in so I'll refrain.

It's a Visual Novel so prepare for reading, but it is fully voiced and has some very charming animations to aid the experience.

The writing is playful and silly half of the time, deep and emotive when it wants to be, combined with the non-linear structure it takes a lot of skill and planning to be this coherent and powerful, although the text is littered with minor grammar errors

Characters are complex and deeply flawed, even a hateful, spoiled, highly bigoted scumbag you've written off as irredeemable can be someone you feel for later. Even if they're still a scumbag.

Ash however, can get in the sea.

Going down different paths will almost always reveal something new about someone, an extra side to them, or an admission that an opinion they shared elsewhere may have not been entirely true..

The art at first will seem wildly inconsistent, it uses very different styles in every corner of the game, but after a while you embrace it, it's wonderful seeing these characters reflected in so many styles, getting a new CG or journal entry feel like real goals I want to reach, and are very satisfying.  Achievements and their names popping up are almost always bring a grin for happy moments or feel like a kick while we're down for the bad times.

The horror while infrequent does not mess around. At it's core it's creepy imagery not quite outright jumpscarey but you may definitely jump in fright anyway, with unsettling animations and disturbing content at times.

The music is phenomenal, atmospheric, unintrusive, mood-setting and heart-swellingly powerful when it needs to be

The voice acting talent is incredible, voices are clean, rich, warm, full of emotion, subtle inflections, heart-wrenching cries.  I rarely have this much praise for English language VA work but I cannot stress enough how much I enjoyed these. 

Long story short. Loved every minute, would highly recommend, would take a bullet for most characters, may have splashed a stupid amount of money to buy the collector's edition. If had time machine would go back and join the kickstarter.

The best way to describe this game to me is: a visual novel, thats a mix of grudge and until dawn, with a divine soundtrack, and beautiful artwork, a story and characters i loved. The choices you make actually make a huge diference in how the story plays out: not if characters live or die, but also their own personality (to a point some cases more then others).

While not perfect (what is) despite having finished the game several times, got good, bad medium and true endings, I feel I still want more, and know more of the characters or the story. Its a pity the kickstarter didnt allow then to add one more chapter. And the soundtrack... I love it.

Like i said the game isnt perfect and I do feel there is a bit too much description of unnecessary stuff, even if it does help better to understand the story/characters. I also while i love the characters and i find then to be complex and multi layered... i dont love then equally but maybe just my own preferences. But even so i would love to have a few more chapters for some characters, even some that didnt had their own chapter, like a certain lady whos name starts with the letter T. 

The soundtrack is amazing (didnt i mention that before), not only does it have beautiful musics, very fitting for each scene, making you feel joy, lightheartedness, distress, terror (OK terror might a strong word but you get the idea). The voice acting i also loved it a lot.

In short this game was a super surprise for me, i got it as random 2º game of a fanatical bundle so i could get white day, but right now I played less then 1 hour of that game, and 45 hours of the letter, and I am not finished, i want to play more!

Personally I highly recommend this game, as it is right now one of my favourite and most treasured games. But bear in mind this is a niche game, not for everyone. But the soundtrack is!!!
Overall not a bad game. The lines are way too YA for my taste, but there are good stuff if you can overlook the cringe and cheesiness.

I normally do not write reviews but I had to after it became clear what a labor of love this game is and wanting to let others know of it as well. The Letter has a distinct visual style that is very striking, setting it apart from most other VNs. The story keeps you engaged and wanting more through multiple playthroughs as you try to unlock the many different endings that are available. With 7 unique playable protagonists, the choices you make have real, drastic changes to the story - not just a line or two changed before you're railroaded back to the main plot. No one is safe, characters can die or be changed in several different ways that cause a butterfly effect to the remainder of the story. While there is only one "True" ending, many of the endings you can arrive at are satisfying and feel fully fleshed out, with epilogues detailing lasting friendships, lives that have fallen apart, and even multiple different romances that can occur.

Overall, a wonderful game that is well worth the retail price, and a sure thing when on sale for anyone who loves darker visual novels. Cannot wait for YangYang Mobile's next effort.

The visual novel itself is great – it is elaborate, features great soundtracks and voice acting, but completing the routes soured my experience irreparably. I have never played a game with more torturous and predatory achievements. It is as if they were designed to make completionists suffer. Farming the worst of World of Warcraft reputations was better than this – and I have done them all.

I encourage everyone who does not care about collecting achievements to play this game, however. Is quite a story.

There are guides to this game that are literally flow-charts. As in, actual flow-charts. The level of complexity when it comes to choices is [i]staggering.[/i]

I really love the characters too. I was trying to hold out til I finished the game to review it, but at this point I like it too much to care. I want to unravel all the mysteries of Ermengarde mansion.

I haven't had too many scares but there are a few jumpscares in this. It is a horror game after all. They have yet to get me good. Kudos for tryin tho. I would hands down recommend this to anyone who like visual novels. It's well-written and suitably spooky.

My biggest con with this game is that you go through all of the main character's perspectives through the gameplay. This can make things terribly repetitive. Granted there are some small changes from chapter to chapter, but you're rehashing the same series of shared events. Sometimes it bored the hell out of me. 

Overall the story is ambitious and very detailed. It's definitely worth a look.

This game is amazing. 
I found it while watching a playthrough done by ManlyBadassHero. At first it's off to a slow start and the pacing can be a bit awkward and take a while to get to the meat of the story— but when it does, it's worth it. 
It's a fun, lively experience. Each character has their own flaws and quirks. None of them are completely good or bad. Those you think you'll hate, you end up not sure if you can blame for their actions. 
I enjoyed this game so much I had to get it for myself. The graphics and animation is fluid and dynamic. The butterfly effect actually matters and your choices affect the outcome. The voice acting is spot on, far better than the alpha version's original choices. The OST is super good and fits the game to a T. The plot itself is worth getting into and you find yourself coming back for more even with the occasional jumpscare. 
Overall, a good game. Not without its flaws, but worth a try.

I really disliked this game, it's not Terrible, but I found the story very frustrating. I really loved the starting character, and the initial mystery of the Letter. But as the story continued, I found most of the characters really... irritating. Not in that I was upset they had flaws, but GOD we had ghosts, suspicion of murder, and one character throws a fit that the boy they like likes another girl and has for years????? COME ON! Similarly, im gay, and one of the characters arcs is internalized homophobia, which is a real thing that happens to people, but I really just wanted to learn more about the ghosts/supernatural mystery.

Horror is often a metaphor for the problems we have in our selves. And i thinks that's what the story is going for. But the friction between the Horror and the Daily Life problems just did not work for me. Ultimately I think this is an issue of personal preferences, but for other folks, just know there is Not much time spent on the letter in The Letter

Some of the dialogue and voice acting could be better but it doesn't take away from the gameplay or story. The QTEs are annoying, especially the one in the lift (if you know, you know). Overall would highly recommend this. It's more of a romance with slight horror story elements but very good.

Virtually zero horror this game is actually a romance sim where you can play as cupid for a group of people. Every once in a few hours something spooky will happen and by spooky I mean a .jpg will pop up and the character will get scared but never mention it again, which is how you should react to this game

What it's not: 
It's more "Santa Barbara" with some horror elements than an actual horror visual novel. There were so many slice of life parts, that it made me want to go on a murder spree myself. I hope they make a fan disc where you can torture and murder all these awful and boring characters. This is the worst thing that has happened to "the Ring" since the American Ring 2...

What it is:
It's basically a multiple route mystery melodrama, where the focus is supposed to be on the characters. The cast is pretty one-dimensional and awful people. Even though all the main characters are supposed to be relatively smart adult people, they are as dumb as your average b rate slasher teens. Also this visual novel does the most annoying thing that a multiple route mystery can do, where you get repeated scenes (multiple times) without anything new. You basically see the scene from a "different pov", but the text is identical. And of course you can't skip it, because it counts as unread text. The art is very inconsistent and some of the proportions are pretty off. The CGs can look really good but the sprites just look cheap (with a flash game feeling to them). The profiles look fine (style wise), but were cut out weirdly with jagged white lines around them (maybe it's not a bug but a feature?).

TLDR: Genres are arbitrary and what is horror or not isn't the same for everyone. I would give this a "meh" for what it is (if I could), but since it's not possible and the game is advertised as a "Horror Visual Novel" it's a skip for me.

Ps. Stop making people imitate accents that not only sound horrendous and fake, but are also probably hard to act out. Kudos for having all the characters voiced, but I'd rather stick pencils into my ears than listen to some of these accents X/

This game is a mess. It's a shame because the artwork, voice acting, music, branching paths, and general story are all quite good. You can tell a lot of effort and love went into it. But the writing itself is AWFUL. The pacing is painfully bad and a constant problem. As soon as things start to get interesting with one character, their arc ends and you're forced back into hours of exposition with the next character. The game is filled with long, tedious inner monologues even during scenes where high-stakes action is happening. These monologues are very redundant and either spell things out that we already know, tell things that would be better shown, or go on about topics that just plain don't matter. It comes off as filler for filler's sake, not because there's something charming or important to convey. There are a ton of typos and grammatical errors, too.

The characters are also poorly developed. I think they had a lot of potential, but the writing for them doesn't bring it out. Sometimes their personalities are all over the place and they'll be quite distant or even mean to each other for no reason. The writers' blatant favoritism toward Ashton is obnoxious. He gets much more time and attention than the other characters and is constantly hyped up as this cool, cunning, mysterious heartthrob (despite him actually being quite the opposite). Meanwhile Luke is an irredeemable monster, to the point where I had no idea why the writers treat him like a sympathetic character or expect the player to be invested in his story at all.

There is huge variety in how events can play out, but it's not always done well. One character died in my playthrough and the group of friends built up to be like family barely acknowledged it - one was cracking jokes and flirting in the next scene, one just looked sad for a moment before shrugging it off, and another you never even see her find out. For a game supposedly all about character relationships, that is inexcusable.

No matter what path you take, the ending is a horrible letdown. After all the tedious reading, I was looking forward to finally unraveling the big mystery and having a thrilling final act. But it just ends with an unceremonious fizzle -[spoiler]the surviving characters argue in a bloody room and then leave. That's it.[/spoiler]There are no surprises or reveals; the game acts like [spoiler]the letter not being the cause of the curse[/spoiler]is a big twist but even for someone notoriously bad at guessing plots, that was glaringly obvious since chapter one. For how much is overexplained throughout the game, it only ends with tons of unanswered questions, things that didn't make sense, and plot threads that went absolutely nowhere. 

Overall, the game has some good elements and I wouldn't say it's one to avoid at all costs. But, the problems weigh the game down too much to give it a positive review. I was pretty frustrated and disappointed by the end of it. The creative talent and passion is there, but the actual writing and editing skills required for a visual novel are severely lacking.

having high expectation on this one , complete the game and feel this game is overrate , the story is kinda generic and have so many dairy activity dialog that is no much important , seem just to drag the gameplay ... collecting branch and story also take too much effort , I couldn't bother to 100 percent this game which is a shame ... even story seem lacking , the art and the voice acting is much appreciate , i can see this is a project with passion but lacking unique story and have a lot of dragging content

If I could give the "Mixed" recommendation I would.

The best thing about this is the voice acting and mechanics e.g. how many choices change the ending - I really liked the branching tree (although I've only done 1 play-through). Unfortunately, for a visual novel set in the UK I don't think they did much research into the UK as a culture. For example, the school settings did not look like UK schools and the supermarket food wasn't true to the UK's classic meal deal sandwiches. Furthermore, the English spelling, grammar, and tenses are not good, for a visual novel all about reading you'd think it would be proof read a bit better - this is the main reason I'm not recommending it.

Just finished playing this and got like 2/3 of the total achievements, I've seen multiple good and one true ending and maybe I'll come back to collect the rest of the achievements in the future... or maybe not. Anyway, I think this VN had a lot of heart put into it, from the gorgeous art to the branching choices to the music (although less memorable than other VNs I've read). Here's a few things I liked and disliked about the Letter.

The Letter managed to write natural and convincing characters. Each character have their own unique take on the situation and their thought process rarely feel similar to one another. And don't forget about the art. The art is gorgeous and special. I'm glad the studio didn't go for the standard VN art and stick to something more westernized. The animated backgrounds/characters shows how much effort was put in and I applaud them for that. 

Voice acting is a hit and miss. The over reacting and the higher pitched voices can be a bit of a displeasure to listen to at times. And almost everybody is overacting which is okay if it feels "right" but the characters aren't written that way. And the horror is kinda bad too, after ten times of seeing the ghost's face, most of us probably just didn't care anymore and find her more of an annoyance than a surprise. The QTEs didn't help either. I'm sure the studios put it in for the game to be more marketable but it became the downside after a while. A good horror is mostly about the atmosphere after all, and the Letter failed to deliver one.

Well to cut it short, honestly, I don't hate the Letter. In fact, I really liked it. I admired the effort put into it and love parts of it. I genuinely thought it has the potential to be a really amazing horror VN. It's almost frustrating to see how good this could have been. Here's wondering how the latest horror VN from the developer, Saint Maker, will fare. Maybe I'll buy it during this summer sale, guess we'll see.
Waste of time, poor writing and one of the first scenes involving a black character includes the n word with a hard r getting dropped and its barely acknowledged
Decent VN, there's some jumpscares, however it's just that, not too spooky, but as i said decent overall. Luke best worst character.
just finished the game after 5 years, the voice acting holy shit

This is a really solid visual novel, all things considered. The characters come from diverse backgrounds, the voice acting is good, the art style is pleasant, and the choices have true consequences. It's scary too! Although, there are quite a few errors in the writing that can be a little distracting at times. I love that the characters have relationships which you as the player can make or break. That's an awesome aspect to add into a visual novel. I've played for 18 hours already, over the span of a year or two, since I'm not a quick gamer, so take that into account, but it feels like a really long game. There's a ton of achievements I have yet to get. I definitely recommend this game. It's a high-quality, cartoonish (although not too much so), horror VN. The cartoonish aspect almost adds to the horror, making the heavy content all the more startling. Or, at times, it softens the blow. Play this game, for sure!

review:
Hold on to pants because you are in for a great story, not to mention the plethora of cultural references and gags. This game is one giant joke like the entire premise was built with dripping sarcasm.

[b][u]Pros:[/u][/b]
[list]
[*]Fully Voiced Acting in English and Great Quality
[*]Funny Punchlines that Made Me Chuckle
[*]Great System of Moneymaking which will make you hate Taxes
[*]Superb Combat System and by Combat I mean letting your Knight do all the work why you mostly support and try not to die
[*]Great Visual Art
[*]Great Storyline and Romance Stories which make me eager to play through again to find out about the other endings.
[/list]

[b][u]Cons:[/u][/b]
[list]
[*]Early Stage Grinding is Brutal after the first few levels the Easy Mode while cheap isn't cost effective compared to training at Normie Mode which is the same amount of time at 250% the experience rate and after a while it becomes a pain to use that till you unlock Beast Mode.
[*]If you haven't done much in the way of romance and talking to the girls in the game the first month and into your second month will be pretty dull as you'll spend most of your time training and have no story events that trigger so I'd advise you carve out some time each day or an entire day to simple meet and talk to all of the girls as well as figure out what they like so you can improve your gains with them by speaking and giving them a gift.
[*]I read pretty fast and it seems like the auto mode did not work the entire time I played leaving me to press the spacebar like a madman otherwise I would have sat and listened to the amazing voicework more, instead it just hangs after they read the line rather than continuing on.
[/list]


Based on this I'd give this game a [b][u]9.5/10[/u][/b] would purchase again and it is a solid by if your a fan of the Dating Sim with RPG Elements.

We hope you enjoy the rest of your playthroughs!

P.S. Don't forget to get the game's true ending, aka the harem ending. You'll be in for a treat. ;)

review:
So, let's see here...
Really good art style? Check
Extremely well done voice over? Check
Well written dialogue and tons of story to go through? Check

This game kind of blew my mind. It is so well made, so far beyond what the dating sim genre usually has to offer, that you can get lost in the story and the decent game mechanics. And you should. Because if you are thinking about buying this game as another hentai / erotic game, this game has little to offer you. The build up and excitement portion is there. It takes effort to win a girls favour and the journey is quite thrilling. However, they are not very rewarding in terms of what you usually get and might expect from such a genre. The unlockable CGs take time and close to all of them are not erotic or even ecchi. Most of them are a fully clothed partner looking at you. So again: if you think the only value you will get out of this is the thrill of eventually unlocking adult content, this game has little for you. But if you are looking for a really, really well made dating sim, maybe even one of the best, do give this game a shot.

[h1] A Great Premise, But... [/h1]

There's a lot I like about this game, because there's a lot this game does well. Unfortunately, the entire experience was ruined because the one thing this game doesn't do well is gameplay. The characters are unique and interesting, if perhaps a bit tropey.  The various subplots were fairly engaging. And I genuinely enjoyed exploring the various ins and outs of the combat. 

But all of that amounts to basically ~10% of the game. Everything else is a dull repetitive grind. You cycle through each day visiting various locations, clicking to get gifts/vendor trash, spending money to very slowly raise your stats, visiting waifus to hear them talk about unimportant things. Every once in a while, they have a plot event that's a bit more interesting, but for the most part its a dull boring grind.

And then for all your efforts, there's no payout. I mean you'll get pics of the waifu's cleavage or pantyshots or what have you. And if you're truly depraved (like me) you can get the community modded...[i] handholding [/i] ...scenes (gasp scandalous). But getting to that point takes literally hours and hours of grind. That's really all the game offers as payout, but I can get that in 10 minutes on other games with better gameplay and don't cost $25.

The standard gameplay loop doesn't serve to engage the player, but rather to bore them, and that wouldn't be worth playing even if it were free. 

[b] Score: 1.5 / 5 [/b]

I greatly enjoyed my time with Love Esquire. It's a great addition to my list of hidden gem games. 

Pros:
+Surprising amount of content. The game has 5 girls total, each with tons and tons of dialogue, character arcs, and special event scenes. 

+Great writing. Stellar worldbuilding. Each girl also has a surprising amount of depth, with believable feelings, motivations, and backstories. Their arcs also come with their fair share of twists and turns, and I became really invested in these girls' stories and wanted to see what would happen to them, and that's all I could ask for from a dating sim/visual novel.

+Fully voiced. Almost every single line of dialogue is voiced. For an indie game, that's actually really impressive. The voicework is no slouch, either; it's really good, and made me care about the characters that much more - especially the Squire himself, who, while suffering from no-eyes hentai protagonist syndrome, actually has his own voice acting. His voice actor is clearly having a blast too. He really elevates the Squire from being a self-insert protagonist into an actual character with a defined personality. 

+Fun gameplay. While some may not find the gameplay of Love Esquire engaging, I did. Whenever I play a game, I want to feel like I'm working towards something. Love Esquire's gameplay loop is all about that. All of the stat training goes towards preparing you for the combat minigame, which, while it starts off easy, actually requires you to strategize and minmax your stats a but towards the final levels. All of the dating sim stuff leads you to the payoff that is the girls' stories. Overall, there's just always something to look forward to while I played, and I really liked that about this game. 
+Clean, colorful artwork. All the named characters look nice and are well-designed. The generic villagers, however, suffer from no-face syndrome, but this is more of a nitpick than a con because who cares about the generic villagers lol

Cons:
-Inconsistent audio mixing. Some voice lines are suddenly really quiet compared to the others. Dunno if this is a bug with the game's sound engine or if it's an audio mixing thing. Fortunately, this was rare enough that it did not impact my enjoyment, but when it happened, it was really noticeable. 

-No full frontal nudity, but fuck if we don't come pretty close. Stop blueballing me and show me those tiddies YangYang Mobile.

Final Verdict: 9/10
TLDR: A hidden gem if you're looking for a lighthearted dating sim with light RPG elements that has compelling characters and a believable world.

Even as a woman, this game is very charming. I'm a huge fan of VNs, and I can tell that this game was made with a lot of heart. My favorite part is the storytelling. As you pursue these romances, you'll see a darker side to their story, and it is well worth a read.

Really enjoyed my time with this game, it got me some good laughs, but it also got a lot more sentimental and dark in places.

The game sells itself as a trashy little title but actually it made a pretty solid impression on me. For a Visual Novel even with more options than expected. I'm really glad I discovered it.

Even if the voice over suffers from technical problems, the actors are very good and yet have made a great contribution to highlighting the dialogues in a positive way. The main character in particular did a great job in my opinion. His way of emphasizing the dialogues and working with emotions might not be every man's cup of tea, but for my part I liked it.

The story and the characters were much more believable and profound than I expected and the usual clichés weren't really used. Another point that surprised me very positively.

I also loved the art style. I think the overall quality is really high and the animated scenes are fantastic.

The features of NewGame+ made it much easier to play the other ways, but a flowchart could have been a lot more convenient here. I can confirm after NG15 the "Ending the day" in particular takes too many clicks and changes of scene, even with direct travel on the map.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Note: The "sexual content" tag is unnecessary. Apart from the really harmless fan service, which is also very rare, the game has no sexual content whatsoever.

Just because there is a mod for it that many use - it is no reason to mark the whole game with it. Especially if the mod doesn't add anything valuable to the previous content.

It's a good dating sim with some RPG and some VN elements.

- There isn't [b]that much[/b] of a central storyline here to call it a VN - sure, there are stuff happening, but it's secondary to the "dating sim" nature of the game.

- There's very little interaction between the NPCs. Most of the interactions is the protagonist interrogating them in a very artificial, dating sim style, way.

- The voice [b]acting[/b] is good. The voice [b]recording[/b] is horrible. Dialogue changes volume frequently, sometimes from one line to the next. It seems to get worse later in the game, as if less takes were made or something.

- The protagonist start out extremely annoying, but he does grow up, which is nice. Won't get into details to not spoil stuff, but it's noticeable that as the game progresses he starts acting more mature.

+ The RPG battle system is pretty cool. Neat combat system.

+ The characters themselves are interesting enough to make playing the game worthwhile.

First off, I need to make a small disclaimer that I can only recommend this game if you know exactly what kind of game you're getting into.

If you're looking for an RPG game that features lots of H-Scenes (like Meltys Quest or Evenicle), then this isn't the right place.

If you're looking for an amusing dating sim game that hints at combat but really just focuses on you bonding with 5 different characters and gaining different endings, then this is far better for that.

That all being said, when I bought this game I thought it fit more of the first description. I was surprised when I saw no explicit content. The only content you can get is through the steam workshop and it just gives you a single sexual scene at the end of a person's romance path, and this sexual scene is a few images with light animation and soft moaning noises, without any voiceacting. That really wasn't the focus of this game and it shows.

But I didn't mind it. I enjoyed the dating sim aspect, as each girl is very fleshed out and none of them feel cliche or one-sided. In fact, while some of them appear cliche at first, speaking to them reveals that each have some very interesting backgrounds, likes, dislikes, fears, secrets, and so forth. And some of the events you can trigger will have an impact on the story, as well as what kind of "ending" you get with each girl.

The "combat" elements leave some to be desired, as they quickly get repetitive as you get four options to pick from and a lot of them it's just alternating between two choices. There is not much in the means of mechanics here, and you can see from the steam trailer almost exactly how simple the combat system is. Since the combat system is the primary way to make money in this game, it'a a shame there's not more challenge or variety.

Pretty much everything is voice acted, and the voice acting for the most part is well done. I did have some issues with some poor quality in certain voiced lines-- sometimes the line would be VERY LOUD, weirdly quiet, or just very poorly recorded. A few lines missed voicing entirely.

One small complaint I have about the game is about an artistic choice, so there's bound to be people who'd say I'm just nitpicking. But, except for a single character, pretty much every male character has no eyes. This also happened with female unnamed NPCs, and your protagonist. They do the anime trick where characters just have a dark area where the eyes should be. It doesn't make sense in this context and just comes across as lazy and pretty distracting, at least for me. Especially with characters who aren't brooding and who don't have bangs. Where are the eyes?! How hard would it have been to draw eyes at least for some of the more important NPCs? 

As far as gameplay time, I bought this game when it was on sale for about $20, and I got over 30 hours of gameplay out of it. I have completed 4/5 romantic arcs. So I think it is a solid amount of content, especially considering if you like to earn all achievements or if you plan on going through more than one playthrough. 

Also, there are a ton of save files and achievements which is a nice perk.

So overall, I would recommend this game once you've read some reviews as well as the game description and understand exactly what this game genre is-- and what it is not. And that being said, I do believe the price is worth it, but the game isn't flawless and could benefit from being reworked in some ways.

whoever is responsible for finalizing this core game mechanism should apologize to the voice and the art teams.

- you can sell things in batches but not when you're buying.
- interactive POIs you can't loot are purely cosmetic.
- day one routine boredom continues to the final day.
- sexual content is less than 2%.

I know other people have already said this, but let me echo them in saying this game is way more fun than it has any right to be. There's a lot of quality content, and plenty of replayability. The fun characters, world, and great voice acting, along with the actual RPG mechanics make this a very addicting game to play. You won't find very many graphic CGs, but the game is so fun you almost forget to look for them and get sucked into the gameplay, only to be pleasantly surprised when one of those scenes pops up. At my time of reviewing, I haven't even completed it, and I'm already planning future playthroughs.

EDIT: After beating the game (which I found it difficult to get to Arc 4 with any of the girls and ended up getting a friendship ending, bummer) It's apparent that New Game+ appears to be broken. Several of the girls have blank relationship bars that won't change, and the one's that do change don't have any cutscenes or story sequences happen. I've found that resetting their relationship meters helps, but ultimately the meters end up getting stuck again when they're supposed to transition to the next arc. I'd recommend skipping new game plus and just starting a new game.

I've really enjoyed my time with this game. Gave me some good laughs and sometimes, feels, and enjoyment of course :P I really like that there are multiple epilogues depending on your chosen girl.

I'm not a review writer expert, but let's see:
Story: It was Good in my opinion. Nothing fancy, some twists here and there to spice things up. For a game like this, it was enough for me. I know everyone came for the plot... ;)

Visuals: I think the environments and animations are great, I especially liked the main menu and occasional similar event CGI! However, the places can feel repetitive after a while.

Characters: I've found something in all of the girls, that I liked (in style and personality). This urged me to complete all of their main story and some additional side plots.

Gameplay: Going back to your bad to move to the next day was a chore after a while, combat in the campaign mode is nothing special and I'm glad! I really don't like complex gameplay in a game that has other focus anyway...
New game+ was a godsent! Thank you for that! It allows you to move to any day in the calendar (only forward, but still), to skip days by going to bed earlier and to see the hidden scenes marked on map among other things.

Options/Controls: I was really missing the ability to configure keyboard keys to certain functions, like, fast forwarding the time, also, to hide the text box. In some of the scenes it was really khm distracting... I would have loved if I could make it disappear for the hole playthrough.

Overall, I recommend this game if you want a good laugh here and there and to conquer the ladies! 
I definitely recommend the mod called "Hip To Be Squire Uncensored" - you can find it in the workshop, you don't want to miss it, believe me!
