The game itself isn't bad, it crearly took a lot of work have all this content, and I wouldn't have played as much if I was bored. But as an H-game, it just sucks. It's super grindy, the rewards are minimal, and if you chose the wrong option, you'll be locked out of endings. I feel like the affection requirements should be halved, and it should have twice as much H-scenes as it does to at least consider it an H-game worth playing.

Pros:
- Funny writing with good characterizations.
- 99.9% fully voice acted
- Cute character design
- New Game+ mode makes finishing routes after your first one go really fast.

Cons:
- First play through requires a fair amount of pointless grinding
- Battle System is good, but under used.
- Too 'PG' for it's own good.

Overall I liked this game, but I didn't love it. It felt like a small scale Visual Novel that was padded out with a lot of grinding to "build affection" on your first play through and then training your squire for the short battle "campaign". I think the biggest problem I had with the game is that it was frankly too PG and I'm not just talking about the lack of sex or nudity in the base game, but the *relationship* between the MC and the girls is extremely PG to the point where it felt incongruous compared to some of the lewd/pervy humor. A couple of the story lines, while short, were kind of interesting (Amy and Vel in particular were a bit different) but on the whole most story lines felt somewhat truncated to me - with each relationship actually culminating right at the end of the game in what felt like a jump from "we kind of, maybe like each other" to "we are in love!!!" that was so fast it gave you whiplash.

Of course, it doesn't help that the abrupt story lines are nestled in between lots and lots of grinding to build affection or fight monsters, over all extending the length of the experience but not enhancing the relationship content that is currently there.

I don't regret buying this game, but I do feel like it could have been a great game instead of a merely good one - and that's the problem with this game. It sounds odd to say, but the problem is that the game is merely "good" instead of great. I needed to be more invested in the MC and his love life, but at the end of the day I was just reading new routes out of curiosity and could have just called it a day after the first one, since they are all largely the same in terms of how they play out.

Anyway, this is a decent game and if you like these kind of games, you should definitely get it when it's on sale.

Generally speaking, romance ONE woman per playthrough.  Once you complete ONE girl's scenes (typically after 5,500 love-points for her), the game ends.  That said, there are achievements for jealousy (1st in Arc 2, 2nd in Arc 3 if and only if you triggered the first scene in the same 120-day game).  Romance 2 girls max until you get that second Jealousy scene.  After that, focus solely on your girl with the highest points.  The Debugger Tool is your best friend.  Activate it at the start, and use it every day to see what important events get triggered.  Check out my Jealousy Trigger Guide for more in-depth discussion!!!

NEW GAME+.  Squire! Get to the Harem Ending...ON THE DOUBLE!
Your choices in your previous playthrough(s) carry over to your next if you choose the NG+ option.
If you're trying to go for "all scenes seen in 1-playthrough for one character, just follow the on-screen instructions at the start of your NG+.  On the plus side, your gift items and your stats (STR/INT/CHAR) also carry over.  It really does make each subsequent playthrough faster if you've already romanced a girl up to a certain point previously.  Another thing that changes for New Game+ is that special scenes and Jealousy Events get their own (!) bubbles now on the map.  Yay!!!

RPG Element: Combat is turn-based like a lot of Japanese-based games.

This is not a hentai game, but there are a DLC and a mod (preferred over the DLC) that you can add to make it R-18+.  If you're 18+, you really should download/Subscribe to "Hip to be Squire".  :)  If you're missing some CGs, try replaying that girl, getting all special scenes and exhaust all talking options.  If that still doesn't work, deactivate your mod and go for the Romance Ending one more time.

This game plays a lot like the free H-game for DNA^2.  Perhaps, it was done using the same (or a similar) engine.

CON: Sound problems are still a major issue in Vel's arc and Kamala's arc.  It's not just the squire, though his sound files are way too low sometimes.

After having played through this game twice, I can safely say it comes recommended, if you like dating sims/visual novels. While it's by no means flawless, Love Esquire is fun, engaging, and at times, surprisingly well-written for what it is. There's no denying that this game definitely feels a little trashy to play, but the characters and the stories of each of those characters is still really cool to watch unfold.

The game is also still getting updated and supported by both the devs and the community, with new DLC having just come out as of posting this review. They've made some nice quality-of-life updates since when I first played it, which is appreciated. 

My biggest peeves with this game are that the audio is sometimes mixed pretty badly - characters will suddenly sound much quieter than they did before, making it hard to hear them over the music. In both my playthroughs, I also ran into an instance where I must have played certain sequences out of the intended order, and I'm not sure exactly how that even happened. Whether or not those fixes have been made, I cannot say for sure.

While entirely optional, the RPG-esque battle system, while pretty simple, is still a lot of fun, and comes with it's own side-story alongside everything else.

The girls all have fun personalities and quirks, and the main character has a nice arc no matter which girl you choose. If you're in the mood, Love Esquire is worth your time.

Sure it's got a somewhat interesting story and the characters can be quite funny at times, but there's no real entertainment inbetween the visual novel so you're just running around gifting and talking to the girls. The campaign side of the game feels way to fixed onto "be a certain level to clear" instead of being intuitive.

Turn out this is a really great VN. No regrets.

Ultimately, I think I'll be echoing a lot of what others have said: interesting premise, poor execution. 

I played through one cycle of the game, and then got to the New Game+, and did about a month of that. I think I've got a very good idea of what the game is offering. 

The story is interesting, the characters aren't all that bad (some are of course more endearing than others), but the game play is just excruciating at times. It's a game that is ultimately about building relationships with the girls, which requires chatting them up, giving them a lot of gifts (Don't worry, they don't seem to mind if they get 231890 tarot card decks, as long as you don't keep giving them only tarot card decks in a row, which of course means that you need to be tracking what you gave them last time so you can keep switching... which is, of course, excruciatingly boring), and triggering certain events once you've gotten to the right "heart" level. 

I dunno if I'm just unlucky, or if it's bugged to hell, but I'd spent at least a month and a half worth of game time just waiting for said events to trigger so I could progress to the next stage. I guess it's supposed to happen when the girls are in a particular spot at a particular time, but the game was either broken, or it doesn't bother actually sending the girls to those spots at those times when the heart events are eligible. 

Aside from the gifts, you raise your relationship by chatting to them about certain topics, unlocking topics as you go. But each topic has only a certain number of instances that you can talk to them about. And there's literally no indication given of how many times you've talked to them about that topic, so basically you're stuck trying, until you run into the "you should talk to them about something else" message. This message of course doesn't happen immediately after you click the button, but instead it closes the menu, loads the conversation screen, and then shows it to you, then boots you back to the location screen. It's an annoying waste of time. 

A lot about the game is an annoying waste of time. Walking from place to place takes too long, considering how often you need to do it. Purchasing or selling items is an annoying waste of time. Click on the shop keep, click on sell, click on the item you want to sell, select how many you want to sell, click sell, click confirm; repeat that for every bloody item you want to sell because god forbid we use a reasonable interface that allows us to purchase or sell multiple items at a time. Which we do, because, as previously mentioned, you need to give the girls like 23829038 copies of the same two items per gal to max out your relationship score. 

I really wanted to like this game (I had originally backed the Kickstarter), but in the end did not. Honestly, it kind of seems like the devs deliberately tried to pad the game play time, but did so in the stupidest fashion, by making stuff that shouldn't take time be slow, instead of simply adding more content. And that wasn't even needed! Playing through once to unlock the New Game+ was like a good 20 hours. That could have easily been shortened down, and it would have made for a far more engaging experience.

As a wise man once said: "You know what Elden Ring doesn't have? Twerking in front of your enemies" and dammit he was right. This game is the best thing that has happened since the creation of Itadaki Seieki. Strap in and brick up coz this game will have you playing with your mouse in your left hand. The question is not why, but who for. A princess? Another princess that's more... Horizontally gifted? The smol woman wearing armor twice her size? The shy nurse?... Or dare I ask? Your step-sister? Or perhaps you came here to sit for hours on end wondering where you went wrong as you stare at Pop's chiselled abs with pectoral muscles you couldn't even dream of having. Sorry fellas, we'll never be him. 

Love Esquire is truly a diamond in the rough. What's not to like? The protagonist is oh-so relatable, being the eager virgin ogling beauties way out of his league. Just like us. (You may be thinking. Cpt. Cringe, who's "us". Sounds like it's just you who's this thirsty. Don't kid yourself brother, join the club and own it). You got good vibes with your pal Hugh, you got eye candy, and you have a combat system that makes no real sense and yet you'll still somehow have the time of your life. Not sold yet? There's fishing. What else could you possibly ask for? 

Do yourself a favour. Buy this game, lock your door, look over your shoulder every minute or so despite living alone in your sad apartment out of fear someone is watching... and judging. You'll thank me, and more importantly, you'll thank YangYang Mobile for the PRIVILEGE of playing this game.

This game has a lot of character. It's quite fun and unique. Nice mechanics, especially in battle. I don't want to spoil anything by saying anything about the story. It's got fun music, fun characters, animated voice actors, a lot of love seems to have went into this. A little extra polish would be good (bessie slides around sometimes instead of her normal walk and it looks weird, sometimes the auto-text will go too fast and not give you the time to read what it says, etc...) very minor things overall. The game is definitely worth playing and trying out. 

Being able to save at any time during the game would be great, not just at the end of the days. Considering you have the ability to load up at any day you saved it would be nice to be able to save after some interactions/adventures instead of having to go through the whole day. Life happens, sometimes you have to leave to computer and having to choose either to rush through things or re-do the beginning of the day you already did. It would just seem like a benefit to be able to save on a whim.

The biggest gripe I have is the audio.
The dev's really need to work on their audio levels though. 
Clips are all over the place louder or quieter than the rest for no reason (not mumbles that are supposed to be quieter). It's really the biggest disservice of the game. The main squire's voice is very animated, but it also must have max'd out the microphone they were using to record because at times it crackles/maxes out and is actually hard on the ears. I'm forever having to turn the squire back down, then on certain events his audio is so quiet I have to turn it back up, just for him to squeal and max out and be hard on the ears to have to turn him back down. I think going through the game with an audio-leveller so you can see where these clips are at versus others would help level them out. You do have the ability to change particular levels of people, but if the game just had level audio across the board to begin with you wouldn't really need to tinker in there unless you just hated someones voice and wanted it gone, haha. I could turn all the audio voices off but I feel like that takes away from the game, since the voice actors involved all are doing a great job despite the levels being messed up.

Overall, I'm really enjoying the game, 7/10 so far and I'm a few hours in. It's very charming. I do hope the dev's see and read reviews so that can perhaps consider levelling the audio better - OR - taking that information into the next game they choose to create because if it's as fun as this one then I'm all over that!

Its a good visual novel with loads of funny and cute characters both female and male. In addition, the storylines for the characters were quite decent making the player want to learn more about them. The only downside I found to this game is that it is really grindy.

The whole battle aspect of this game is more or less pointless at the end of everything.

Still i think this game is worth picking up for the humor alone.

So much better than what I expected. Yangyang really outdid themselves here the voice acting is absolutely top tier and every character i s funny and interesting in their own way I expected comedy when I bought this but the closer you grow with the girls the more you see their struggles and once again gotta give props to the writers and voice actors that can make even a goofy guy like the player character project sincere emotion when things get tense. Also fun fact Amy best girl.
Repetitive, you do like 3 actions and have to go to sleep. A quick sleep button is needed or just less time for each action. It takes you two hours to get home from the plaza? I dont think so.

Got recommended this by a friend, thought it would be funny to date my "sister" and accidentally uncovered one of the greatest spy networks on the continent after I almost watched everyone I loved die.  Got a pardon for killing someone because I'm the protag though. 10/10

I recommend this game for reasons you might not expect. 
The good:
It's hella funny. The jokes are lighthearted and well thought.
The story line for each route has its unique setting. The more you get to know a girl, the more depth you find in the plot.
The voice acting is great. It really brings the game to life when two characters chat with one an another.
Great story. As a visual novel it does a good job at pulling you into the story.
Excellent art. The main characters are well detailed and there are many CGs to be had.
Scenes can be watched again in the menu once you've seen them.

The meh:
This vanilla game does not contain real adult content. The most you'll get to see is panty shots, some cleave and the mention of sexual intercourse/toys, and that's it. If you came here looking for that, then you'll need to install mods for it.
You're forced to choose only one girl at the time. Even if you try to romance all of them, the game prevent you from progressing with all of them.

The bad:
Voice volume sometimes go way lower than it can be heard.
Terrible store system. You can sell in bulk, but you can only buy one item at the time.
Your inventory and progression can only be seen at home.
Special events are hard to unlock. These only happen at a specific time and place. It is really difficult to get them without help of the console and a guide.
The godless grind. Each girl has 3 different endings, to which you'll have to start from scratch each time. Although, this is mitigated by the NG feature that allows you to keep your progress each time you get an ending.
Awkward conversation system. You may only chat with a girl once a day and each topic can be brought up six times. After that, you'll get prompted to a scene saying you need to choose a different topic and then you'll be slowly sent back to the point before you clicked on a girl.

Overall I think this game is an 8 out of 10. Just generally good. If you're into Visual Novels, give this a try.

I have to say... when I bought this game, I didnt expect much of it and was thinking about refund. However, after put some hours on this, I am glad that I didnt refund... One of the best dating sim/visual novel I ever played.

I went in this game having played the demo, I already enjoyed that but I actually enjoyed the game even more then I was expecting! I think the characters are really fleshed out and it's fun that I could experience every girls story and beat the game and get the romance ending in one play through on normal mode. I think the main pro about this game is how fleshed out and interesting the girls are. I genuinely haven't found a dating sim in a long time time that made me interested in the girls as characters. Good stuff!

might not be the best visual novel out there but i love it and proud to call it my very first entry in this genre, good story, interesting characters with good backstories (Vel ;_;), a nice turn based mini game. Would finish Vel questline as many times as it takes. 10/10

Despite all of the beautiful graphics and good voice acting, the gameplay loop is a boring waste of time. You can't skip combat animations or day skip animations, which make the game take 10x longer to finish. The combat is simple and the event system is kind of bad. Sometimes you just have to guess on what day and at what time a character's event can be triggered. You'll struggle to find all of the scenes, even with the somewhat complete steam guides this game has.

The characters and story fit into basic troupes and don't have much depth. It feels more like a visual novel in its early stages rather than a complete game/stat management dating sim. The music and soundtracks are not that varied but they're decent. Overall, I would give this game a 5/10. It's overpriced for an incomplete game. They probably blew their budget on voice acting instead of making a decent game with good story, which is more important for visual novels than having any voice acting at all.

Amazing dating sim, probably my favorite on the platform. It's hilarious, it's wholesome, it's dialogue is sharp as a knife.

Honestly a fun time-management VN with engaging dialogue, great waifu's, even better humor and an interesting turn-based combat. The story and gameplay has no right being as good as it is. The only downside is that if you're looking for an H heavy 'story,' there really isn't much emphasis on it. I'm 16 hours in and most of the art you get is ecchi at best, but honestly the game is so frickin good it's not even a major problem.

So I definitely recommend this to veteran  and beginners of the Visual Novel genre. It gives a refreshing feeling to play a visual novel with this concept. worth my money. no regrets buying this. With a fantasy twist, the added element of wars and kingdom era to include an RPG element is a good addition that makes it entertaining.
ALSO, for me, a good visual novel's secret ingredient is its heroines and the HEROINES HERE ARE REALLY A HIGHLIGHT. Each girl have their unique personality, traits and background. Getting to see their arcs and knowing them each add a spice and uniqueness of your playthrough. review:
I expected a cheaply made and thinly veiled porn game.

What I got was a genuinely heartwarming tale of a squires quest to find love before going off to war. I cannot overstate enough of just how good this game made me feel. In spite of it's seedy appearances it left me wanting to play through again, just in the hopes of giving my squire his success in his quest for love.

10/10 would recommend

So, what i like about this game is that i can relaxe and no need to overthink stuff. The game is simple enough, and yet still enjoyable. This is kind of game that i will pick if i want to relaxe for a moment, and escape from the reality hahahahaha......
Forgive my english, but i hope you get what i mean... Enjoy.....

Very fun game. All voices are great, suit their actors, and well versed... there was definitely effort put in the voice acting. MC was funny and likable, not OP in any way. All waifus were unique enough to want to try different playthroughs for romance, and each fun to romance. Their back stories and personalized storytelling actually make you want to see where the story goes.

+Voice Acting was amazing
+All characters involved were enjoyable
+Although the rpg aspect of this game is generic for a VN style game, the RPG minigames are extremely enjoyable, unique and somewhat challenging.
+Each waifu has their own personalized story making each playthrough a bit more different each time. Each of their stories is actually well thought out.
+There is a debug menu in the game you can always have up to actively find events so you don't get lost.
+Fun CG's
+If your wanted the hentai/ecchi theres not too much here in the base game, but there are add-ons that add entire hentai scenes making the playthrough worthwhile.

-Days are short but can be quite repetitive.
-If you don't use the debug menu you will likely miss a lot of events an opportunities, or find yourself save scumming.
-Unlike most visual novels, you actually need to put in work.
-Walk cycle can be a bit annoying on how slow it can be, but honestly at longest its 5 seconds of walking.
-Rare items don't always spawn.

Honestly, I wish all romance/novel/arc games were this way. 
Pros
-The diversity in the personality of the characters makes them feel like completely different people. Some romance visual novels have characters that are copy and paste with different hairstyles
-Voice acting is the absolute best. Full English and very funny. 
-There is combat in the game and I found it so amusing.
-Great supporting characters.
-The main menu is a piece of art.
-There is a harem ending.

Cons
-I ran into a bug where i had completed the game and started New Game+. I couldn't see the progress of one of the romance trackers so I basically had to guess how much they liked me.
-Sometimes the audio level changes in dialogue to the point where it is difficult to hear.

This game is now one of my favorite games I have played. Unfortunately, this set a new bar for games in this genre that most games won't live up too.

The first thing you'll notice in this game for sure will be the voice acting, and it is simply amazing! The voice actors convey a ton of emotions and have amazing delivery for punchlines and back and forth, which just elevates this beyond most Dating Sim I've played in a long time.

The graphics are great, with a very clear art and bright art style, detailed Backgrounds and animated sprites

Truly, the game has a ton of care put into it, and just for that alone, I can't help but recommend it.

I was going to wait until I finished a few different routes, but after finishing Vel's I had to come and write a raving review about this gem of a game!

First and foremost, this is one of the best visual novel hybrids I have ever played. Traditional VN's are great, but Love Esquire takes dating sim as well as RPG mechanics and mashes it all into one masterfully. Take your waifu on a date in the morning, go slay creatures in the afternoon with your homie Hugh, then train your stats in the evening (or swap those around to your heart's content). There's gift buying, dialogue choices, and DRASTIC replayability. Each route is so different that it feels like each girl is their own individual game. Also, it is completely voiced in English, and even Squire (the MC) is fully voiced. This game has heart, is unique, and is full of amazing story that gives countless hours of fun. As a university student who also works security, I have to be selective with what I play; I don't want to waste my time on a bad game with what little time I already have, and I can promise that Love Esquire is not only worth your money, but your time as well! Hats off to the developers!

Overall, I rate Love Esquire a 10/10

A hilarious and surprisingly heartfelt script more than make up for the repetitive RPG minigames and dodgy presentation ("skipVideoControllerText" instead of a proper prompt for example). Each character has a deceptive amount of depth to them, and most if not every single line is fully voice acted. 

The game is frequently part of key bundles, but is more than worth it even at full price.

Nice story telling all the routes are good specially with h-patch decent gameplay for a dating sim then lots of story content. but not a lot of guide on the internet so cant get all friendship endings and bad speacially the harem ending but for the romance ending there's a lot of guide to it.
After perilously grinding intelligence and strength for hours to become my best self for her, I got friendzoned by Giselle. I now declare myself the biggest fucking simp on this planet.

I was excited for this game and bought it right around release I knew nothing about it but I liked the trailer (I saw it a week ago);
I played it all day today and 8 straight hours later beat the playthrough; I've played a lot of VNs but there are some super bad parts about it other VNs have long sinced solved

1. No Ecchi; the stream description of the game states I wouldn't last 5 minutes.... 8 hours of play and there wasn't a single ecchi scene. I read later this is because of steam; but why advertise your game as mature or say you "won't last 5 minutes' if theres not even any hentai to speak of?

2. The combat is kind of dull; I know its not a real fighting based RPG but taunt heal taunt heal taunt heal AoE gets a little annoying after awhile

3. This part annoyed me ALOT it is a multilayered problem
First of all; despite giving you six save slots; you can only save in one at a time
Second; when I finished my romance with Amy the game actually forces you to begin her "ending" sequence as soon as you wake up; and this ENDS THE GAME. 

I literally had an entire month left and I was romancing 3 girls at once; then the game just decided to hard-lock me on the one ending because I didn't know any better. Multiple save files or an optional "hey do you want to do this" or even letting us see multiple endings would've fixed this

I like the art, story, characters, and I would've LOVED some ecchi but honestly; the faults with the save system, game pacing, lack of forewarning to end of game, etc make me ultimately not suggest this game

review:
I was a bit skeptical at first, as many dating sims have weak gameplay and focus too much on H-scenes for my taste. I was wrong - Love Esquire has neither of those issues and is a perfectly enjoyable visual novel.

+ Extremely funny, with great writing
+ Each girl is at least decently interesting
+ Most routes are well-made and transition neatly from comedy to dramedy
+ Battle system is simple but effective and quite original

- Gift system is simplistic and repetitive
- Most routes focus so much on the girl that the Squire, while a good character, gets little development himself

Also, the CGs use the same animation system as The Letter... which for me caused a mild anxiety every time such a scene played, as I wondered when some horrible monster would suddenly jump into it.

I'll be the first to admit it, I initially slept on this game. I saw the concept and thought this game would just be one big meme. And, well, it is, but it's so much more than that. This is a fantastic visual novel/dating sim that will make you laugh then cry. As of writing this, I've only completed one route, Giselle's.

Let's start off with what's [b] Great: [/b]
 *The voice acting is phenomenal. All of the VAs knock it out of the park, even the side characters are voiced! Plus, the main character having a voice adds so much to him. So much so, that I don't know if this game would work without his talent. Honestly, it's hard to imagine any of the cast with a different voice. 
 *The characters are all likable and unique. Each of the cast goes beyond the initial trope they appear to be. As you get to know each of the cast, you start to learn more about the MC and their world. The way everything melds together is brilliant. Even the characters I was less interested in at the beginning I came to care for even just from viewing snippets of their story.
 *The problems these characters face are strikingly real. This is a game that will make you underestimate it due to its memey nature and fan service. But oh man, when I mentioned you'd cry, there's a good chance you will. It's easy to get drawn into all the humor and tasteful anime tiddies, but you'll likely stay because the characters will grow on you.
 *The art is fantastic. The backgrounds are all varied and beautiful, each zone feels unique. You may not get to visit too many places, but they all add something different to the atmosphere of the town. Plus, each of the unlockable scenes are slightly animated.
 *And of course, the combat. The combat is fun, with each decision feeling meaningful with benefits and consequences. I found myself looking forward to the combat just as much as learning more about each character.

Now onto what's [b] Good: [/b]
 +The music is definitely enjoyable, but I found it a bit repetitive, but a kind of repetitive that gets stuck in your head whether you want it to or not.
+As much as I like the combat, it felt easier the stronger I got. That is [spoiler] the limit breaks feel too strong. The animations on them are absolutely amazing and hilarious, but perhaps too impactful for how often you can use them. I also felt like stunning on shield break makes the Good Boy Borks++ easier than the two before them. [/spoiler] That being said, I very much enjoyed the final boss fight. Also, it's worth noting that there are just a few enemy archetypes and they just become numerically stronger, save for one type.
+The overall gameplay cycle: The cyclical nature of how the game plays feels pretty good. The use of time, leveling up skills, hanging with people, [strike] stealing [/strike], collecting loot from the zones, they all make you feel like you accomplished something. The more you learn about the game's individual mechanics the more you can optimize and force the MC to have terrible sleeping habits!
 +The fanservice is tasty and well placed. I originally likened this game to Huniepop, but after beating it, it's nothing like it. This game respects itself, it allows the fan service to, I guess, augment the experience. From what I've played, it was used well and it wasn't obtrusive. I'm generally not a big fan of overt fan service, but I found myself enjoying the bits that appeared. However, you will not see them nearly as often as games like Huniepop or others, nor are they quite as lewd.

Now onto my [b] Complaints [/b]
 -Training skills is such a chore. Which, makes sense, but the further along you get in a line, the more times it takes to see improvement. Levels 1-6 really aren't too bad, but leveling in beast mode gets kind of rough. However, once your able to optimize your time leveling your skills just becomes tedious.
 -It's possible to skip over some of the dialogue if you progress a character's arc too fast. Talking to a character daily and learning more about them was on of the best parts for me, I was always sad when I missed a bit of their dialogue.
-There were multiple audio issues. Sometimes the volume spiked and got sharp, sometimes it sounded like they characters were recording in a tunnel. I know the developers are working on these issues, and hopefully they can be ironed out soon. Just, if you're wearing headphones, beware of random sharp spikes in volume.

I'm not usually the type to do more than one route in VNs, but this game left enough food on the table I actively want to dig in for second helpings. The new game + mode should help eliminate some of the tedious parts of the game too. If your a fan of visual novels and/or dating sims, this'll be a great game for you. The combat is unique and fun. The voice acting is amazing, the art is grea

review:
This is what a good visual novel is supposed to be. I hope future games use this one as inspiration.
- Interesting story.
- The dialog between chars is well-made: not too long, not too short; you never feel the urge to skip them to advance the game.
- Top+ tier voice acting. I can't stress this enough.
- Simple strategy game activities.

The only thing this game lack compared to other top tier VNs is diversity of meaningful choices in dialog, if you give this feature too much importance. Even so, I recommend you try Love Esquire; you are hardly going to regret it.

On side note: Giselle is too precious; if anything happens to her, I'm going to break everyone's legs. S2

What I expected:
An echi game about boobs with squire jokes.

What I got:
A f**king masterpiece.

Of all the novels I've read this ranks 5th currently.
In order:
G-Senjou no Maou -10/10
Muv Luv trilogy- 9.5/10
Fureraba - strong 9/10 (though 10/10 for a moege)
CLANNAD - 9/10
And now Love Esquire- weak 9/10
( my scale becomes exceedingly harsh towards either extreme which required me to allow for some qualification of 9's since a 9.5 is unequivocally a masterpiece of literature, while a 10 is literal perfection, meaning I would not have changed anything. In general to receive a 9 wholistically requires a novel to have an absurd degree of excellence throughout, while a route can usually get a 9 if it brings a tear to my eye somehow or another. I do not cry normally. Ever. )

And in this case I can really point to exactly why I think it's amazing.
It's the voice acting.

What may be literally the best voice acting performance I have ever heard in a game.
All because they decided to ACTUALLY FULLY VOICE THE MAIN CHARACTER.
*gasp*
I know. 
It's literally unheard of for a VN to be fully voiced like that. 
Let alone in ENGLISH.

Alejandro Saab delivered a stunning performance that singlehandedly elevated this game from 8ish territory into 9 territory.


Unlike most novels (read ALL) where the majority of text is unvoiced, often internal monologues of the MC, in this one the game plays more like an interactive film.

Sarcasm is not interpreted from [i] italics[/i]. Rather, it is ANNUNCIATED. A tender moment, has a tender voice, jokes include impressions, funny voices and more. And good lord, does this guy have a register.

Frankly, if you played this game muted... it would be pretty mediocre, but the voice acting is incredible. You CANNOT find another VN out there with this kind of English voice acting in it.

Moving on to routes:
Kamala 9.5/10 absolute masterpiece, best waifu, etc. Do hers first (the plot will seem less important otherwise).
Vel 8/10 
Amy 7/10
Giselle 7/10
Beatrice 6/10

Harem: 10/10 Quite possibly the best 10-15 minutes of the entire game

Overall, there was one excellent, one great, two good and one okay route. And then the harem which is just a nice big cherry on top to make you fall out of your chair laughing.

But the game has more than just a few binary choices that branch you off into a route.

There is a time-management dating sim type game where you have to run around town and actually talk to people, trigger events and work out your stats (STR, CHA and INT).

These stats allow you to do better in combat as you support your knight in frankly far better turn based combat than you might expect.

I've heard people complain about the grind, or difficulty... As a veteran of the Epic Battle Fantasy series I really had no issue, and there's story mode which doubles stat gain and health to make it easier if you don't care about the 4 or 5 campaign achievements.

It's clear that they spent a lot of time working with the voice actors to get the performances down pat. And it really shows, never has emotion and humor translated so seemlessly in a novel.

Again, I would really compare it to more of an interactive movie. Sure, you can read faster than the voices, but you're doing yourself a disservice if you do.

Overall, I bought this game because it was cheap, looked interested/entertaining, and it was a FAR better buy than I could have hoped.

TLDR: The game's great, the voice acting alone makes it worth buying. And heck, it's only $25 when I would have happily paid full VN fair (~$45) for it.
developer_response: Thank you so much! It's an honor for us that Love Esquire is among your top 5 VNs! ✧♡

review:
A charming Light novel that will keep you engaged with its wonderful cast of fun and likable characters. It took me right around 14 hours to complete my first run of the game with best girl Giselle and the experience was quite enjoyable. I was actually really surprised about the polish of the game with it's bouncy art and the fun voice acting. The side activity where it takes the appearance of a JRPG like Final Fantasy was a fun distraction as well. 

My only negatives in regards to the game is that the first playthrough felt like a slog sometimes.  Some characters character progressions felt like the game stalled when I ran out of topics to talk about with each girl. Plus, the drop rate on rare gifts is so frustrating. I also think the story could have used some more random story events to make the game flow better.

This game is an easy recommendation for light novel fans and when you reach new game+ the game really opens up. 

8/10
Honestly really touching and well made. The story and the characters lowkey had me invested, and I absolutely LOVE the voice acting! Art is super cute too. Great game, and an even greater VN.

The story is real cute and silly. There's a little bit typo and there're some parts that voice are too loud or too quiet.
Overall is good, but Wow... Even in visual novel, I've been friendzoned :( 10/10 friendzoned forever. Try hard, but cry harder ;(

Loved this game. Thought it was very addicting and I loved all the references to other video games and anime. The only problem I had was with the voice recordings. Sometimes the voice acting was super quiet and then it would sound like they are right up into the mic. Also the only way I could figure out how much gold I had was to spend time walking to training areas. Other then that I love the art style and the music. Hope to see more games like this in the future.

This game presents itself as trash, looks like trash, by all rights should be trash - but is actually a hidden gem, an absolute MASTERPIECE. I didn't really know what to expect, but this game not only has the trashy but hilarious humor you'd expect from its appearance (reminds me of Konosuba and borderlands 2) but also a surprisingly rich world, deep story, and complex characters that stay away from typical tropes. I was drawn in by how much fun it was to get to know the characters as the plot developed. The combat, though simple, was also pretty fun and challenging on hard mode. 

The art and animations are great, the music is atmospheric and perfectly suitable, and the voice acting is Amazing!

It has visual novel elements but I'm not sure I'd really call it a VN. It's more like a simple RPG and dating sim.

As it stands, I've played A LOT of visual novels, Eroge, A-games and H-games. Love esquire is the perfect combination of humor, interactivity, and of course romance and (with the downloadable content) ecchi/erotic content. I saw this before it was released and instantly fell in love. Played the beta and was left with wanting more and waited patiently for the release to steam and purchased it launch day. As of now with only 60 hours left I intend on constantly playing this game because it doesn't lose value when you replay it between the witty banter between the main character and love interest. At this point I'm just rambling for the steam awards badge tho.

TL;DR - Buy this game, fall in love, repeat.

Not meaning to white knight this game but;
With no means for disrespect, you can tell who plays on story mode and who plays on challenge mode when they complain about completion. It's even stated that it only takes 5500 affection to reach final level which leads to the final scene. It's why it's a time management game; do you wanna power through love and romance? do you want to complete the campaign and use the ocarina of love on your waifu of the save slot? In an age where people want fast results 5500 is a fair number. Challenge mode can still lead to early finish [spoiler](Found this out the hardway when I bad ended Giselle with 2 or 3 weeks left in last month)[/spoiler]

A great game if you like parodies/Satire of the VN/Dating sim genre. Had fun for a majority of my playtime, the special fighting animations can get a bit repetitive after a while and it can be pretty confusing at times when asked to do quests, but other than very small things I had a good time.


Get it if you like fan service, silly characters, and a pretty good story.

I have played a lot of dating sims / erotic visual novels throughout my life and I can say that for me "Love Esquire" it is easily among the best and funniest. A steamy comedy, mixes in a very good way the genres of visual novel, dating sim and puzzle game. The art and the animations are beautiful, the music complements each moment very well and the plot is quite high quality, with each route presenting many details, memorable scenes and some surprises. 
The characters are charismatic with a well-worked background and the protagonist, faithful incarnation of the "perv sidekick", reminded me a lot of Pee Wee of "Porky's" (yes, I'm old) but as the plot unfolds it begins to transform into a character more and more lovable. The combat can be somewhat deceiving, despite what it looks like, it should not be played as a JRPG but as a challenging puzzle game, where each movement must be mathematically calculated. The game has some points to polish: the performances of the voices are very well achieved but in several occasions (especially the protagonist) loses fidelity. The gameplay can be excessively grindy at some times and begs for the existence of a "New game +" but, in short, "Love Esquire" is a game that I fully recommend.
This game is absolutely adorable and the voice work is awesome.
The characters are good but my god the gameplay is a boring grind. It legitimately would've been more fulfilling to sit around farming in an MMO.
Much more than just a dating sim, you can clearly tell the developers put a lot of love and effort into this game.
I loved it because it's partly a VN, part a dating sim, and part a JRPG, plus it has a New Game + feature that makes playing Story Mode worth it!

Honestly a very amusing game. The writing is comedic and enjoyable, the voice acting is great (forgiving the inconsistent quality, though I know they're working on that.). I actually very much enjoyed the combat, though it can get a little repetitive by late game. Game could probably use the addition of a "fast forward" option as opposed to an outright skip in places, particularly when replaying scenes in the Extra Menu. The way things tie together after completing all five routes was both hysterical and brilliant. The one real complaint I have is that you made me like the Shrimp, you bastards, she went from bottom of my list to the top of it and I still don't understand how. That this is the worst I can say about it though should tell you pretty clearly how good the game is though. I've played a lot of Visual Novels of vastly different quality, and this is without a doubt one of the best (keep in clear mind it's tone.). 9/10, absolutely.

(For the sake of it, ignore my play time for this game, I played it largely offline due to some technical issues I was having at the time, and Steam never managed to fix it. My Achievements is a clearer indication of my progress.)

One of the best VN's for characters, and the voice acting is amazing. There were a lot of funny convos as well as sad ones. Achievement grinding was a bit too grindy for my taste but at least there were interesting endings. All of the characters were good, including the side characters, except Kamala, she was kind of mid. It's hard to even pick a favorite though, probably Amy then Giselle.

Fun game for the price. Has replayability. Free dlc for extras. Has workshop mod for even better extra. 
Not exactly what I thought I would get but fun nonetheless.

A very good game! Honestly the voice acting and art alone make it one of the best visual novels ever! However, I would like to say that even though I did some of the requirements/event triggers, I still did not get it some of the achievements! But I'm here for all the endings and that's what I did! <3! (Also to any first time players, do Amy's route last like I did, because there's a bunch of plot twists that if you play hers first, you'll never look at some of the characters the same 😅)

Originally started on mobile and fell in love. It was a lot easier (android like early this year) to grind for money and exp, but I still love it. Sending as much support as possible to this beautifully voiced and designed game! Can't wait for another game like this one!
this game is very fun but it kept locking up when i would be fighting and try to use the big moves. if they would fix that is would be an amazing game.

For what I was expecting, the story is well fleshed out and the characters are incredibly well written; not like this review. I did not expect to find a great world building story when I purchased this game but I appreciated. Really gets the creative juices flowing, oh and the witty banter and obscure references made it all the more enjoyable. #Lovethesquire #Caerulianblue #alkosifever

This is hands down one of the most fun I have had playing a dating sim/rpg. The characters are hilarious. The storyline for the various girls are interesting. There is a ton of replayablility so you can get through all the characters arc. The grinding and fighting are an interesting addition. The only real downside if the audio quality. The voice actors gave the characters a beautiful personality but sometimes the lines are so quiet you cant hear them and then other times the mic is being blown out and it sounds a little painful.
Its so hard keeping up with everything but you get used to it. Also I have a question...Is the woman who plays Serena the same VA that played Botan in Yu Yu Hakusho? o.O

Not enough of a porn game to keep me playing for that and not good enough of an RPG to keep me playing for that. Game was slow to me and boring. If I had more character options, better gameplay during combat, or more time to interact with the other characters for the dating sim stuff it would have been more interest.

Surprisingly deep and hilarious at the same time. You can really get stuck in the world building and you learn to love nearly every character. It has good replayability due to the different character routes. It is a must play for visual novel lovers.

I enjoyed this game. The story is very simple but I found the characters to be endearing. The vocal performance they got for the main character is excellent but it is occasionally undermined by mics peaking or the volume suddenly dropping out. There are no sex scenes in the game and a successful romance results in a fade-to-black in the ending sequence. All aspects of the game are quite simple. That can be refreshing, but you should know what you're getting into.
Totally enjoy the story!!! The girl characters sounds are so perfect. The game will make you laugh while also warming your heart. This game is Hidden Gold among all others Visual Novel games.

Good game but there are quite a number of bugs. Love the MC, his Voice Actor puts a good emphasis on his role, a goofy and dorky but somewhat serious character (MC looks like a H* protag with the stereotypical no eyes look, but is a good guy). The game mostly has spoken lines, though there is a problem with the poor audio mixing where you can't hear the MC at times or the other characters you romance. In addition of audio peaking (voice too loud). Besides the MC, the romancing characters have good VAs, which bring out their character.
[BUGS]
This is where the majority of the cons I come across.
When you romance and reach pink heart status from one character the number of events to progress with the other characters drops or disappears for a while. Especially when doing new game plus.
Another con might be grinding to give gifts, when there is no surprise events, to build up the relationships.

Strangely addictive with such a wonderful narrative and interesting characters and decisions to make. Highly re-playable and worth every minute. Wonderful game and art work from the developers and look forward to more like this in the future.

[list]
[*] [b]Holy Fertility God's Ball![/b] I'm impressed that game is full voiced down to every characters' dialog, including males and supporting characters! (which are not the case in JP's VN)
[*] Although I'm not usually listen to English dub, but this one's voice acting is good. But If i'm going to nitpick there is some minor volume normalization issue between voice clips.
[*] The character depth and story is VERY well made and planned.
[*] I used 16 hr to finish my first playthough. (this was with speedhack help to speed up screen transition and animation)
[*] Saw other mention 100+ hr to get 100%.
[*] I was greedy on first play and tried to advance various girls story, that landed me in bad end, as expected. But I dont felt it as punishment, the game is very generous on NG+, it allows you to carry everything to new game and continue unlock what's left. So I'm not even upset.
[*] HAREM end exists, but you need to play for [spoiler]5[/spoiler] NG+ playthough to get.
[/list]

All in all, it exceeds my expectation, gives fresh date sim and VN experience. And lots of content!

PS. Because the RPG minigame of Love Esquire, I had an itch to dig RBO (Ragnarok Battle Offline) to play again. The reference is too obvious! including those Poring and BGM!
honestly its worth it for the story and is actually pretty good  but can be a bit grindy and missions can get in the way off the quests

Story is great, very funny, with surprisingly serious and interesting story arcs. A lot of effort was put into this, and mostly everything is voice acted, and well too. Worth it at full price, even better on sale. 5 Stars

Pros: Very funny, entertaining game concept, unique protagonist, surprisingly good stories (which actually get quite serious).

Neutral: The voice recording isn't the best, but the voice acting makes up for it. The characters aren't always appealing, but they always get there.

Cons: A bit grindy or repetitive much of the time. Impossible to get all of the scenes with a girl in a single playthrough, and there's no way I'm willing to sit through all of that again to try to find what I missed.

Extra Notes: I do not recommend giving gifts. They speed things along and you end up missing stuff because you progress to the next arc too fast. I recommend finishing the dialogues with all of the characters. Really good, plus they give achievements. And if you want to enjoy the full romantic experience with the character(s) of your choice, make sure to visit the Workshop and subscribe to Hip to Be Squire Uncensored.

Growing up, I LIVED on sim dating browser games but as the years went by, less and less started being created. I would search for dating sim games and it would only take me to visual novels with limited choices if any. Very thankful for steam recommending this game because it has everything I wanted. Romance, Great art style, Jobs to buy gifts, Pretty characters to romance, Comedy, Skill building etc etc. EVEN VOICE ACTING!! Like .. FULL voice acting. 
I'll admit I originally downloaded this game for free, HOWEVER, I loved this game so much that I had to purchase it the same day. 
I hope more games very similar to this get made. The people beg. I beg. Please. If I wanted to only read, I'd buy a kindle.

I want to thank the developers for doing an amazing job. You have yourself a lifelong supporter.

Came for the waifus, stayed for the plot, just one route ended up way longer than expected. Plenty of content for the money if you want to go through all the girls.

Not a big player or reviewer of VNs -- but the friendzone ending will still likely be more common if on a blind playthrough? With 4 major choices that need to be all correct to unlock the romance ending.

Great voice-overs overall, even for the nerdy MC and Hugh. Comic relief was present without feeling too forced. Combat and grind is a bit tedious but not unmanageable -- not many minigames in between, but a plethora of dialogue and all the transitions are smooth.
This game is a great relief from the working world where women never talk to me. 5 stars

I'm gonna try to make this as objective and spoiler-free as possible.

This Visual Novel has excellent voice-acting, amazing art, and cute music. It has a very decent narrative, but since the game's relatively short (You can get to an ending in between 2 and 4 hours of play) it doesn't tell a very complicated story.

Not that that's a bad thing, of course. It's a short and sweet story, but I felt like sometimes it tried to tell more about its world and build a story for the relationship of some characters than it could because of its shortness. Of course, due to this short-lived type of narrative, choices aren't that important, but again, it's not a big deal.

I wish it were longer, but because I would've liked it to expand on the lore and relationship between characters, and sometimes there's some glimmer of a subplot here and there, although it doesn't go all the way. It's worth your money. Just for that beautiful art... My god!

It was a great experience. I loved it and it's an LGTB+ story, so props for that! We need more of those :)

Perfect Gold is a romantic/Slice-of-Life VN.  Although I'm really not into Slice-of-Life Visual Novels I really enjoyed Perfect Gold.  The main reasons where:

-  ABSOLUTELY GORGEOUS looking game.  I mean the artwork is just GORGEOUS, beautiful, and I could go on and on and on about it.  My hats off to Ryuu the lead Artist.  I am sure that her career will take off with this kind of quality of work I'm sure she will be busy for a long time to come with job offers left and right.
-  SUPERB Voice Acting.  Let me say this for starters.  This is THE FIRST Visual Novel that I actually ENJOYED the ENGLISH VOICE OVERS.  99% of the Visual Novels I've played have really bad English Voice Acting, and I always switch to the Superb Japanese Voice acting.  Well, in this one there is no choice to pick Japanese but even if there was, I would pick the English.  The Voice Overs are amazing.  And there is sooo much passion put into the voice acting and that's one of the reasons that I really enjoyed Perfect Gold.  Not to mention that the Voice Acting is accompanied by lip-sync.  So that makes it feel even more cinematic than it is.
-  Even though the story is Slice-of-life, and would seem to be not that great also and abit generic, it's the dialog, and the warm, passionate, voice acting that carry this Visual Novel.  And the dialogue is pretty well written and gets emotional at times, again mostly because of the SUPERB Voice Acting, and how AMAZING it looks.  Honestly I think it's one of the best looking 2D/3D art I've seen in any VN.  Gorgeous Sprites, backgrounds, and CGs.  Also has a music box in the extras to listen to the tracks which are pretty decent.

The VN IS choice based so as you would have guessed it DOES have more than one ending.  However keep in mind that this IS a short Visual Novel.  I finished all routes in 5 hours, and I MAY have been AFK for an hour.  So it's pretty short.  

But in conclusion I definitely recommend playing it, and I will have to give it a solid 8/10.

This game is soooooo cute and warm and sad and happy I just I can't even talk about it properly.

Made me smile big time. Highly recommended if you're a flowery lesbian like me..

This was so cute! Great voice acting and visuals (loved how the sprites/backgrounds subtly moved), and while I almost wish it had been longer because I was attached to the characters, it worked well as a short story. A lot of wlw visual novels feel either hypersexualized or too subtle (aka, subtext & not explicitly obvious it's romance), so it's refreshing to see a well-written romance. I was a little nervous going into this because I had a lot of issues with The Letter (another visual novel by this studio), but they really improved and did a great job with this game.

My only minor complaint is that the choices felt unnecessary—it's pretty obvious which ones lead to the good/romantic ending, and that's what most people who buy this are playing it for, so it felt like they were added in to avoid having this be a kinetic visual novel. I get it, since a lot of people are put off when there's no choices, but I don't have any desire to go back and get the "bad" ending.

But overall, I'd highly recommend playing this! I'd love to see more games like this from this studio.
This game is so good that I feel like a bit underpriced, the visual is stunning, voice acting is remarkable. Not only a great game but a unforgettable experience.

I'm a bit conflicted...
On one hand the the story is ok, the characters likeable, art is nice to look at and the music is nice to listen to.
On the other hand, the way it's presented, with constant switches in the chapters between reminiscing and character POVs made it rather confusing...
All in all rather OK experience, nothing extraordinary.
(side note: don't believe the NSFW tags: the most explicit thing shown or talked about is kissing)

Pros :
+ Gorgeous arts and cg's
+ Smooth character animation
+ Fully voiced and great voice acting
+ Story is kinda light but enough to make you feel emotional
+ Dialogues is used to express the character emotion, feeling and thoughts (instead of monologues)
+ Music is okay
+ Trading Cards!!

Cons :
- Cannot go back to previous text/dialogues (can only use log)
- Backgrounds are repetitive (there are only 5 or so backgrounds)
- Transition to past/present is kinda annoying, it's taking too long

Overall this is a must buy for visual novel fan, i've only played a bit of vn games but i can say this is the best among those that i have played.

Seeing their relationship blossom as I play this in my basement made me realize I am destined to die a slow, terrifying death alone with nothing to show for my life.

But yeah great story, great characters, amazing art 10/10 would recommend.

Extremely cute fantasy slice-of-life yuri social drama. A bit on the short side, but fully voiced. Beautiful graphics, slightly animated.

My main complaint is the heavy use of flashbacks in the story telling, sometimes it works to reinforce the themes of the present time, at other times it's jarring or distracting from the story.

I played this offline so my game hours will not reflect properly.


This is actually a super sweet story, voice-acted and gorgeous artistry. It's about two girls and their realizing what they want and don't want. I only found the two routes, would of loved a MUCH longer game, it was so sweet. Achievements are easy to get and happen as you progress, if that's your sort of deal :) I don't want to spoil the story, so I won't say too much, I recommend it though.

Compared to other yangyang mobile games this was a very short and straightforward VN. Has great quality live 2D art and many CGs while the english voice acting is top notch as always!
A simple story of how 2 girls like each other but can't convey it well. I think they could have done more with the story but for what it is, it's nice.

[h1]Fire is the stars we look up to at night, giving us hope, guiding our way.[/h1]

[h1]Story[/h1]
In the city of [b]Castlecoast[/b], magic and alchemy are used for many things and there is a college where students can learn about them. [b]Audrey Clary[/b], an alchemy student and [b]Marion LeRue[/b], a rebellious elemental magic student don’t really get along. One day they got into a fight and they both had to go to detention. However, [b]Marion[/b] was not going to sit around because the [b]Sunflower Festival[/b] was going on and she had someone she wanted to meet.
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3043268513
Audrey did not want to miss it either and they both decided to escape detention to go and find out what events the Sunflower Festival has to offer. While overcoming their hardships and finding out why these two students started to hate each other.

[h1]Pros and Cons[/h1]
[b]Pros[/b]
[*]A genuinely nice story with very nice characters
[*]You play as both Audrey and Marian and learn from their perspectives
[*]Characters are fully voiced
[*]Animations are smooth and well done for both characters and backgrounds
[*]Beautiful art style that is very nice to look at
[/list]

[b]Cons[/b]
[list]
[*]Some plot points were not resolved throughout the story
[/list]

[h1]Story/Characters[/h1]
[b]Perfect Gold[/b] allows us to follow both [b]Audrey's[/b] and [b]Marion’s[/b] perspectives throughout the events of the [b]Visual Novel[/b]. You will be told events both in the past and present on how they both met, other people’s reactions to their relationship, and their falling out. You see how a high noble family functions in society and how some other people function around them.

You also see other people’s reactions on how they view other people, but that development is the side story stuff that the two protagonists deal with. They are dealing with the struggles in their families' relationships and the relationship between the two protagonists. They both specialize in their very own fields but have different goals they want to go for.
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3042949926
[b]Audrey[/b] must deal with the standards that the higher-class people have and what they think of people in the lower-class system. They are rich people who think they are all high and mighty and think of the lower-class people poorly. She also has to deal with her family and how they may think of the relationship between her and [b]Marian[/b], although we don’t exactly see them in the story. It’s still something that is worth thinking about.

[b]Marian[/b] has to deal with what those people think of her and the strain she has with her sister, [b]Simon[/b]. Who has not been staying connected with her and she thinks [b]Simon[/b] thinks nothing of her, but has plans to meet her during the [b]Sunflower[/b] festival. She is also not from [b]Castlecoast[/b], so other people tend to view her much differently.

The characters are also fully voiced and they all sound amazing, I had zero complaints with the English voice acting.

The only issue I had with the story of [b]Perfect Gold[/b] was that some of the plot points that the [b]Visual Novel[/b] highlighted were not resolved. I would have loved to see a conclusion to what [b]Audrey’s parents[/b]' feelings towards her relationship with [b]Marian[/b], or even what other people thought of [b]Marian[/b] herself. However, none of these ever got resolved and it would have made a nicer impact on the story and could have even deepened our understanding of the characters that we saw throughout the [b]visual novel[/b].

[h1]Graphics and Performance[/h1]
The art style for [b]Perfect[/b] Gold is beautiful, it’s extremely detailed with it’s backgrounds and they are animated beautifully. I tested the game on an NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 3GB, AMD Ryzen 5 1500X Quad-Core Processor 3.50 and 16 GB of RAM. I experienced no issues during my playthrough with everything running smoothly.
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3042953372
[h1]Final Verdict[/h1]
If you are looking for a nice, short and sweet yuri [b]visual novel[/b], then I think [b]Perfect Gold[/b] would be a fine choice. It’s only about two hours long but I definitely had a wonderful experience. I have to also give the [b]Visual Novel[/b] props for making good character development in a short amount of time as well, something that other games in this genre seem to struggle with.

[quote] C'mon! Do you really want to miss out on an adventure at [url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/5297658/]Life Needs a Little Sin [/url] with the valiant trailblazer - Marion LaRue[/quote]

Absolutely adorable yuri visual novel where you get to see the story unfold and decide it's fate from the perspective of two lovely ladies, Marion LaRue and Audrey Clary. They are students of the magical arts, trying to battle between the expectations of family versus the passion to do what's best for yourself.

The story is quite short and heavily relies on flashbacks, which is a cheap way of storytelling in my books, but the story in it's simplicity is boosted by the fully voice acted characters, animated CGs and sprites and the over-all lovely artstyle. The character design in this visual novel is also very pleasing to the eye and you can tell that the characters all have varying personalities and are not cut from the same mould. 

My only gripe with the game would have to be, that the choices felt a little unnecessary and there were not enough endings. The story can end in two ways, a happily ever after and a less happily ever after, but still happy. It would have been far more interesting of a story if the two endings would have been polar opposites, or if there were multiple endings i.e. romance end, friendship end and bad end. 

Overall, I would highly recommend people to play this game! If you are a fan of cute gay girls doing cute magical things or you want to have your heartstrings tugged by array of emotions, this is definitely for you. The price is also very good for the amount and quality of content you get.

The animated character sprites and voice acting are The Future Of Visual Novels, I swear, they add so much to the experience.

The characters are sympathetic and relatable, and they have great chemistry. I wish the story were longer and deeper, because I really liked what was there and wanted more.

So what can I say. There will be "no" spoilers in this (maybe character names, but I hope they count.) just opinions. Love them or hate them, they are just that.

[h1]The Good.[/h1]

[u]The story.[/u]

It is lovely. It is well put together, well executed and flowed really well. 

[u]The Characters[/u]

These were believable and there was so much love in all the characters. It felt like they really cared about each other. Even Professor Beaufort was really kind in her own way. 

[u]The Artwork.[/u]

This also really good. The colours were bright and vibrant and went well with the whole flow of the story. There were also enough of them that not too many were repeated. 

[u]The Voice Acting.[/u] 

They did a really good job with the characters. I especially loved Audrey's character actor! All of them seemed to really take pride in the lines they were speaking

[u]The Music.[/u]

It was good and set the mood for each chapter well.


[h1]The Bad[/h1]

[u]The Story[/u] 

It was not really believable. Again, without giving too much away, the little conflict that there was in it was not done well. It was washed over with very quickly and after about 10 lines of dialogue all was well again. Don't get me wrong, I love cutesy, but this went a little too far at being too cutesy, Also, the novel was VERY short considering how long the delays were in it coming to Steam. I was very disappointed in the short length. I was hoping for at least 10 hours or so. 

[u]The Music[/u]

It was good and set the scenes well, but there was nothing really spectacular about it. The only memorable tune was the one they played in the videos on the adverts, all the rest were just there. 

[u]The Characters.[/u]

The main characters believeability (not a real word, I know,) was something that you could easily question. I guess it was because of the very short story, but boy it was rushed. You never really got a chance to know them. Or really how they met. Yes, you get snippets, but that is about it. It felt like you were on a rollercoaster ride, or the story writers were getting bored and just wanted to get this finished which is a real shame.

[h1]The Ugly![/h1]

[u]The Sound Levels![/u]

These were a mess! The voice acting sound levels were either soft or very soft. Yes, I get that the character voice acting is supposed to be secondary and the whole purpose of a visual NOVEL is to read the thing, but, if they went to the expense of getting voice actors (and, to be honest I really do like it when they get voice actors in,) at least let us be able to hear them! Some characters you could hear not very well, but ok, but the main characters voices were just WAY too soft! I could barely hear them. And there was no way of making them louder, either as they were on maximum volume. Yet, the background music and the "sound effects" were way too loud. I had to literally have the background music volume nearly off to be able to hear the voices. It was so frustrating. 

[u]The Engine.[/u]

This is no renp'y! For people not knowing what I mean by this is that in renp'y VN, you get lots of nice, options. In Unity (which is what they used here,) you get far fewer. Like, for example the option to turn off transitions, the option to change the text size. The option to be able to save screenshots. That sort of thing. Not being able to change the text size was a real let down for me. My 50 year old eyes are really quite bad and, as I have to sit about 6 feet from my monitor, having such small text made it really difficult for me to read. (which is why I was so disappointed in the LOW voice acting volumes.) OK, so these lack of options may not be the Unity Engines fault, but I have yet to play a Unity Engine VN that lets you change the options above.)

Anyway, this review is FAR longer than I intended it to be and will probably be ignored by many, but to all those that have persevered, thanks!. Berate me at will! I wish steam had a thumb left option as I really don't want to not recommend this game, but, sadly, the cons outweigh the pros and I am truly disappointed in that considering how long I had to wait for the flipping thing. But, hey ho.!
developer_response:
Hello Yogibear,

Thank you so much for playing Perfect Gold! We'd just like to address a couple of points for improvements:

- The sound levels. Can you please provide us with more information? How soft were the voices? Was it way too soft that you literally can't hear what they're saying? Were you using the game's "default" sound levels? How loud is your system's volume? 

During our beta demo, we received complaints from players that the output was too loud that it distorts/breaks the VO, that's why we had to lower it down (just by a bit). We tested the new volume on different speakers and headsets, and it seemed okay, since nobody complained about the volume anymore. That's why upon reading this, we're quite surprised that there's still issues with the VO's volume (and a little frustrated tbh; we're scrathing our heads here, haha). >:

- The engine. We didn't think the option to change text sizes was necessary, but I guess we were wrong! We'll see if we can implement this in our future games!

Thank you so much for your review, and we're sorry the game fell short of your expectations. We'll do our best to improve! :)

review: Perfect Gold is a heartfelt visual novel that I wish was a little longer. I loved Marion and Audrey relationship and the world building.

A short but very sweet visual novel about two young women in love. I absolutely loved it and it made my lesbian heart so happy. If you are sapphic give it a go, you'll absolutely love it.

Only complaint is that there isn't more of these cuties together, I would love a bonus chapter of them later on in life.

[b]My Score:[/b] 10/10

[h1]Prologue[/h1]
City of Castlecoast, the capital of the kingdom where the prestigious school of magic is located, where several great magicians of history had been trained, and now home to two girls, Marion LaRue a country girl who is a prodigal elemental magician as well as her sister specializing in fire magic, and Audrey Clary, a student in the course of alchemists, heir to a noble family that for several generations has specialized in this field of magic and therefore has high expectations about the young woman. These two girls who guided our history, while exploring the past and present of these young women, who were so close since the first day, until something happened in the past of the two to exactly one year. 

A year ago at the annual sunflower festival, where in the midst of many wonders and smiles the two confess their love for each other. But something between this happy past and the not-so-happy present, with the two friends now seeing each other literally as a rival, avoiding and literally doing things to irritate the other. However, the same time of year has brought the sunflower festival again, and nothing will stop these two girls, not even a detention, from carrying out their plans and who knows, solving problems of the past, rekindling the pink flame of love, among these two girls so different and yet so close. 

[h1] Characters[/h1]
Our history not only focuses on the present two characters, but also presented previous events, from the point of view of the two. On the Marion LaRue side, we met a country girl with tanned skin and blond hair, full of desire to explore the capital and make new friends, but with no acquaintance in the capital or any family to whom she could ask for help, or so it was. she even thought she would start approaching the class representative on her first day of school, a student in the alchemist class, and that she would soon make the young woman feel hotter than any fire can. 

On the other side we have Audrey Clary, with her white skin, long purple hair and pink eyes, a student of the most prestigious course of alchemists and heir to a family with a long tradition in this area, in addition to being representative of the class from day one. The perfect life for a princess most would think, but not Audrey herself, who despite everything, is not happy turning things into gold, and prefers to use her powers to heal others but hides it from everyone, until the day that she met a young woman from the countryside who controls flames, with whom she can finally open up for the first time. These two girls each with their own story that we will accompany, together with some other characters, in this romantic story. 

[h1] Art and sound[/h1]
Along with the story, one of the things that I liked the most, the art is especially beautiful, both for the scenarios and some featured items, but it is especially beautiful for the characters, who both in the dialogues and in important arts are constantly on the move , leaving no monotonous scene. In addition we also have the incredible soundtrack that I liked a lot, the point of purchase in the store just to be listening, over and over again, even without playing the VN. Completing the sound part with a great dubbing in English, performed by all the women who gave their voices to the characters.

[h1] Conclusion[/h1]
Perfect Gold - The Alchemy of Happiness is the first Yuri Visual Novel published by the Philippine studio Yangyang Mobile, which not only maintained the quality of they previous works, but also delivered a great and pleasant experience with this love story between two young women, who despite the problems they manage to find happiness after all, having pleased me from beginning to end, and a work that I recommend to any fan of the Yuri genre, because despite being a little short, it lives up to every penny invested in this work, which I'm sure the left with a smile on everyone face at the end. ^^
