short and sweet story around 2-4hours of gameplay, pleasent music, beautiful art and very nice voice acting

I wasn't expecting too much from this when I bought it, other than it would be a cute little story. I was actually surprised by how well done it was from the backgrounds and art to the voice acting! (especially Audrey). 

While you don't get to do much other than make a few choices, I found myself finishing it in one sitting! 10/10 would play again <3

[h1]Perfect Gold Review-Good for Yuri Fans[/h1]
Perfect Gold is a Philippines Fantasy visual novel written about the relationship between two girls.
[b]Presentation[/b]
Many EVN(English language Visual Novels) lack quality but I can tell in terms of the presentation, a lot of hard work was put into making it look good. The art is pretty, there is animated cg and sprites,there is even voice acting(very good too for English, the two MCs Marion and Audrey were good!). This VN doesn’t look cheap(it's only 7.99). I am really glad the developers put a lot of work into making it look the way it does it is something lacking from EVN. Now sound(music) is the only thing that did not stand out but it was fine.
[b]Writing[/b]
The overall story is told with flashbacks and present day scenes which can get annoying when that transition card between them (also between povs) happens so damn fast and frequently and I have to wait but at least the card looks good and is animated. The main conflict was written very standardly as you would see in romance/coming of age stories. Also the resolution did feel too quick and kind of cheesy the way it was written which made me roll my eyes. 
I was not expecting anything engaging in terms of story; all can be forgiven because the focus should be on the characters.The two characters are so cute! I loved their interactions a lot. This VN is too short and quick, after the conflict was resolved I wanted more cute dates and interactions and [spoiler]They only had one on screen kiss ;-;[/spoiler] give me more cute things pleaseee
[b]Final Thoughts[/b]
This VN was extremely short ( my time says <2hrs) . The steam page says the choices affect the ending but other than the extra cg and dialogue, I got 100% gallery with the normal ending. Someone please tell me different however the frequent transition screen is making me not want to go back unless it is a secret ending with more kissing.  For me the price was okay because I really enjoyed the two characters and the presentation. Plus I love yuri. If you are not a yuri fan but curious I would recommend trying to see the demo to see if you like the two MCs. However I do not see how this would appeal to non yuri/romance fans and should not want to spend 7.99/2hrs unless they were a fan of gl. I would really like to read something like this but longer adding more cute interactions.
if ur thinking of buying this game do it, its like a warm cup of tea on a cold day

A super sweet and wholesome visual novel, full of happiness, love, drama and even some sadness. It's well written with good voice acting, and a fitting soundtrack as well. I also love the art style, I think its super cute. This has probably been one of my favourite visual novels in recent memory. I think its well worth the price.

If I had to say one bad thing about this game it might be that the time jumps to past and present might've happened to often because how short some chapters were. Its not even that big of an issue (or at least I didn't think it to be) 

So yeah if you're looking for a cute wholesome yuri visual novel I do recommend this one!

review: As I was reading this aloud with friends in a xbox party my heart could not handle the emotions that the characters expressed from Audrey's struggles with social and familial expectations and Marion's doubt about being good enough. On top of that the art was beautiful and animated CGs god I felt spoiled. 10/10.

I've played a lot of yuri VNs and this one, my sapphic friends, this is one of the best.

It's short, but the length it has is filled with absolute quality. Great voice acting; beautifully animated backgrounds, characters AND CGs; lovely music; and stellar writing. It's a character driven and intimate romance and the characters are absolute chef's kiss. Marion and Audrey, you both deserve the best--you both deserve each other.

I think my biggest complaint about it is that the story jumps back and forth in the timeline and I honestly don't see the reason why. It would probably worked better as a linear story instead of going back and forth. Also, you hit the skip button when you try to open the steam overlay, so I'd move that button.

All in all, a wonderful experience with absolutely excellent emotional payoff. I will definitely be following these guys and I'm looking forward to their next work.

an amazing experience, i didn't expect the voice actors of their characters to be so talented, i can feel that their characters are alive, and tell me stories. 

the development of each character's story is also not processed in a hurry, i can capture every moment of the growth of character relationships and the development of conflicts which turn out to be deeper than i expected. 

and lastly I am amazed at the character illustrations and backgrounds which are really beautiful and mesmerizing!

Perfect Gold is a super refined visual novel. The UI is the best I have ever seen and the graphics were just perfect. Most of the lines are voiced by voice actors too and they absolutely killed it. The story was pretty cute as well and the soundtrack was nice. I really like how this visual novel made, it felt luxurious and you can tell that a lot of effort has been put into this game. Definitely recommended!

Cute game! The beginning was a little confusing, going back and forth between the past and the present, but once I got the full picture it was pretty easy to piece everything together. It isn't super long, so I'd recommend it if you've got an afternoon to kill.

The artstyle is gorgeous, this mix of 2d and 3d, how immersive the universe is, how good the voices fit the characters... yeah, this is a bit of a sugarcoatted romance, but I love how it managed to add layer to its characters, to make such a good coming of age story in such a short span ! 

It's refreshing to see genuine wlw love stories, only about LOVE, without this hyper-sexualisation of women in yuri. Proof that yuri, when made properly and caring about its characters, can deliver wonderful stories. So no, sorry, no woo-ha moment, only sweet, pure love. 

You'll feel like watching a really good romantic movie. 

The angsty teen in me would have wanted a "bad" ending, or at least a bittersweet one, and more impact with our choices, but given the price and the quality of my gameplay, I can never recommend this game enough. 

15 year old me would have killed for a game like this, and I'm so happy that baby gays can experiment it. 

Don't hesitate ! <3
One of the better Yuri visual novels that I have played. Great characters with good voice acting. Interesting story with good writing combined with cheap price makes this easy recommendation.
Has to be the gayest game I've played, very bubbly and sweet, would recommend. The art's very good and so is the story.

A cute visual novel. It's fairly short but also small priced, if you like yuri stuff, it's a nice experience. Story is kinda classic romance with pretty common modern tropes, it does the job. The visuals are stunning, and the animation interstellar, making the VN very lively with characters animations, gorgeous special scenes, and even moving objects part of the decorations.

Recommended.

Perfect Gold is a great little yuri visual novel with superb voice acting,the game has a beautiful visual style that has the characters moving and swaying like they are real people it really adds to the immersion of the story.The game is essentially a romance love story between two girls from very different backgrounds afraid to express their love due to societal standards;it makes for a interesting dynamic between Marion and Audrey(main characters) with how they grow and eventually begin to accept each others differences despite what people around them say.The VN has alot of themes that young people face on a daily,growing up to meet the expectations that others want from you,feeling underwhelming as a person and too those around you,accepting the way you truly feel about something despite the popular outlook Perfect Gold has it all!.Overall Perfect Gold is a great play for anyone that loves youth themed romance stories and wants their hearts strings tugged on.My only gripe was that PG was short I wanted more of the world and more of Marion and Audrey hopefully we can get a PG2 from YangYang other than that thumbs up go play it.
A bit "cliché", but still a warm, cozy story. Animations and voice actresses were amazing.
Beautiful, well voiced and a fantastic story. On the shorter side, perfect for a night of free time to curl up and enjoy. Would love to see a longer sequel of sorts with these delightful characters.

I backed that game on kickstarter and saw it coming to life. At some point, there was a discussion regarding the price as the VN takes around 5 hours to go through and they were afraid that the price was too high for it and wanted to give $2 back to us as they were contemplating lowering the price for the market. I refused the discount and have absolutely no regret, on the contrary, I'm very sad that they lowered the price of this amazing VN. 

Mind you, we're speaking of a fully voiced and animated VN, they chose live 2D to animate the characters so you get to see their whole body and the transitions between poses, the CGs are animated (!), the story is pretty good, I didn't notice any big typo or encountered any issue with that game. 
It's fluffy, it's soft, and I want to see those characters growing up and learn what they become in the future. I want to see Marion's fireworks and Audrey facing her family. It's a good story and it deserves to be paid full price on contrary of numerous games which don't deliver for twice this price or more.

At the end of the day, what I would like is for you to consider giving a try to this VN at a full price. Let's say that you pay and you give it a good hour of reading and decide if you want a refund and continue later or if I'm right and you'll devour the rest of the story like I did. 
There aren't this many indie companies producing VN of such quality, we should support those as much as we can. 
I can't wait to back another project from the developers, I know their style and I know they will deliver a polished game with an excellent story, whether it will be horror themed or yuri fluff.

Bought this game and played through the entire thing with the boys, honestly one of the best visual novels I've played. Love Esquire was fantastic and thankfully this was on par with it. Looking forward to any future Yangyang Mobile releases.

Perfect Gold is an adorable visual novel with a few choices (but not many). It is more about the story itself and less about one's control over the narrative. 

Importantly to this review, I greatly enjoyed this story. The characters and their choices feel real to me. Their pain and anger is understandable, and while the story follows a classic trope (love of one with different social standing), it was done in a way that felt fresh and new to me.

The voice acting was engaging. I enjoyed the characters and their interactions. There were a few twists, as some characters revealed greater depth along the way. These were not dramatic turns, exactly, but were welcome surprises that felt real. The ending payoff feels earned and fitting. I was definitely pulling for the girls the whole time.
I will admit it made me cry multiple times. Not difficult to do but this is a real great one. So glad I played it. Probably will end up replaying it in a few years to see them again

If I had to put this game into one word, it would be personal. The intimate story of two people torn apart byt their own struggles coming to terms with who they are, who they want to be and how they want to live their lives. It really got to me. I dont think I've cried as much in any other piece of media, the writers felt like they peered into a soul and saw my own troubles and put them into the form of entertaining and well written characters. The only issues I have are tiny nitpicks like the constant timejumping for the first 2/3 really made thigns a bit too confusing at first, even if its very clear why its like that, and that some information that would've been really great to include in the game is only in the artbook dlc. But those are the tiniest complaints, I really cannot put enough praise on this vn, if you want something gay and cute, here you go, if you want something introspective about responsibility and self worth, here you go. If you wanted both, this will be some of the best time you will ever spend. Please give this game a shot, this review is so long and rambly if you made it this far you're clearly interested.

Perfect Gold? More like Perfect Gay.

Seriously, this visual novel is fantastic. It has a killer cast of VA's, the nice mostly soft classical music soundtrack fits the tone perfectly and is honestly just good as study music or that sort of thing, the happy moments and dialogue are sweet enough to make you melt, and the story has just enough angst and conflict to keep it grounded and interesting. I only really have 2 minor critiques about Perfect Gold and the first is that it become a bit overly noticeable that the same backgrounds are being used. This certainly isn't most egregious fault since it all mostly DOES happen at the sunflower festival, but it might have been nice to see one or two more areas of Castlecoast or maybe Doveport if only so we could get more beautiful world building. 

The second is that I kind of wish there was a chapter select because it is a bit easy to accidentally hit the skip button, and therefore accidentally skip the beautiful voice acting and story beats. This can be subverted if you save often, but then it wouldn't be a proper accident then either. It would also just make it easier to go back and enjoy certain favorite moments with the dialogue and all.

Finally, a last critique. I know what your thinking, if I praised this game so much how could I really have another complaint? Well it's simple, Perfect Gold is...perfectly short. Its a short and sweet yuri visual novel and that's what I like about it. The story leaves some questions open but mostly resolves its main narrative and therefore doesn't have anything else to tell. Now I wish I just had more stuff like it. I did get this as a gift from a friend, but that was only because she beat me to the punch. I would have happily payed the 7 dollar price tag and I think it was well worth it.

This story was absolutely beautiful. While the beginning had me confused, I found myself understanding the two's history quite well shortly after. This felt incredibly different to much of the other visual novels I have played before and I can tell the people who made this really cared for this project. The art is beautiful. The characters are adorable. The voice acting is AMAZING. The two character's dynamic, while a common trope for sure, I have never seen it played in such a way. 

Definitely one of the best I've read recently and recommend to anyone who loves a good visual novel.

I played the demo first and fell in love with the game. So when I got the email saying it was released I literally bought it right away. And I have absolutely no regrets!

And most importantly, the story was really sweet, heartwarming and I constantly found myself smiling from ear to ear (especially at the end). Both Marion and Audrey are very well-developed characters with their own flaws and problems, and I really love that. I was completely immersed in their universe and didn't want to stop playing.

This game is so sweet and cute that almost made me explode. 10/10

I really enjoyed playing this Visual Novel

I love the characters, the details behind everything, the animations, the music and especially the voice acting.

Deffinetly one of my favourite Visual Novels, hope to see more like that in the future

I backed this game on Kickstarter based solely on the art and premise. I wasn't sure what to expect, and I was blown away at the quality of this VN. It is definitely on the short side, but the developers packed so much world-building and character development into the short timeframe. The two leads, Audrey and Marion,  have great interactions with each other, and I really enjoyed seeing their story play out. The UI is smooth and looks great. The voice acting is well-done. The sprites and CGs look beautiful. The main criticism I have is I wish there was some more variety in the music--what was there was really good, but I would have liked one or two more songs.
I would love to see a spinoff or sequel set in the same universe.
In summary, this game is definitely worth playing if you enjoy yuri romance.

Perfect gold is a short and sweet experience that manages to peak extremely high with endearing characters, sharp dialogue and a plot that gets you invested in its first 3 chapters. It has a great structure that weaves past and present, which makes you want to know more about how it is that Audrey and Marion broke up and how they will rekindle their relationship. Regardless, it is in Perfect Gold's strenghts that its weak points are revealed, and they are almost as big. 

First of all, Perfect Gold has some great things about it:

[list]
    [*] The game looks very good! The backgrounds have a good amount of detail and they all pop in color, giving you a peek into this world of magic and making Castlecoast look as beautiful as characters say it is. This also includes the CGs and character sprites, with both having small animations that make the characters feel more alive.

    [*]The soundtrack, while having some songs that I'm not particularly eager to listen to again, is amazing. Tracks like the game's main theme immediately caught my attention whenever they played, while others like "Our Golden Days", Audrey's Theme and Seeking Magic enhance scenes so much that I found myself wanting to be able to listen to them outside of the game. 

    [*]Perfect Gold is very accessible. The game isn't particularly long, only taking around of 3-4 hours at max to finish. This means that the game bases its scenes around mostly just dialogue with little to none inner monologue. You get to just enjoy character dynamics and see their interactions. The log is easily accessible and includes voice line replayability, with a list of achievements that I enjoyed getting and reading the flavor text for. Perfect Gold is solid on a technical level.
[/list]

Having said that, there are some things that soured my experience a bit.

[list]
    [*]Perfect Gold's voice acting is definitely underwhelming. Every character is voice acted, and while some of the secondary characters like Professor Beaufort and Simone are solid at best. Others like Marion, Audrey and the extras feel like they are overacting most of the time. The audio mixing on certain lines can be wonky as well, but it's not a prevalent issue throughout the experience.

    [*] The UI of Perfect Gold can be somewhat frustrating. Scenes that take place in the past are announced to the player with a little intro that plays for every chapter and explains when the scene is taking place, however, every scene in the past also has a sort of shadow around the border of the screen throughout the chapter. This can make it so that the excellent backgrounds feel like they are hidden, and even making it so some important CGs and their objects of focus are phased out of focus. It feels claustrophobic and makes me wish that they had just kept it like the rest of the scenes that take place in the present.

    [*]The game features multiple choices throughout the story, but none of them really matter. There's only two endings, with them being essentially the same other than the true ending featuring a 3 chapter epilogue. Some will give you a CG, while the other simply skips a ton of dialogue at a time. The game not having wildly different endings isn't necessarily a bad thing, but when choices have little to no differences between them in dialogue makes me feel like there could be a little bit more lik extra dialogue or slightly different scenes.

[/list]

Perfect Gold's story, to me, is very frustrating. 

The game starts with an extremely interesting chapter that sets the basis of our character's relationship, with the next chapter showing us them having broken up and getting into trouble. This sets the format of the story, with one chapter taking place in the present and the next one happening in the past. The protagonist of every chapter also changes between chapters, and while I've read about some people finding the change in POV and time confusing, I did not find it to be that at all. The game does a very good job at communicating these factors.

Perfect Gold's first chapter does a great job at getting you invested. Perfect Gold's second chapter sets up the tension between Marion and Audrey, with them fighting and having a very hard time getting allong, but with small glimpses of how much they remember about each other and how they are still present in each other's mind. They fight, they struggle, but still want the best for each other and still like each other. This dynamic is amazing, and it made me wish to see more, but once the fourth chapter hits, the tone changes far too much. 

Suddenly the game is full of happy scenes and Marion and Audrey getting along like nothing has happened, leaving behind the need for them to rekindle, the main conflict of the game. We get some worldbuilding during these middle chapters, all while leaving behind the interesting concept of a couple having to learn to love each other again. These scenes are sweet, don't get me wrong, but makes it so that every scene that takes place in the present day becomes a shadow of what it could have been if this dynamic of them evolved more naturally throughout the 21 chapters of the game. Plus, I also wish that their feelings towards each other were less obvious, but as it stands they lack subtlety, which I feel would have made the experience a lot better.

Then, towards the end there's an argument that comes very suddenly after a myriad of interactions between Marion and Audrey who have been getting along perfectly fine. This argument makes them fight just like in the beginning, which leads to another tonal whiplash that is hard to understand. Showing us really well written tension between Marion and Audrey that dies instantly, only to resurge towards the end so that our protagonists are separated before their inevitable reconciliation makes me want to see more of that, it makes me want to see them earn those sweet and adorable moments rather than just doing it right off the bat. 

If anything, every scene in the past is perfectly fine. They do a great job of showing the blossoming romance and decay of Audrey and Marion's relationship.  The impossible romance between different social classes, a rule-breaker protagonist with a by the book, shelteredd girl, learning to dance together, showing Marion's traditions to Audrey, and more? It's amazing, and it captivated me far more than anything in the present. The game has strong lessons, and it's genuinely heartbreaking seeing how they slowly fall apart. It's just the payoff that isn't rewarding, since it feels like the pieces are all there, but are in disorder.

Besides that, the lenght of the game is a double-edged blade. Sure, the game being short means that scenes go straight to the point and makes it so that you spend time seeing the character's develop. The dialogue is great, and characters feel very genuine. However, it also means that characters like Simone and Professor Beaufort are featured and important enough to impact the story, but aren't given enough time to make their relationships with each character feel as meaningful as it could have been. Personally, I wish the game was slightly longer.

Perfect Gold is a visual novel that you should definitely check out if you love the Yuri genre. It's endearing, sweet and at its worse simply makes you feel somewhat dissapointed. The pieces are all there, but with this current product it feels like the story simply doesn't fully commit  to what it shows you in its trailer and at its very beginning. Missing each other, everyone's opinion on their relationship, learning to forgive, all of this feels like its done at the very end, making most of the game feel like it lacks a vision on what it wants to do.

Regardless, Perfect Gold was still a fun read. I feel like it had the potential to become one of my favorite stories in the genre. But as it currently stands, it's just a fun game to play in between some more involved narratives.

It's a quite typical short romance story of two very different girls meeting each other and working through their differences. What I liked is the characterization and fluid writing, bolstered by some nice illustrations and occasional animations, but everything else is serviceable at best.

There's barely any world building, the time jumps every chapter is disorienting for the better half of the narration, and the choices have no immediate feedback.

Voice-over is generally good, but has some moments that bring it down a bit to mixed results, but that's largely a preference choice, I think.

On the technical note, there's no keyboard or gamepad navigation, so it's a bit painful to play on Steam Deck (plus, the sound options do not work properly there, so voice over is always drawning in the BGM), but it runs very well otherwise.

The art is great.
The voice acting is here and there.
The story is simple and sweet but leaves kind of a bad note in my mouth.

The art style isn't anything super special but it's clean and the animation really adds a lot with how everything flows around so smoothly, it's a lot more lively.
And the main couple are way too cute together so, y'know, some great art of them in there.

I feel like the voice actors went a bit too intense with the delivery on everything which can make it a bit grating if you're sensitive to that kind of thing. They're not bad, but maybe a bit amateurish, idk.
There's just no subtlety to literally anything, every moment the two main girls are together they're pretty much fawning over each other and somehow both oblivious to it. Like the way the lines are read they're always flustered all the time. It's kind of a lot.
It's got a vibe in a way, but not for everyone.
But that intensity comes in handy for the scenes that deserve it. There's a handful of pretty moving scenes with a fair bit of emotion behind the VA.

Story wise, it's pretty cliche on the whole, but that's fine.
Being on the shorter side, it does feel like it has to rush into things a bit quickly. Personally it would've been nicer to see their relationship grow more before everything kicks off, but I get it.
As it is, the first half of the game has you flopping back and forth between the past and the present as you wonder how the present ended up how it did. But it just felt a little confusing when you have to keep remembering what happened in each thing. And the outcome of the drama you're wondering about is a pretty typical, unsurprising thing that doesn't feel really worth telling the story in this mixed up way.
The world is also just some typical fantasy magic academy type beat.
It's fine, nothing too interesting, if you like that kind of thing then it'll be good, otherwise it can feel a bit goofy being dropped into this world and there's all this drama with different mage factions and stuff and not a lot of smooth buildup on account of the shorter runtime.

Most of the emotional parts of the story work pretty well, but it felt like there was something missing that just kinda denies closure, at least for me.
Spoilers though:
The thing is just that Audrey does so much messed up stuff that hurts Mari a lot, and that much comes through really well with plenty of good emotion with Mari expressing how much she was hurt by everything.
But, the problem is that Audrey never owns up to any of it.
The most she ever says is literally just "I'm so sorry. I shouldn't have left you.. Do you forgive me?"
She gets 10x more emotional talking to her teacher about how she doesn't want to be an alchemist, which fair enough that was a good scene, but where's that emotion with Mari?
She goes on about how she's realized she should do what she wants instead of just following her family's expectations and that she wants to be with Mari again despite her family disapproving, but that's it.
Mari basically had a breakdown about being left in the dark for so long, and told Audrey straight to her face how she made her feel like she wasn't good enough for her, like what they had meant nothing, and then Audrey STILL just walks away breaking things off and letting Mari keep thinking that way.
Audrey is like the reason Mari has so many insecurities.
And She just never makes up for that at all.
There really should've been a scene where she matches that level of emotion, like she should pretty much break down crying at the guilt for making Mari feel like that for so long, and she should've been trying her best to make sure Mari understands that wasn't true at all.
But instead it feels like she either doesn't care or still doesn't understand how messed up she was being after all that, which just feels wrong since they get back together and everything is all lovey dovey again.
Mari just kinda easily accepts her back without much fuss like the pathetic pushover she is because she was still pining over Audrey even after she left her again.
Like I can kinda buy that honestly with Mari feeling so desperate, but I know that's not the vibe the story was going for. That's a much more complicated dynamic that would need a longer story and then deeper rooted relationship issues coming up later etc etc.

Anyway Idk, lotta rambling. Mostly just feedback to the writer I guess If they see this.
It was pretty close to being exactly what it wanted to be, but just felt like a scene was missing or something.
On the whole though the experience was pretty enjoyable, even if for the art alone.

Sidenote I guess, the writing has wayyy too many name drops. Specifically with like the pet names.
If I had a nickel for every time Mari says "Princess.."
I swear she was ending every sentence with it at one point.
There are other instances throughout the story but you get the point.
"Dear child" "Sunshine" "etc"

[h1]YURI MANGA INCARNATION[/h1]

Perfect Gold feels like a playable version of the most basic yuri romance manga that you can find - and I'm saying it in a positive way. Sometimes, you need a good dose of something positive and fluffy in your life. Perfect Gold does exactly that. It's a short game (max 2h) about love. Nothing less, nothing more.

[b]Story[/b]
As I said in the beginning, the story is really simple. It's basically the most basic version of the "enemies to lovers" trope, where everything is resolved rather quickly and with a happy end. Of course, another popular cliche from mangas is also here - conflict caused by lack of proper conversation about problems. As an avid yuri reader, I enjoyed it. It's just the right amount of silliness and adorable moments mixed together. 

[b]Gameplay[/b] 
Gameplay-wise, there's not much to talk about. It's a standard kinetic novel with all the important stuff that needs to be there. There are some choices, but they don't matter at all (just minor flavor texts and achievements).

[b]Audio and Visuals[/b]
The whole game is insanely beautiful. High quality backgrounds, character's designs, CG's, animations (!), visual effects. Definitely one of the best Visual Novels in that department.
Yet another important thing - every character is fully voice acted (and the quality of VA is really good). That's not a common thing in VN genre, so this will be always a big plus for me.
Rest of the audio is solid. I would love to have more variety in soundtracks, but that's just a minor nitpick.

Overall, [b]I can definitely recommend Perfect Gold[/b] for every yuri lover out there. A little bit short, but still a delightful experience.

I backed this on Kickstarter and the creator offered a partial refund to backers because apparently this was "overpriced" compared to other VNs of similar content. If you backed this on Kickstarter, I implore you to decline the refund; you chose to back this, you chose to help bring this to life and help make this a reality. Crowdfunding isn't meant for us to get things at or less than cost, it's meant for us to back projects we want to see brought to life. In some cases, that means we "pay" more than we would otherwise pay.

This may not be the longest light novel, or one with branching story lines, unique mechanics or any other feature I didn't mention. But what it does, it does so with polish, grace and love.

I backed Perfect Gold's Kickstarter because I was a fan of Yang Yang's previous work and at first I was expecting something of similar length and depth. But reading the posts after the Kickstarter I learned that wasn't what they had in mind. So when they released the game within the year of making it, I finally got around to going through the game. And... I wouldn't want them to change a thing.

The writing is astounding, as I would expect from a Yang Yang work. It's a VN that tells the story of two mages who attend a school, become friends, and fall in love. The story is a bit more complicated than that, but I don't wish to spoil. The two have their inner turmoil to deal with as young people do and the story explores them wrestling and succumbing to it. It's absolutely sweet and adorable seeing Marion and Audrey spend a day together all while passively flirting and sharing their feelings with each other.

The character art and backgrounds moving and vibrant. While there is only art of 5 total characters in the story, each one is given plenty of expressions to show their range of emotions throughout the novel. With active movement to show these character's aren't just static 2D portraits. Would have liked to see some of Marion and Audrey's portraits be shown in the active position, but I do feel that's a nitpick.

The game you'll probably finish in 2 casual sittings or 1 long play to the finish. If you're looking for two girls from different backgrounds to earnestly explore their feelings for one another, this is the Visual Novel for you!

[H1] Perfect Gold: The Alchemy of Happiness[/h1][hr][/hr]
Two young girls from wildly different backgrounds meet and fall in love at the magical academy LeFay, a school for those seeking and pursuing the art of alchemy, healing, and controlling the elements. Audrey Clarey is a heiress who has always lived in the city of Castlecoast, her family famous for their transmutations into gold; while Marion LaRue is a rebellious girl from Doveport, a small town near the sea, seeking to hone her fire elemental abilities and earn her fame. It’s almost love at first sight, but their spark sputters out after their first kiss due to the completely separate worlds they live in. Can they get a second chance? Do they even want one?

In particular, I really liked the art style; it’s super cute, and the animator did a fantastic job with facial expressions and casual movements. While I do think this VN is honestly kinda cheesy and can be a bit over dramatic, it’s absolutely worth picking up and playing if Perfect Gold even remotely interests you.

[i]Set in a magical city, two schoolgirls with bad blood find themselves stuck in detention while an annual festival goes on without them. Determined not to miss it, they decide to put aside their differences for the day to sneak out and attend it together. As they walk around the festival, the girls struggle to embrace their true selves as well as the feelings they never quite lost.[/i]

Rating: General
Length: Short (~2-3 hours)
Warnings: None, really!

Branching visual novel in ADV format. There's only one ending, but making the right choices will also get you a cute epilogue.

Pros:
✨ Beautiful, artwork. The character portraits and CGs are all animated in, assumedly Live 2D, making everything feel much more unique and alive.
✨ A very sweet and short WLW romance without any focus on the hardships that come with being queer. One of the supporting characters uses they/them pronouns.
✨ Excellent world building. In such a short time, the story gives you this new magical world that feels lived in and vibrant. You don't really question how it works and may find yourself wanting to know more.
✨ You have the option to jump directly to new unread text or the next choice without sitting and waiting for the text to finish skipping.
✨ Full voice acting.

Cons:
✨ A pretty basic storyline wrapped in a beautiful bow. Presentation wise, it's well worth the price of admission, but you won't be getting a story that hasn't been told before. 
✨ The characters aren't super memorable.

Overall rating: 7/10
Recommendation: Buy it. (It's worth the price just for the beautiful artwork alone.)

    [*] Exceptional quality voice acting
    [*] Chilling atmosphere 
    [*] Centres around mature themes such as religious horror
    [*] Simple & modern interface
    [*] Succinct story (approx. 5 hours long) that does not overstay its welcome

[/list]
[b] [u]Cons[/u] [/b]
[list]
    [*] No branching paths and only one real ending (there is also a bonus ending)
[/list]

[quote] If you would like to see more reviews written by me, please follow my  curator page and help support my work [url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/34936724/] Cookie Reviews[/url]. [/quote]

[h1]Story & Gameplay[/h1]
The teenage protagonist, Holly Beltran, finds herself sent to an “religious recollection” at an isolated religious convent. Here, she meets a seemingly strict nun, Sister Adira and another troubled teen, Gabriella. Holly’s past trauma slowly begins to reveal itself as she is forced to face the truth, as well as the truth behind the holy dwelling she is residing in. Holly battles her personal relationship with not only herself, but her family, faith, and the supernatural.

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2971187341

The game is surprisingly short for a VN; however, I did not find this to be an issue. It is well-paced and still facilitates character development, just without any large, noticeable filler content. The characters are fantastic, and whilst the themes are mature, I felt a connection to the protagonist. There are very minor “horror” scenes, but the game’s biggest strength is how atmospheric it is, making these scenes much more impactful. It is important to note that the story is completely about religion, religious abuse, and extremism. This is all conveyed through young girls’ perspectives and already adds a large element of horror to the game on its own, without the supernatural elements it also encompasses.

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2971188195

[b][u]Interface & Game Design[/u][/b]
Saint Maker has all the features a VN desperately needs, and makes playing, or replaying, a complete breeze. Whilst there are no branching paths, there is still an option to swiftly fast-forward the text sections with the Tab key. This will skip to the next choice section. The dialogue choices are mostly trivial and will only result in a handful of differing sentences, but I still felt like I had a small method of crafting Holly’s personality. Many choices are also linked to achievements and can be easily replayed to collect them. The UI can be hidden so the background art can be enjoyed on its own, and there are numerous save slots. There is one bonus ending that is the only achievement tied to multiple different choices, otherwise the game is very linear. Whilst I did not mind this design choice, I would have liked to see some more ending routes and/or automatic fail scenes to increase replayability value.


[h1]Visuals & Sound[/h1]
Set almost completely within Saint Idelora’s convent, there are still numerous gorgeous backgrounds and animated illustrations to enjoy. Each are very eerie and detailed, filled with religious iconography and psychological scares.

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2971187593

Each character is fully-voiced, and the English voice-acting is phenomenal. The amount of emotion in every sentence was palpable, particularly for Holly. I believe that these voice actors are what made the game so memorable for me, and completely caught my attention right from the very beginning.

[h1]Technical & Stats [/h1]
[quote][b]This game was played using mouse only, and with the following PC specs.:[/b]
AMD Ryzen R5 7600x 5.4 GHz
32GB DDR5 5200 CL36 RAM
Radeon RX 6800 XT
2560x1440 resolution
NVMe 3.0 SSD
Windows 10[/quote]

[b]Bugs & Issues:[/b] None encountered.

[b]Playtime:[/b] Approximately 5 hours for one full playthrough and subsequent plays for 100% achievements

[b] Controller Recommended? [/b] No 

[b]Replayability value:[/b] None, there are no branching paths.


[h1]Conclusion[/h1]
Saint Maker is a brilliant VN with an exceptionally eerie story about religion and religious abuse of teenage girls. It is a grim narrative that manages to delve deeply into the characters histories. Its short, linear length seems unusual for these developers; however, it works well here. Overall, it is a fantastic, short but gripping VN that I recommend to all fans of visual novels and horror.

[quote] Follow our curator page, [url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/32732116-IndieGems/] [b]IndieGems[/b][/url] if you enjoyed this review and wish to see more like it[/quote]

[h1] Where are all the mens? [/h1] 
Was just a joke but some male presence wouldn't hurt :P 
This is another amazing short masterpiece by the devs of "The Letter" I call it short because everything was done within 6 hours. But I also say it's amazing because of the phenomenal voice acting and interesting story. 

[b] Story: Better than the Nun :D [/b]
I am a fan of horror and I can say this is a story that talks about religious repression plain and simple. It's an interesting theme and the execution using two girls, one not so religious and one is raised in a very strict religious family. Both are sent for rehabilitation by their families after certain mysterious incidents that happened to them. Since the genre is horror I would say that compared to "The Letter" the horror element is not that horrifying lol. Also there is one secret ending and one normal ending. There are no bad endings which is a bit disappointing. I like the premise of the whole story, it focuses also on the secrets of the monastery that they go to. The choices in the game have two different directions which it can go to. Which is pretty obvious. When the story progresses you do learn a bit of history about the saints and such, idk if it's historically accurate. So get ready for some intense story about a long forgotten monastery and its hidden secrets. You become the hidden guiding voice to shape their actions to the good or the better (I meant literally) I liked that concept and it was very original unique idea. 

[b] Characters: Making saints since the dawn of time [/b] 
I felt the characters are very well done they have a lot of development. The main character, a very disciplined girl with a secret fascination about witches, not in the evil kind but the teen witch kind. It was her escapade. There is also her rehab-mate: Gabby, who definitely don't believe this religious crap but is here to please her mom. Very polar opposite personalities and you see them bond throughout the story while navigating their pasts. There is also their mentor in the monastery, an older nun who is super strict and believes in biblical mysteries. There is also a handful of secret characters related to the history of the place which I won't talk about. I felt that the character development is amazing in this one, hands on one of the best. We all know that religion can be super bad if you grasp it in the wrong terms. Obsession about it can be fatal. 

[b] Gameplay: welll polished [/b] 
Has all the related features. The menu has an extras section as well. 
There is fast forward. I do wish that the main character had a own personal ready to open diary which we have access to. In the story-line she does possess one but I felt like some interactiveness would have been great. 

[b] Art and music: Orchestral! [/b] 
The music and voice acting is top notch. It is very atmoshpereic. The art is super well done. 
I did feel the sprites would have been made bigger, for 1920x1080 resolution it was a bit small for me. 
To the VA's amazingly done and this is one instance where I feel like AI can't really beat the human voices. The emotions in every word was portrayed by the talented crew here. 

[b] Achievements: Not hard [/b] 
Achievements are not that hard and easy to get.
You navigate each option you choose on the story and you can unlock these. 
I did get the secret ending in the first go, so idk if the game difficulty is tough or not. 

[b] Overall [/b] 
The game really tackled a theme that most never even dream to touch and made it into a masterpiece. 
It is a bit short though, I would have loved to play more. When I played it I was super addicted to the game not willing to put it down for even a day.

It is with heavy heart that I don't recommend this game. 

Look, at first, this might be the perfect game for me. I really respect The Letter and enjoy it a lot. I grew up Catholic, and I am well aware of how subtletly abusive that can get. I must be the archetypal target audience for this. 

 I genuinely cried in one of the final scenes, as the game suddenly drawed all of its plot beats into one singular, genuinely brillaint moment that delivered and delivered like almost no work has done before. 

But it's simply not worth it. 

You will spend most of the game in the same 3 rooms with a script that goes to great to "constant anime grunting". Most of the time is spend on building mysteries that are tangentially related to the ending, but never get properly developed. One of the main characters is just *kind of there* and never contributes anything. Almost none of your choices do anything for the story, and when they do, it's just one little optional scene; it's as if they were there just to complete achievments. A lot of the story's thematic weight only really makes sense in the last 30 minutes when certain crucial pieces of information are dropped. 

Throughout the game, there are a couple of scenes that stand out for their portrayal of religious abuse, and they are good moments, but you won't spend time doing that. 

It's frustrating because, with a better thought out script and plot, this could have been a masterpiece of a story. But it isn't, and its good points are, very regrettably, not enough to compensate for the bad ones. 

Play it if you're really interested. But it's not The Letter by any shot.

Those looking for a similar experience to The Letter, stop immediately! This game will disappoint you massively. The amount of characters in the story is minuscule. The length of the story is short. There is not enough going on for you to get emotionally invested in the characters, nor to care about what happens to them, which significantly lowers the scare factor in horror scenes. Speaking of horror scenes, it is hard to consider them a nightmare, and more adequate to classify them as a bad dream. The story is primarily about mental trauma, PTSD and hallucinations. The branching choices you make in the story seem more like an illusion of choice rather than something that actually could impact the direction of the narrative.
All in all, this doesn't even come close to The Letter, and the price for this game is inadequate.
However, if you are terminally bored and need something to distract yourself with for a few hours, it's an okay experience - something that will keep you occupied, but not something that will leave a lasting memory.

I am leaving a thumbs up despite my negative review, because I didn't hate the game, and because I want the publisher to get their act together and finally work on something that will not only contend in excellence with The Letter, but would greatly surpass it.

If you came to this page because you enjoyed "The Letter", then don't buy this game!

I was compelled to buy it because it was from the same creators from The Letter, a truly amazing horror VN. "Saintmaker", however, I can say it is one of the most boring games I've ever played. All the good things The Letter had are NOT present in this game:
Choices matter? Not much.
Interesting dialogues? No.
Nice characters? Not one.
QRTs? Nah.
Characters with the lives on the line? Not really.
Tense and scary story? Nope.

The main character is just...flat. I've got no sympathy/empathy for her whatsoever. Her decisions are so stupid that I couldn't care if something bad would happen to her. The other girl is just an annoying brat that complains about everything and tries to be the rebel all the time. And the nun... didn't feel anything for her unless despise.

The religious talk in the game is boring and just made me skip quickly those parts of the dialogue. The plot twist (revealing what really happened) is alright, but since choices basically don't matter at all, it just felt completely plain... Oh yeah, there is a "secret ending", but it doesn't change anything in the story, just add an extra scene.

If you feel like checking it out, then at least buy it on promotion. For me, Saintmaker is a no.

Fun, mildly spooky VN about the perpetuation of generational trauma in several young women from a strict religious sect. It will likely resonate with anyone who went through even much milder indoctrination, and has the added bonus of some of that gnarly old-time religious 'why are they praising this highly messed-up thing?' If you've ever been taught to venerate women who starved, scourged, or mutilated themselves out of love for God, you know exactly what I'm talking about.  

I didn't personally care for the voice acting, but fortunately that's easy enough to mute. There are no QTEs to worry about, and achievement hunters can note that replays are significantly faster due to a blessedly speedy auto-skip (the 'Not in the Slightest' achievement was the only one kind of tricky to get).  

Overall, a slightly different type of chilling tale perfect for a cozy evening's playthrough!

I was very excited for this game and I got it right after it was released and it was kinda worth it. The story of this game is amazing and scary and on top of that unpredictable. It also has great graphics, art style, amazing soundtrack, fun characters and details. The structure of the game, if comparing with " The Letter " , is very simple and doesn't really have a branch which can be a good for completing achievements, which I've finished fully. But this did lead to the fact that game is only 4 hours long and I don't think is worth the full price, so my advice would be to buy it on sale. Also there is no difficulty at all, and as I previously said the story is just in a straight line which can make it a little boring in case that you enjoy games with multiple endings and choices that affect each other. The story itself is for sure better than in " The Letter " since there is more focus on the story than on characters' lives and POVs and there is no repeating except some moments that are supposed to be that way. Overall a decent game worth playing. My rating would be 8/10.

So this one was kind of 'meh' to me. It starts out as a fairly standard 'religious horror' story and to its credit it does it pretty well but it also hits a lot of cliches that are only disrupted by one character's final act repentance. In terms of game play, even for a visual novel, this game feels depressingly linear. You're given lots of choices to make but it seems like almost none of them matter in any significant way other than a different sound byte playing and sometimes not even that much differentiation. The game frequently gives you two choices and then ignores what you chose with a blunt 'No. I can't say that.' Once or twice might be forgivable but this happens over and over again. And frequently you don't even have a listed pair of choices at all, your options are 'Say Y and Do X' or "Say Y slightly differently and then do X.' They may be trying to play with the linearity of an oppressively religious upbringing but in gaming terms it just brings home home few choices you can really make during the game. A straight visual novel without choices might have been preferable, at least I wouldn't have been constantly looking for a decision that actually mattered. The story doesn't really explain at the end what's 'really' going on but it is a somewhat emotional ending.

YangYang releases another banger. Solid all around. Great story, great music, great art and all the voice actors did amazing work. 

A bit short and lacks branching paths but the story kept me engaged throughout and had also had genuinely creepy moments. Kept me on edge and trying to guess what would happen next.

Another excellent horror vn from the developers, a great successor for "The Letter", and I have absolutely enjoyed it.

This vn tackles a lot of sensitive topics like suicide, death, abuse, blind fanaticism and some. 
Most of the theme present are the guilt and denial of accepting their own flaws to the point of trying to achieve perfection while not seeing the reality that is happening around you and the person close to you. Its either you make up a story about it for yourself and play pretend or you move forward to be better and accept the truth.

The voice acting is PHENOMENAL, every sorrow, pain and every bit of feeling for each character was really expressed. 
The story is quite short compared to "The Letter" but it was good, there are a lot of times where it was creepy, touching and joyful. I really got the most out of it. and its just enough to end it in its length
Looking forward to more stories from this developers.

This was my first "visual novel" game - although it's not much of a game: there aren't any puzzles, challenging choices, or even QTE's... but there is a level of spook, writing, and emotional acting that I'd expect from a Mike Flanagan level tv show. 

Amazing art, PHENOMENAL voice acting, and a legitimately good story. 

If you like Miky-Flan levels of spoop, visual novels, or have suffered trauma from being forced into a religion you weren't sure about as a child... I highly recommend "Saint Maker". 3 Hours went by in a flash, and I don't consider it time wasted. 

Well done!

I understand people are disappointed that it's not like The Letter, which has more to it, like more routes and some quick-time events. This one is purely a visual novel with some choices but the story was SO good. What an emotional roller coaster. Don't go in expecting it to be the horror game that The Letter was, it's not that scary and not that much romance. More like psychological horror and tearjerker. There isn't really much replay value, if you're not confident and really don't want to replay, I would suggest using a guide for the choices to get the bonus secret ending on your first try. Love that it's fully voice acted and most of the art was good. I didn't really like some of the 3D models in some of the cutscenes since they didn't look consistent with the usual look of the characters, but it was a good attempt to keep the characters animated throughout. I'm really impressed by the quality of the games the devs make, considering they are a small team in the Philippines. I remember them mentioning they would add more to their games if they had the proper funding.

Even tho the letter had a story with many branching paths to explore the world to the fullest, I think Saintmaker is still their best work in therm of horror and that the more linear approach  of the game does not take away anything from it.

this game.. was.. a big let down after playing the letter. Unfortunately I felt 'meh' at the story and a lot of my choices didn't matter. It was also way shorter than the letter and a lot more boring. =/

First and foremost, this isn't really a horror game. I was expecting it to be pretty intense based on the trailer. But besides a few startling parts and morbid imagery, this game is more about drama than scares.

The first two-thirds of the game mostly plod along, everything happening pretty much as one would expect in the scenario presented. The last third is where things get interesting, though. This game succeeds where The Letter failed, in terms of providing intriguing plot twists and actually uncovering a mystery, fully understanding the paranormal forces at work. Overall, I think what the story provides in the last third makes the game worth playing, as things that happened in the earlier parts do significantly come in to play later on.

The voice acting is impressive, particularly for Adira's character. For a visual novel, it's excellent. This story wouldn't be able to hold up without the actors sounding genuinely terrified, furious, or distraught. Holly, Liana, and Gabby all have their voices pitched distractingly high or low, but the acting itself does hold up. The music and artwork are fine, though nothing that really stands out. Sometimes it's hard to tell what's happening without narration or animation - like the screen will shake and a character cries out, but it's unclear whether they're getting grabbed, pushed, hit, etc. It takes the wind out of tense scenes when they're not depicted clearly.

Unfortunately, while there are many dialogue options in game, they have almost zero impact and the story is completely linear. To some extent it works in this case, as being deprived of choice leans heavily into the game's themes. But if you're looking for a VN with branching paths, this one won't have that. 

It's a flawed visual novel, but the story's eventual strength outweighs the problems for me. I played this fresh off of finishing The Letter, and this game is much more satisfying in that it ties everything up and provides a solid final act and ending. If you can get through the slower, monotonous parts, you'll be in for a treat.

Saint Maker isn't the horror game I really expected it to be, it was more of a reflective piece. Emotional too, at times. Sure, some CGs made my skin crawl (stone statues impossibly twisting their necks, the little girl) and some scenes made me feel uncomfortable (Liana, nuff said); but after playing YangYang's last game, 'The Letter' (high recommend), this one was a big bit of a let down horror-wise. Saint Maker is still an alright read, but don't go into it for a horror game experience.

Yangyang Mobile did it again. They got me too emotionally invested and I ended up paying the price

To anyone finding this game after their previous horror visual novel, The Letter, it should be noted that the two games aren't really comparable. Saintmaker is a lot shorter, there are fewer overall characters, and the choices don't matter too terribly much. They give achievements, depending on if you take a certain option over another, but that is it (for the most part).

But man, the story fucking rocks. It can have its lulls and low points (like all things can), but when it peaks, it really peaks. The end of the game blew my brain apart and I was a crying mess after. I really can't divulge much info about it because the story is kind of the whole point. It is religious horror, so if that scares you too much, maybe stay away.

The CGs got me a few times as well. The game isn't TERRIBLY scary most of the time, but it had some moments that either made me jump or just generally rattled me. Most of the time, it was a truly odd visual encounter accompanied by some VERY fitting and creepy noise (whether it be original or royalty-free)

Audiowork is also really nice. I really enjoyed the voice work, especially of the main girls (holy fuck I think they did great). I felt boiling hot rage at some of these characters, with the way they talk and handle themselves. I felt sadness when they sobbed and wailed, distraught at what happened to them. Alongside the voices, the music was a great tool in evoking emotions. The music was a nice blend of softer, serene music for those calmer moments and jarring, intense tones that play when you have to face the horrors. 

Go try it, I loved it (just try not to cry at the end {I may just be a weakling so take this statement with a grain of salt})

I may be a bit biased since I voiced Cornelia, but I can easily say that this was the emotional experience I wanted it to be. When we were recording my lines, I didn't get the full story, but the parts that I did encounter touched me deeply and I couldn't wait until I got to learn more. The entire voice cast absolutely killed it! As another reviewer said, it's not so much scary as it is [i]sad[/i]. If you like stories that hurt your soul, but also offers some hope and light in spite of that, I definitely recommend this.

While this game seemed to be advertised as a horror game, it only has few horror moments in the beginning, which quickly fall to the wayside.
That being said, the overall story of this game was great. It has a lot of incredibly emotional moments and I actually teared up a few times.
Of course, it helped that the voice acting was simply phenomenal. Everyone is giving it their all and really make you feel for the characters.
The game doesn't really feature any branching paths or multiple endings, but the story doesn't suffer from that.
If you were expecting a horror game like "The Letter", you might be disappointed, but I would definitely recommend you give this one a try if you liked any of Yangyang Mobile's previous games or just enjoy visual novels in general.

Don't get this game thinking it will be just like The Letter, choices are taken away and there are no branching stories for the characters.

Its just a straight line  visual novel with no scares. besides the creepy story and a few "ghost events" here and there it was not a very scary game and I found myself skipping through stuff. I feel like they focused way to much on the cult and their  beliefs and it was just way to boring,  

Devs. next game you make go back to the formula used for The Letter, make the ghost scary like before, let the choices we make matter and let their be real danger to the characters. This game was super boring, if I could refund I would.

Pretty nice VN overall! The story does a great job at combining paranormal elements with psychological horror and despite being somewhat predictable at some aspects, it's still interesting! I really liked the setting and art (especially during the scary scenes). The ending rather disappointed me (I was kinda hoping for a longer conclusion) but the rest of the experience was enjoyable due to the amazing intense atmosphere.
Zero choices, only one branch, only around 20 (slightly alternated) backgrounds. Sorry this was a major step down from ´The Letter´ imo.

Not at all like The Letter but something else entirely; which isn't a bad thing. I feel like this story had more heart to it that will touch those who can relate in their own ways. Saint Maker is a different type of horror, which I appreciated a lot. The devs wanted to tell a story, and they did that in a beautiful way.

I've always been a big fan of The Letter, the story and the differences between the choices and their results were really satisfying to discover for me, and it's been a game I've always recommended to others and sometimes even pressured to play with me on stream, it's genuinely good. So I was especially excited about and had high expectations from Saintmaker, and I was not disappointed. The theme of the story, the complicated emotions and the relations between the characters were very intriguing. The voice actors also did absolutely amazing, it's the one thing I truly appreciated in The Letter as well - fully voiced and expressive, yet not over the top with it, enough to make it feel real and immerse the playet into the story. It's yet another game I'll keep obsessively recommending to people :D

An interesting story. Touches on sensitive topics such as religion, abuse, and blind fanaticism to name a few but overall it is an entertaining experience. The scares are few unlike the previous horror title The Letter and from observation that this game focused more on the nuances of drama rather than relying on the horror. Also, I noticed that the game is shorter which is understandable for the narrative perspective and I think that it could use a little more hours to flesh out certain aspects of the story as well as the "whys" of some characters but that's, to me, a minor thing. Of course there are scares but that is a given and to me they aren't cheap scares but instead tries its best to enhance the narrative on the more otherworldly encounters and occurrences in the story. I would recommend this game, for all its perfections and flaws, most definitely.

I'm not sure why people are leaving negative reviews because they expected another The Letter. It was clear from the description that this game was going to be much more reserved and linear. That being said, I thought the game was just okay. 

It took me roughly 6 hours to beat the game and I feel as though it could have been shorten down by 2 hours. The game relies heavily on "flashbacks" and honestly put me to sleep more than anything. When I got past those scenes, I found the mystery around the convent incredibly intriguing as well as the struggles the protagonist was going through. By the end of it, I was left with a ton of questions and zero answers. I wish more time was given towards fleshing out the mystery of the convent but I'm okay with what I got.

Also works perfectly on my steam deck idk why it says unsupported

Where do i even begin... Everything about this is just too good to put into words. First off, the story is short compared to The Letter (one of YangYang's VN). Short but totally not simple or cliche in that matter. The horror element in this wasn't as strong as The Letter and I don't think that was the selling point here anyway. It's scary but not to the point where you'll jump out of your chair and if I were to say it's more eerie than scary. What got me really hooked was the voice acting like don't get me started on how passionate the voice actors are. I lost count on how many times I got goosebumps from it. 


If I had to nitpick about something, It would be how the ending wasn't as satisfying as I would like it to be. There is no epilogue which made me yearning for it more than anything. I'm still curious to what happens to the protagonist and other characters after the main event of the story. Also, I was kinda disappointed on the lack amount of ending that this game has which makes the choice making quite bland and basically not important to the story as almost every choices you make doesn't have consequences.


That's just my 2 cents about this game and don't get me wrong, it has it's cons but I absolutely enjoy my time playing this masterpiece. YangYang if you're reading this, please don't stop making horror visual novels. I feel like you guys are not getting the recognition that you guys absolutely deserve for making such amazing games. Much love ❤️

Beautiful art style and amazing voice acting. The story was also good, however the choices don't change anything major, with maybe a couple of line changes later on, so if this game is on sale (or full price if the premise really intrigues you) then it is worth buying.

i love this game sooo much!  the topic, the character portrayal etc. it reminded me that i was a girl who lying on my bed alone and praying for a miracle. back then my family was full of violence, no body was gonna save me...   god know how badly i need a magic spell to take me to another world. and i acted exactly the way holy showed. smile, be a good girl, but inside im already broken. 

actually i like this game more than The Letter, but alas it seems like not very much player this game got. anyway, this is my favorite, i will remember it. by the way im a Chinese and not good at English EVEN AFTER PLAY ALL MY GAMES IN ENGLISH HAHA. maybe after finish The Letter, my english will be better.
as an older sibling with a roman catholic upbringing........... damn.............

Having beaten the game and getting the main ending, I feel fairly confident in being able to give a review on this. So would I recommend this game to others? 

Surprisingly, despite how much I loved the Letter... No. No I wouldn't. After doing a little research it seems that this game has different writers from the Letter.. and it's shows. And not in a good way. 

Where the Letter excellened with multiple characters to interact with and worry (or not) over, in this one there are three. Two, technically, and they're not really that engaging. The Sister is a tragic antagonist that when you find out her background, you can feel sorry for, the main protagonist is..fine for a character, but the other girl .. really barely has any presence in the story. She's just kind of ..there... to further plot hooks and points and to help in the end. 

Granted it could have been the route I ended up getting, but to me, you could remove her and not lose a whole lot.

Aside from the lack of characters though, it's biggest flaw is the lack of narration, and for a visual novel that's almost criminal. Many times it represented "something" happening with shaking the screen and a noise, while I'm sitting there going, "...kay what happened; guess I'll have to guess based on the barely-there context"

There's a lot of back and forth flashbacking which just seems confusing at points, but the story does get stronger near the end when you find out what actually is going on. They don't overexplain things and take away the feel of it. But presenting this came as "scary" or "creepy" is a joke compared to the previous game. Looking at it on its own merits, it's a pretty Meh story, but not interesting enough to warrant replayability. But that's just my opinion.

Saint Maker is an alright horror visual novel that the story and characters were fine. If you're expecting complex branches like The Letter, you'll be surely disappointed. It's just an linear story that your choices barely matters unless you want the secret ending.

The voice acting is GOD TIER. I really loved the story and the characters especially Holly are very relatable in a lot of situations. Its not very scary but I still really enjoyed it and I'm looking forward to the next one! <3

A great horror story to play in one sitting. Compared to their previous game The Letter, it's definitely a much smaller story (and doesn't have major branches), but it has an even better story and still has stunning art. There was a glitch that caused one of the CGs near the end to display without one of the character's faces, which was.... interesting. Other than that, no noticeable typos or bugs. 

Worth noting that the game has a few jumpscares, but all of them are predictable. Not saying that's a bad thing, but for people like me who can't handle surprise jumpscares very well, you're given a pretty good chance to prepare mentally for them based on the context of the scene. The creepiest one is even completely avoidable.

[i]This place...there's a really special air to it.[/i]

It's been a while since I've played a horror game that really knocks it out of the park! I had fairly high expectations since I really enjoyed Yangyang Mobile's previous horror game, [i]The Letter[/i], but [i]Saint Maker[/i] improves on aspects of that game I loved and fixes most of the small issues I had to deliver a tight psychological horror character drama that just got under my skin.

Plenty of horror stories clumsily attempt to incorporate Catholic imagery because they (correctly) intuit that a lot of it is very visually creepy, but they usually fail to grasp why. This game, however, understands perfectly what to hone in on in stories of convents, sainthood and devotion, i.e. the relentless misogyny and the unwavering obsession with suffering as a means to better oneself. (#NotAllCatholics)

Everything (the excellent voice acting, the visuals, the sound effects) comes together to tell the story of the women and girls of the convent, and the game really benefits from its quick pacing and small scope, with a few characters set (one might say trapped) in one place for a few days. What makes a saint? How much suffering should we be ready to inflict on ourselves and others to reach sainthood? And what then?

This game is an easy buy for anyone interested in horror, especially religious horror. It was exactly what I was hoping for judging from the summary and steam page, and I can't recommend it enough.

I have played a good few visual novels and I have loved them all. I honestly think my favorite one was The Letter. Mainly cuz I love how each path was different and to see each side of things and how they react to the situation. There is a small bit of that in this. All in all the story is good. The little girl. Oh my god the first time I saw her I was not expecting it. It was a shock to say the least. But after that first shock I felt so bad for her. Every story of the characters in this was heartbreaking. I very much enjoyed every bit of the story in this. Not to many freaky moments but there are times where it is unnerving.

I was excited for this visual novel. "Returning to our roots," or whatever. My biggest grievance is the fact that the game is buggy. It says that the files get saved on the cloud, but they really don't, and in my case (as well as the case of others) I made it to chapter 35 and my save file became corrupt. I can no longer load the file or continue, because every time I try, the game crashes. Please test your game thoroughly before charging people money for it. Thank you.

It took me a while to figure out why Saint Maker didn't hit me as it should. The story isn't bad, I like the art and voice acting is also pretty good. So why don't I feel anything? It's only by the end that I noticed it. Maybe this is something that plagues every visual novel but I've never noticed it before like here. Basically the issue is that every single line is seperate. No one ever speaks out of turn. There's even a noticable gap between one person speaking and the next. It's all very unnatural. There are a lot of emotions flairing up within these characters. They shout, they cry, they wimper. Even those emotions are all in turn. Someone will cry and the other person waits... the other person speaks and there's no crying in the background. When two people argue and shout they never speak out of turn. You can have the greatest script for a movie but if all the actors just stand there motionless waiting for their lines to come up it would make for a terribly fake movie wouldn't it? I've read a lot of visual novels but I never noticed it like this. I also read/played The Letter from this developer. Maybe it's because there's so much crying in this game but everything just feels off to me.

A very well written, albeit short, horror visual novel. Very likable characters all around, and some very dark subject matter. Not sure if it's worth 14 dollars. I paid full price for it but I'm a fan of Yangyang Mobile. If you like The Letter I'd say it's worth it on sale.

This game is so boring and the voice actors are quite bad, especially Holy's voice, she sounds so fake. To me, it's 3/10. I bought it because The letter is so good but this game is totally a bummer. Luckily, it's cheap.

What an absolute experience. Stellar work, through and through. 
Although, I think the unlocked animated CGs will need to be patched. As they are currently, they will run through in entirety, meaning that you can present yourself events of the game that you have not experienced yet. 
I highly recommend to patch it where a scene is not unlocked until it has been experienced through the main game . Until then, play through the entire game before re-watching the animated CGs.

This was a fun horror story , I enjoyed every moment of it. I got it because I played The letter which I loved so I thought that their next game will be just as fun and it was. What the other reviews are saying that this is not like the Letter is true, this is a linear visual novel and it is shorter as well. That fact didn't take away from the fun I had. I think it should have been noted in the game description that this is linear , there are choices that help you unravel more of the story and secrets but they don't change how the ending goes.
They nailed the creepy atmosphere, the horror moments were solid and the sound design was 10/10. I was playing this with headphones on one night and all the knocks and other sounds in the game while the haunting was going on scared me enough to turn the lights on.
The voice acting is 10/10, I have nothing but praise for the acting. It was great!
The story is pretty much a ghost story with some interesting twists here and there. The reveal of the mystery was awesome so I don't want to go into details to spoil it. If you want to experience a haunting in an old covenant where some really demented nuns live in, I promise you'll enjoy this :D
The art is hit or miss, when I first started the game it felt like a downgrade in comparison to their other games. Then I went back and took another look at The letter and realized that it was the same there too. It wasn't so obvious in The letter because it was more cartoonish while in this one it's trying to be something else, it's like they wanted to make the art more serious to fit the atmosphere but also didn't want to let go of the anime look. The illustrations for the special moments look great , the ghost and monsters look great as well. But the character art (not the one that shows up next to the text box, those are solid) is most of the times kinda bad. There was a close up to the faces once where it genuinely looked like their eyes are stickers plastered on a 3D model , it was strange.
In conclusion if you enjoy horror stories and want a visual novel with voice acting in a pretty classical location then you will have a good time. If you are unsure because its a short game its worth giving it a try at least when its on sale.

A big letdown from the amazing game 'The Letter'. Everything is worse, the drawings, the voice over, there are no choices that matter and no branching, no exploration of the monastery. It is not scary as the former game, more like a thriller.  I had big hopes for the next game of this studio and had hoped it would expand upon everything that made The Letter great and even made it better but this is a major step down budget wise.
Story is great, not as complicated as the letter (too many branching paths) but at least this time the true ending is a good one. Great voice acting too (too much crying here)
Haven't finished it yet, but really enjoyable. VA is great, story doesn't drag out, art is amazing as always... so far can only recommend!
