Saint Maker is a wonderful horror Visual Novel that I highly recommend. A great cast of characters with amazing voice work and a well focused story makes this a great, short experience.

If you want many branching choices, a very long game, and a ton of endings like we had in The Letter, you won't get that here. But what you will get is just a much MUCH better story, one well written and heartfelt in it's exploration of religious trauma, authoritarianism, faith, cult dynamics, death, abuse, growing pains, and the struggle with accepting reality as it is. Highly recommend this game 100%, especially if you enjoyed religion based horror similar to Outlast 2!

Saint Maker is definitely more than just a horror visual novel. Behind the eerie atmosphere and religious-themed scares is a rich story with great characters. I went into it expecting a good scare and left crying at the end from the sheer emotion that went into it. The voice acting was impeccable and the visuals were beautiful! Sometimes terrifying as hell, but beautiful nonetheless.

Horror isn't normally my kind of thing, but I just couldn't put the game down -- I just had to keep going.

CW: child abuse, body horror, religious abuse/horror

Thanks for the devs for giving me a key, I have a lot of good things to say about this game and also some bad things about it but I'm still giving a thumbs up because it's still a good horror visual novel.

The story in this latest visual novel has taken me on a wild rollercoaster ride of me feeling sad, angry, happy and confused and I like it that it made me feel like that. Of course, it's different than the first game, The Letter but it's unique in its own ways and it's different.

I have to admit that when I first streamed the demo, I had high expectations on hoping Saint Maker is going be same as The Letter but that was my mistake, I felt disappointed and confused on what the game was supposed to be but that cleared out when I played the full game later.  

There was no QTE, no consequences of you picking the wrong choice and you will die and also the horror wasn't there like The Letter.

I felt crept out with most of the scary moments but there are some moments that scared me for real so good job on that but still, the whole game doesn't feel really scary like how I felt in The Letter. 

But in my opinion, there's no bad game, just a matter of preferences. I like the game itself but it's not amazing like The Letter but others might like this more than me.

It was a good thing as well that I was disappointed since it made me open my mind about the future changes and ideas you wish to explore in your future horror visual novels.

This religious, convent nunnery-based visual novel was absolutely amazing. An amazing work of fiction from YYM. **Its not like The Letter - neither the story nor in importance of choices** It's short and spectacular, and it works that way - the themes are circulated aound religious life and devotion however it's actually a lot more than that. Don't go into this thinking it's a choice-based game because you'll be disappointed and, in my opinion, miss the alternative direction the story is supposed to go in. I was, at first, until I kept going with the story and realised that this works SO much better. Seriously. It really hones in on the points the story hits the hardest. It kinda reminds me of the way The House In Fata Morgana works with the choices - very minimal, mostly there for show until the point when things start to fall 'back' into place.  
Oh and the voice acting... genuinely one of the most memorable, emotional and fitting VA work for every character that I've ever heard in any VN. Probably one of the best I've heard in video games period. 
Art is immaculate as usual (coming from The Letter), the music is perfectly tuned for each scene... I went into this thinking it can't be that good, being so different from The Letter which is one of my all time favourites but this really sizes up. In a way that is very refreshing - and resounding. Felt too real at times too. 
I just think that it's a bit short for the full price it's at, but I wouldn't have regretted getting this at either to be honest. 
8.5/10

It's an interesting enough visual novel. If there was an option to say "mid" I'd probably rate it there. There are a lot of "non-choices" that make sense thematically, but still felt frustrating - I play VN's for the varied routes, and this one just didn't deliver. The only reason I gave it a thumbs up is because I think it's interesting for what it was. Buy it on sale, if you need a middling horror experience.

Okay, bad point first - it's not The Letter and your choices don't really matter. It's shorter, there's a handful of characters, and much smaller scale. With that out of the way, it's beautifully voice acted, heartfelt and touching, and very sinister at times. It would be easy to make Adira an irredeemable monster, but there is enough balance and depth to everyone to make them feel lifelike. 

I would love another Letter, but until that day, this was a beautiful exploration of a story. Well acted, well told, and very touching. Thank you to the team that worked very hard on it.

It's a very linear game where choices don't matter. 

Still, I had a blast with it. The story is gripping and made me very emotional. I love the themes of perfection and guilt, how you can get broken by pressure and become a worse person, because you bought into the bullshit that you were being taught. One of my favorite visual novels of all times.

Saint Maker is a high quality VN with a solid story and some deep topics. Sadly, it doesn't have the depth that I hoped for after playing and loving their previous horror VN "The Letter". While this is partly due to this one being much shorter and having minimal branching, I feel like it is also just lacking a bit of coherence when it comes to the actual horror aspects. That being said, I still very much recommend this one to any VN lovers, since the story is good and the art and voice acting are simply superb.

Having played through The Letter several years ago, I was VERY excited to see this game released. The characters are all well thought out (even if Holly's whining annoys me sometimes), and I honestly had a lot of fun with my own speculations and "what if" scenarios. If you are looking for a chill, relatively short visual novel (one that will take under 10 hours), then grab this! You won't be disappointed. My playlist: 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NSWluAye_RI&list=PL5Jimra-A3jI5XpQE2kC4PvyBoIbQEnvt&index=1

It is a pretty fun visual novel. 
Art is as beautiful as always, some tense moments in here, fitting soundtrack.
Story is ok. It was going great, but then the super rushed ending happened. It felt like a lot of things were set up and then went nowhere. I didn't hated it, but didn't love it either.
But, hey, [spoiler]gays survived in a horror story! You go girls.[/spoiler]
Overall, it may not be the best horror vn I've ever read, but there's also not that many of them, so... Give it a try, I guess? 

And that's why, kids, we need a "maybe" button in reviews.

Saintmaker: A Divine Disappointment

Ah, *Saintmaker*, the visual novel game that tries so hard to be something... special... but ends up being something... else entirely. The story, or what passes for one, meanders like a lost soul in a purgatory of half-baked ideas.  You follow a protagonist who, much like the target audience, seems to be perpetually stuck... in life... and in a narrative that goes absolutely nowhere.

The voice acting, well... where do I start? It’s like they hired a bunch of V-tubers and told them to channel the most over-the-top, early-2000s anime dubs they could remember. It’s so unbearable that even die-hard anime fans—those who have braved the worst dubs—might find themselves hitting the mute button... and considering... a life of silence.

Then there’s the pacing... or lack thereof. The game has this peculiar obsession with... ellipses. Every other line ends with them... as if the characters are constantly trailing off into an existential crisis. You’ll find yourself clicking through three of them in a row... only to realize... that nothing of substance is actually being said. It’s like the game is trying to pad out its runtime with... dots.

And let’s not forget the "artwork." Or rather, let’s try to forget it. The visuals are as bland as a bowl of unseasoned porridge. There’s nothing intriguing to look at, nothing that catches the eye... just... more... of the same uninspired images. It’s like staring at a screensaver.

In conclusion, Saintmaker is a divine disappointment. A story that goes nowhere... voice acting that grates on the nerves... an overuse of... well, you get it... and artwork that’s as exciting as watching paint dry. If you’re looking for a visual novel game that will make you question your life choices... this might just be the one.

Not sure what compelled me to get this game considering that I'm not really the religious type but I gave it a shot anyway. Honestly, it wasn't terrible but it wasn't good either.

Pros: 
-The visuals were clean and the style was good.
-Voice acting was actually pretty good, maybe a little awkward at times but each character had a strong voice. The vocal ranges made this visual novel come to life.
-The ambient music was eerie and the sound effects added to that effect to make it chilling and suspenseful.
-The plot was interesting. I really thought it would revolve around the main character only but the further you read the more it unravels that every character had their own plot twists. I definitely didn't expect it to end the way that it did for both the main ending and the secret ending.

Cons:
-Main character was kind of annoying (lol) but I think it's because her voice was kind of fake-sounding (?). Not sure how to explain it. 
-The "horror" wasn't actually scary. The only "jump scares" were amplified by a sharp and loud sound that lasts for a second. 
-It didn't really seem like like a "choice matters" game only because you would choose an option and it wouldn't matter because the game automatically chooses for you anyway to keep it more linear. It doesn't change the story at all. This feature, in this game, is primarily to get certain achievements, it seems.

Overall I still enjoyed this. I can't remember if I paid full price for this but $14 isn't so bad for what is offered. I'd still recommend getting this while it's on sale instead.

Despite the occasional employment of morbid imagery and ghost story elements; I don't think this game should be framed as a horror visual novel. I feel that this game it's more structured around revealing the complexities and back stories of the game's characters. The occasional horror elements feel more like they're meant to lead-on the player to something more unfolding behind the game's story.......BUT, without spoiling the game too much: the truth it's all out there in the open from the beginning. It's a fun visual novel, for sure..... really lovely artwork and top notch voice acting.
I wish it was a bit more interactive and added more narrative branching options; that would have gave me the excuse to go back and play it again a few more times.
Highly recommended nonetheless!

It's a meh rather than an endorsement but due to the amount of crappy VN on Steam it would be an insult to this game if I didn't recommend it.

The thing is it is quite short (about 5-6 hours), has no replayability, at least half the choices have no consequences and the art is minimalistic: 5-10 backgrounds, a few sprites and a few characters that aren't that well drawn.

The story is ok but moves quite slowly and it is quite predictable.

So buy it if it is not too expensive and if you're not expecting The Letter 2.

The ideas and the flow were very well made, it also talks about topics that are considered taboo in our society.
In my opinion it was much shorter than the letter but I'm very pleased with the results <3

Made the mistake of starting my playthrough of Saint Maker at 11pm and determined to finish it in one sitting, and ended up experiencing a great visual novel that I don't think I can make myself play through again for a while. I want other people to be able to go in blind, so anything that isn't mentioned on the Steam page, I'm gonna mark with spoilers, and say what kind of spoiler it is.

It is specifically because its writing is good that it made me feel viscerally uncomfortable at some points (which, don't get me wrong, is what I came into this expecting). I don't have a good standing relationship with Christianity, so religious horror in that vein was always going to freak me out a bit. Didn't [i]scare[/i] me, but definitely made me squirm a bit. Then when it comes to Holly and (spoiler: character reveal) [spoiler]Liana[/spoiler], the writers do a good job of dropping hints and foreshadowing alongside the reveals that happen along the way as to what really happened. (Spoiler: talking about the lead-up to said reveal in more detail, but without saying what the reveal is) [spoiler] It's well done here how the game slices up the information reveals, starting with Holly having a sister, then her name is Liana, then SOMETHING happened to her, then you do learn what happened, but it still feels like you're missing something...[/spoiler] It's all just well-paced, and by the end it gives a much greater insight into why Holly acts the way she does, even in seemingly out-of-character moments.

Then there's Adira and Gabriella, whose existences I'm not marking with a spoiler because they're in the store page images and you meet at the start of the game anyway. Well-written characters, though from the playthrough I had, Gabriella ends up feeling a bit underutilised at times. Early game is good for making you question if either of the characters will be on your side, or if you'll be stuck as a protagonist with two antagonists, which adds to the tension. (Spoiler: talking about the characters in more detail, including some information about their character arcs, reveals, game choices/mechanics related to them, and in one case a character's similarity to another from a different game, which could count as major character spoilers) [spoiler] Gabriella in particular ends up feeling a hell of a lot like an [url=https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Main/Expy]expy[/url] of Chloe Price from the Life Is Strange franchise, which if you know the game you'll probably know exactly what I'm talking about. This isn't a bad thing, but the similarities are undeniable, meaning that reveals probably intended to have more impact can be dulled if you know the character. Either way, my playthrough was mainly one of trying to not cause trouble, so I didn't choose many early-game "side with Gabriella" choices. As I've said, I can't actually will myself to play through it again for a while, but maybe there are changes in how the relationship between her and Holly develops if you side with her more early on. As for Adira, the game does a good job with occasional perspective-shifts to her to show her inner thoughts and experiences. It's evident from the start that things don't quite seem right with her, that she's battling her own demons but tries to not show it outwardly to the others. It becomes evident the true nature of what she's like as the game goes on, with her obsessions with perfection and the statues demonstrating her own trauma before the full extent of it is revealed.[/spoiler]

I'm not going to go into any extensive detail about fully spoilerific stuff, again because I don't want to ruin the plot for anyone who sees this review and can't help themselves with checking the spoilers. But it is a very good visual novel - I'd say it's as good as The Letter, the previous horror visual novel by Yangyang, but the games take very different approaches to their game lengths and amount of changes your choices can make. This may make it seem unappealing to those (like me) who were blown away by The Letter's branching choices, but I assure you, this game is better for how it handles the choices it does give you, even if the story is more linear than that game overall. Too many choices with too many major changes to the plot would have ruined the pacing. (Spoiler: slightly more detail about the nature of the game's choices, including effect on the overall plot) [spoiler] While the choices don't affect much in terms of overall plotline, there are still changes - just not the massive branching ones of The Letter; the game places great [i]thematic[/i] emphasis on choices overall, instead of the more pragmatic, mechanical one The Letter uses, so all is not lost - it's a different approach, but it works well in tandem with the characters, and the story being told.[/spoiler]

I don't really know how to end reviews, 'cause I don't write them very often. Maybe you were drawn in by me saying it's a great game but I can't play it again for a while. If so, you're probably wondering why. I refuse to spoil the game or the plot of my own life, so all I will say on that part it that the content warnings are there for a reason - I've experienced a lot of them in media before, and most of the time, it washes over me just fine. This time, very definitively, it did not wash over me. It left me feeling borderline sick. This is not to say the writing is insensitive - it isn't. It's just that good a quality of writing that it actually had that impact on me, when I find a lot of other media doesn't.

TL;DR: It's a good horror visual novel - it's not The Letter, but it's still very good on its own terms. Play it if you haven't, it's worth it at full price.
The game isn't that interesting and the characters aren't likable at all. It's a complete 180 from The Letter.

I'll be pretty direct, the horror part of this really isn't a major factor. The best of it is the story it tells. Because when it comes down to it, I felt a lot of this, and if you give it a chance it's easy enough to do so yourself. The VN is short but it manages to give everyone such depth of feeling that you still care about them and their actions. Maybe not worth full price due to the lack of reply value and short playtime, but definitely worth getting when on sale.

the story was fantastic and i enjoyed it a lot! 

it's a shame that some of the art isn't as good as the art in The Letter was but i'd recommend it nonetheless.

I've waited this game for so long and I am happy with the end results! I'm a huge fan of The Letter and thought that this would be similar. The story is much shorter and choices do not matter as much, but still I loved this visual novel!
The quality of the game is so good. I am impressed by the voice acting, it's amazing!
Story of this game is super well made and touching. I almost cried at the end lol. However if you are sensitive to stuff such as cults, religions, abuse or sh then this is not a game for you. Stay safe!
As someone who has played the letter, this game isn't as long but still a good story!
This brought me to tears multiple times, I really enjoyed the game. As a horror fan who doesn't love jump scares, this is gold!

[H1]Yangyang's Finest[/H1]

I've played three Yangyang games now, skipping only Love Esquire. All respect to the studio, 'horny guy does zany things to seduce women' did not seem like my thing. Saint Maker is kinda fascinating to witness: it's using the simpler structure of Perfect Gold to tell a horrific, personal story in the style of the Letter. They're taking multiple past successes and stringing them together for this new project.

The results are extremely strong. Keeping the focus tight on Holly and her story helps us connect with her, get inside her head. It makes some of the false choices hit far better, and the game has a few strong surprises in store by the ending. I feel this is a very strong case for a Western 'Kinetic Novel', and while tastes are subjective, I feel this is ultimately stronger than their debut. A brisk pace, closer focus on character while maintaining the scares... it feels like this has the Letter's greatest strengths, while shedding it's weaknesses. 

That said... while I suspect it was outside the scope of this project, a little more branching could've made this game go from solid and strong to downright amazing! Can't elaborate on that without spoilers, so... [spoiler]I feel there was room narratively for a 'bad end' here. I'm not sure exactly how I'd pitch it, but with the involvement of Itadora and the Angels, Holly's fragile mental state, Gabby's survival being unclear... I feel Holly not managing to grow, focusing on what she wants to be true, could've led to a pretty cool tragic end. I like the game's ending, but branching, showing how it could've gone differently... IDK, I just feel that worked well for Perfect Gold and could've worked well here too.[/spoiler]

But don't let what's not here detract from what is. A strong horror story that's more than worth your time!
Amazing story, voice acting and art. Also has gay people yippee!!

I liked bits but overall I'd say meh.

On average I'd guess it takes somewhere between 5 - 6 hours (if you wanna see everything).

[h3]Dislikes[/h3]

* It's a very linear story.
* Highly predictable throughout to the degree that it feels almost like a paint by numbers visual horror novel.
* The vast majority of choices don't matter unless you want to see a "secret" ending.
* The beginning really drags on.

[h3]Likes[/h3]

* The full screen CGs that're meant to be creepy and on occasion happy are nice.

[h3]Last Thoughts[/h3]

Saint Maker is definitely worth the sale price that I bought it for (£3.53). I'd definitely skip it at full price. As my opening sentence suggests via the "meh" comment, the title wasn't really my cuppa but despite having more "dislikes" than "likes" nothing about this visual novel really stands out in a manner that I'd definitively say skip it or buy it other than the price.

In short, Saint Maker is a okayish short story that, when bought on sale, might entertain for a weekend/single night. I'd say that's just about good enough reason to give it a try.

I am angry I bought this game. DO I recommend this game? If you bought it for the reasons I bought it - then no. I bought it because I was a huge fan of the letter, and its horror.

This game is still a horror game, but a very different hororr game. I normally don't pay attention to trigger warnings, but I should have. This game explores some really deep, disturbing  parts of human psychology. One that I am not comfortable with. And I honestly, regret buying and playing this. And quite honestly, I am done with Yangyang mobile. This isnt what I signed up for, this isnt what I wanted, and I despise the company for showing me the stuff it does so late into the game. If it happened within the first two hours, I would have refunded.

For people who love their horrors to be filled with human psychology horror and paranormal horror, this is the game for you. For me, I only want paranormal horror. This game is too much for me, but I would be doing a disservice to the quality of the voice actors, the writers, the artists, the developer and the whole studio by not recommending this. For me however, like I said, im done with Yangyang mobile.

Much like their previous horror VN, this game gives me really uncomfortable AI gen feeling.. maybe it's just their style?

But this is where the similarities with The Letter end. Because this game is extremely linear, very short and has a really tough time with "keeping the atmosphere" because of the constant chiming of achievements just ripping you out of the scene. Thankfully the story itself is better so it has that going for itself. I knew it was a great idea to stream it on discord. 

We need more "horror" games from you guys!

Not much of a Horror story, more sad.
But 100% worth playing
Short and plain. Not as good as 'The Letter', not even close.

Saint Maker is a steaming turd of a visual novel "horror" game. The story is following a 15 year old girl with the sound of 6 year old Kylie from The Letter (a REAL horror visual novel btw). I haven't played through enough to know what the story is about but I do feel disappointment towards the game's "pick-your-choice" mechanic and I say this with quotation marks for a reason.

The company's game "The Letter" allowed you, the player or reader, to explore a story from the perspective of 7 different characters, Zachary, Luke, Hannah, Isabella, Rebecca, Marianne & Ashton. Everything was interconnected and things flowed naturally with the story. You had a lot of power as the player to determine how the story went, you could essentially save everyone or kill off all the characters if you so-wanted. Saint Maker only follows 1 character, which is disappointing on its own, but it gives you choices so it makes you think you could achieve multiple different endings right?

Well you'd be wrong. Saint Maker's story is linear, I have only played 37 minutes through the game and there would be moments presented for you to make a choice that were supposed to change the story but the game stuck to it's linear path anyways, regardless of what I, as the player, chose when presented with an option. It's shallow and presenting the player with choices that actually aren't choices, because ultimately it doesn't matter what you pick the story won't change anyway, is the company lying to it's player and that is not okay.

It's hugely disappointing since Saint Maker came after my favorite visual novel of all time, The Letter, so I had high hopes but nope. Sadly no, Saint Maker does not care about letting the player have any control and it's not like this is inherently bad. If I knew that that was the main approach the game wanted me to focus on I would be alright with it. But it is not okay to present the choices 

"I should make something up"
"I should tell her the truth"

Have the player pick "I should tell her the truth" and then have the response from the game be "No that idéa is stupid... I should make something up."

Why would you lie to your player? That is the biggest question I am asking. Have them know from the start "In this game you can make choices but they will hardly impact the story in any significant way. So it really doesn't matter what you choose.". That way they know what they are in for and won't end up feeling disappointed and lied to. The game also appears to be leaning in more towards being "supernatural" than "horror" so it's probably leaning more towards being spooky than scary. 

Final verdict, not a good game

Shorter than the letter and not much branching choices, but I got a bomb story so that's all that matters.

The story's much better than the letter in my opinion, so there's that.
This was one of the best visual novels i've ever experienced. I played the game in one sitting and It was one of the most devistating things i've ever played

TRIGGER WARNING: If you're sensitive, too young, or if you have suicidal ideations, do NOT play this game.

There is hope for you. You can beat this. Please seek help.
US: National Suicide Prevention Hotline: 800-273-8255 for English and for Spanish speakers.

This game is about youth dealing with different stages of grief due to the loss of relatives.

Achievements are easy to get, with some branches having mutually exclusive achievements (might have to save and reload before making a choice if you get an achievement soon after.

This is a relatively SHORT game from YangYang Mobile, with a tiniest hint of YURI (girl crush), The romance aspect is NOT explored in this game (like it was in Perfect Gold, another YYM title), as this is a jump-scare horror game and not a romance game.

Nice short visual novel with slight horror elements. Good voice acting and music. Would recommend this game if you are looking for something to play in a single session during a free evening or over the weekend

Holy shit. I've never played a game that made me feel THIS sad for the antagonist. By the end I was rooting for [spoiler] Adira [/spoiler]. And I never do that! Well done, and well worth the wait/price.

Like usual, I went into Saintmaker blind. I saw the cool cover art, and knew it would be some kind of religious horror and that's all I needed to pique my interest. The only horror trope that truly scares me is sacreligion due  to the fact that I was raised in such a religious home, even despite having left the religion behind, sacrilegious imagery terrifies me to this day, haha! 

They tell a relatable story about a young girl using escapism to avoid her problems brought on by a strict religious and abusive home. What she's reading is considered "satanic" so she's hastily dispatched to a convent for a dose of spiritual reformation. There, she meets another girl who was sent there for her own mysterious reason. 

It was a lot of fun, creepy, and had some heart in a few of the scenes. I enjoyed it, and if you like visual novels with a horror twist, you might too!

Breathtaking game with such well crafted story with themes of religious trauma, mental health, and questioning what is right and wrong. 

I did think that the story had more potential, explaining some of the more scary supernatural elements. However this doesn't take away from the beauty of the story

A very solid, short visual novel. I really enjoyed the story - it was captivating and genuinely engaging. The visuals, music, and voice acting were all high quality and added a lot to the experience. I especially appreciated the theme, I wish more games explored the topic of religious trauma. Holly and Adira were interesting, well-developed characters, and I definitely got invested in their stories. Unfortunately, I can't say the same for Gabby - she felt more like a narrative tool to avoid having Holly just talk to herself, rather than a meaningful part of the story.
I knew the novel was short, but I still wish it had been longer and offered more than one ending. Because of how linear it was, I had a feeling that outside of achievements, my choices didn't matter - on several occasions, Holly outright ignored the option I picked, which made the decisions feel pointless. I also would have loved to see the convent's history explored more deeply, as that part held a lot of potential but remained too vague for my taste.
Despite these flaws, I really enjoyed [i]Saint Maker[/i]. It has its weaknesses, but the strong storytelling and pretty visuals kept me engaged. With more content and branching paths, it could have been an amazing visual novel.

Saint Maker is a good psychological game, some things are easy to figure out, while others left me like WHAT.

But all in all, fantastic game, good story and the thematic is *chef kiss*
Sure as hell a good read if you're into all that catholic trauma.

i would say its a fun short game. its not too scary but it has an interesting and somewhat sad plot. 
loved the story, just wish the horror aspect was more prevalent.

7/10

While I didn't find this particularly scary, this is a competently written and generally well constructed horror adventure title. You might find it scarier than I did if you have a background that resonates with the setting of this title.
Pretty good worth the price on sale. Very linear with only two endings that are not different enough for a second play-though unfortunately.
I really enjoyed this, voice acting was great and it did a really good job of making me uneasy throughout the story. I definitely recommend this.

[b]Its a nice little VN to play in 1-2 sittings(around 6h)[/b]. I expected it to be more horror-y based on the demo, but it ended up going in more of a psychological horror route. Its also very linear [spoiler] with only 2 endings that are basically the same  one with second haveng 2 extra SGs, no bad endings either [/spoiler]. The VAs did incredible job! I was a bit muffed about Hollys VA in the first 30mins, she sounded so anime... but later, I honestly got really impressed and thats actually why I decided to write a review! :D

8/10
This is a horror-genre graphic novel where players select dialog options that affect how the grame progresses. The protagosit is a 15 year old girl, but the story is very dark and touches sensitive topics. I would not recomment it to children younger than 15. The story is also suprisingly thought-provoking and relevant. All in all, this is one of the better stories I've seen in a video game. It is even better than many books I've read.

THe game is fully voiced. That is every line of dialog is voice-acted. There is still a lot of text to read though. Unfortunately some of the dialog text is red on black, which might make it challenging to read for color-blind people. Background music is pleasant, and sound effects are fitting. Artwork is good, but is pretty limited. There are only a few backdrops that keep getting reused. I am not at all certain that dialog choices you make in the game make more than cosmetic effect on the game.

In summary, this is a farly short game, but I would definitely recommen tit for the story. I'm looking forward to the next game released by this team!

for any battle-hardened veteran fan of horror... the game is a snooze fest. 
That being said, I'm sure that many will still enjoy it. 

+ nice art 
+ decent attempt at creating a moody atmosphere
+ solid effort put in to the VA
+ cute story

- not a riveting story
- story seems drawn out at times and scenes frequently repeat with the same dialogue (I get it as a plot device but it comes off as lazy writing)

I'll recommend it, but I guess I expected more from this game.

I love YangYang visual horror novels, they always deliver amazing story, great voice acting, beautiful art, it keeps you hooked. This one was absolutely fascinating and enjoyable. Highly recommend if you feel like reading a whole story that will keep you brainstorming amidst horrifying possibilities and relatable situations.

I'm kind of disappointed for the only single ending. I wish there are more endings I can play in the game. The story is not very horror compared to the previous work, the letter. But it's not the bad story though.

A great horror VN ! The story itself is pretty simple, but the characters and the writing are just wonderfully done ! The voice acting is great, the actors did a fantastic job ! And it's gay too ! Couldn't be better ! Oh and I want to praise the devs for putting this "auto speed" option god it made reading and listening to the game a breeze, thank you so much.

Phenomenal religious and trauma horror visual novel, with bonus queer flavorings (if a bit on the subtle side, at times). There were a few typos here and there, and some of the audio clips went on *way* too long, but overall, extremely good story and well worth the price and playtime!

Grab this vn while on sale, the storyline is pretty creepy and made my heart jump several times.  Do read the trigger warning before buying this vn.  The art and soundtrack is amazing.  My only complain is the text is too small.

Fantastic Visual novel, with a great story that actually has real emotion and grips you to the end. The art and animations are well done but the writing and excellent voice acting are the real stars of this wonderful 5-6 hour experience. The themes and setting are handled with care and are identifiable to many people who have been though similar situations growing up. touching on LGBTQ and Religious trauma, and suicide. This a must play and a absolute most own in anyone's library.

The voice acting and sound design is excellent, and gives this visual novel a lot of character. It's a heavy story, difficult to get through. Well written and thoughtful. The art is unique and fits well.

An interesting choice to depict religious trauma within a male-centered and misogynistic religion with an all-female cast without much mention of the contrasting gender roles. In a way, it's bold to isolate the experience. Because of that, it really emphasized on the "mother issues/parental trauma", rather than get too deep into the critique of misogyny in the religion.

It's a beautiful coming-of-age story anyway.
I loved it and the story really gripped me in.
good night, cornelia.
Yeah it good

A quite interesting FPS game, it clearly needs a lot of polish in every way, but at it's current state the game is quite fun to play and the gameplay mechanics work nicely most of the times.

Visually the game looks quite nice and feels really smooth, on the other hand I'll say that what I think needs a lot of work is the matchmaking experience (since the times between matches feels quite long) and the amount of selectable game modes. Also I would love to see a game mode with a more classic 5v5/6v6 distribution of players.

Here you have some footage of the game: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Olh8Pjj_Vr0

Sadly it isn't a 5v5 and there are a lot of shooter games already in the market. Queue times are long and it is noticeable that there are only a few players (along with developers) that are playing this currently.

I would not recommend Xandata now. Maybe in a few updates.

Works well with Proton (Linux)!
Please don't fugg with EAC, I hope to play this game more.

As for the Early Access review:
- Please refine the controls. It feels convoluted unlike other FPS's (Overwatch, CSGO, Valo)
- Improve loading time
I really like playing the classes with the different elemental affinity combos, and the lore of Planet Xandaigdig and its cultures.

It's pretty much Destiny PvP, but stripped down. I didn't see anything original to stand itself out from Destiny PvP. 
Unfortunately I don't think I see Project Xandata lasting, unless they do something unique that you can't do in Destiny PvP. 

An example and something unique: 
- Weapon pick ups in the map like an actual arena shooter.
- Apex Legends Arenas, where you earn credits in each round to strategically buy weapons/abilities. (Because there are meta build classes that just dominates)

Secret 6 kinda dropped the ball by making this game poorly optimized for low end PCs

too many Filipinos play trash games like Valorant and LoL purely out of hype as well as peer pressure

a Pinoy shooter like this would have appealed to a wider audience in the country alone.

i enjoyed it and i do recommend it.

but these guys dropped the Ball.


a single player campaign would have been nice though
Not gonna lie, this game is pretty awesome! The devs are super involved with the game and are actually really cool people! I'm excited for when this game pops off and has full lobby's!

When I queued for a match, I have to wait quite a while only to get clapped by a bunch of pros. Also, For a game with cartoon graphics, it does require a REAL good rig. I'm on a 1050.
It overall feels nice and the handling and movement are comfortably similar to other games making it a good choice in my opinion for new PC gamers

Amazing game with fun game play. However you can't even play the game because not a single other living person in the universe has this game so there a just no servers. Even if you are so how lucky enough to find a game it is filled with the most stupid ai I have ever had the unfortunate experience to play with. The custom lobby's don't work yet so if you want to play a game with your friends you will constantly have your experience ruined by the ai. this game has lots of potential as long as other people play it but the ai are going to make every poor soul that tries this game leave immediately so that will NEVER happen.

Pretty good game with dynamic plays and a lot of playstyles to choose from. Choice in abilities and weapons keep the gameplay snazzy, and there's a lot of movement options with a high skill ceiling for players to strive for. Devs are very active in updating the game and listening to player feedback, so it has my vote

Even for me who's not that great with guns, I can rely on the Magitech System and some weapon perks to still make an impact in the various game modes.

A bit of a learning curve but after 3-5 games i got the hang of the moving, sliding jumping.
A lot of work still needed but definitely lots of potential to grow.

Comical and fun to play with friends! There are a lot of skill combinations that can cater to different types of players. Movement feels great and firing weapons as well. The customization feels just right and the visual effects are "chef's kiss"!

game has a lot of promise.. the main value prop is an overwatch/destiny type arena affair without the shield bloat of OW and more mechanically focused. Theres a few things holding it back at the moment, mainly the amount of uncounterable power abilities and weapons. The gun fights feel nice until these abilities come out and put an anti-climactic end to them. If they retune some of the cheesier perks and abilities and put them in some kind of moba-type economy where you choose which to unlock over the course of a match they could probably scrape together a decent competitive mode.

Still on early access the gameplay is somewhat confusing but it is a mix type of breed of fps. I hope it will progress later on. Will keep on playing until the game matches improve! Kudos to the creators. Para sa XANDAIGDIG

Been eyeing for this games since i saw it at a convention, way back 2015, to my disappointment its still the same as before, it took a lot of time, so i was expecting new elements within the game, New weapons, Maps, and Class, sure its great that each weapon has its different customization but currently its limited cause we have few weapons yet, but id say its smooth and natural once you get the hang of the mechanics, just need to improve a lot

- Add new weapons, what we use today is the same old guns we had last 2015 at beta. 


-Tone down or change the announcer, as if she trying hard to copy TF2's announcer, her remarks is so embarrassing and cringey 

-Add more class, limited to the 3 gets boring eventually 

- make it 5 v 5 or more, more people more chaos~ 

- guns should be purchasable so that there is weight in the game that you cant just go yolo like in counter strike

its a shame, i was expecting a lot of changes since 2015, but turns out it still the same, this game has potential, and you have to step up since there are a lot of FPS game in the market, or eventually people will get bored.

Sadly there are not enough players to get into a match but the game looks very promising so I'm going to counteract all those memelords who wrote negative reviews because writing "LOL" as a review is not only dumb but also mean without any reason.

(It's weird to me that there's no marketing behind this game. They should've collected hundreds of wishlist entries before releasing even an early access version imo. This is why people are calling EA + full release "the two releases of a game".)

Anyways, the music SLAPS, the movement feels nice, the gameplay feels deep and polished. Would really like to test it in PvP.

If it wasn't closing down and had a playerbase, I'd recommend it.

233 peak. the most people that were on this game a month ago were about like 24 upon checking the steam charts. This is an overly saturated market. Trust me, I want a new FPS as much as the next person. Overwatch, Apex, and pubg are not made people like me.
But unless this game gets some kind of advertisement or player boom. There isn't much to do here, the mechanics and performance is solid. The graphics are a bit generic, but from what I can tell. I honestly love the game and it has a strong potential but due to lack of players its very hard to find matches.
Good game despite needing more polished. More people need to take notice of this game

I think this game has got a future and that this game is generally really fun and addicting but, I have a handful of additions I would like to see:


Something new that is just going to help the game in a big way, an example could be, ways to decrease cooldowns (in a game) making it so every time you get a kill your power cooldown get reduced by 5-10 seconds.

More variety of weapon choices; eg, New power weapons (a grenade launcher/sword perhaps), just more weapons in general and either make less weapon perks or balance them. This also leads onto my next point.

Balancing of weapons, classes and abilities; some are just a bit too powerful and I think a good way to fix this on the abilities side of things is to make them more situational, I do not personally have an idea on this but other more experienced players may be better to talk to on this subject.

Server increase, I believe would help this game in huge aspects as lag and, disconnects is turning players away from this game and I believe that it would help the game gain recognition.

The performance in this game I believe is in a good place so I wont say anything against it.

To finish this off I would like to add that i do realise that this game is small and, the one of newest games that has any recognition right now. That the development team are quite small and this post is just what I would like to see in the games future. I also realise that I myself have little to know time in this game but, I am planning to put more and more time and effort into this game as I can see a bright future for the game, the items on this list are thing I myself would like to see improve/added onto the game. People with more experience can make comments on this post to add more opinions to this post.

Game's just the right fit for me. Fast-paced, in-your-face FPS, probably even faster than many FPS's out there. With this in mind, I feel like it being "faster" than other games does pose a few problems. Here's a pros and cons list.

[b]PROS[/b]
[list] 
[*]Easy customization of perks and classes right out of the box, no need to grind for weapons
[*]Satisfying gunplay and skills/abilities/ultimates
[*]It's free-to-play
[/list]

[b]CONS[/b]
[list]
[*]Close-range fights can get very confusing really fast, aided with the fact that EVERYONE has [b] double jump [/b].
[*]There are some maps with too many nooks and crannies [i](looking at you, Wellsping Biotech)[/i], you can just camp in different hiding spots with the shotty and get easy kills.
[*]If you're on the other end/about-to-be victim of the camping player, it's almost impossible to clear the entire area without getting sneaked up on. [i] Especially true for Overcharge. [/i]
[*]General performance: Many hiccups. Still in early access, so understandable. Hoping to see big improvements.
[*]120 hz cap
[*]Everyone can run fast and jump mid-air but [b]can't reload while sprinting??[/b]
[/list]

I may seem mad on my statement above but I genuinely like the game and would like the game to succeed and I will still recommend the game. I just hope this gets polished even further in the future and I will edit this review whenever I get to play this game sometime again.

I barely make any reviews, but this one really turned my brains gears and all for the wrong reasons. 

Love the game, played it in the last Early access(?) and I though that it was really good and really lived up to the hype for a Filipino shooter. HOWEVER, after coming back recently with the game now available for everyone, I'm just sad that it just feels like something went wrong and how it just wasn't as fun as before. Of course there are some things that I really liked compare to before like the addition of new elements and some balance changes, but I have some reasons on why it's not as fun as before. Like the player base is a bit too low compared to before where it'll usually just takes a couple of seconds to join a match and a full lobby too, compared to now where it'll take me almost half an hour (at worst) to get in a match, only to find myself playing a 1v1 against a hacker (already? seriously?!). Even at the end of the match I don't even have the chance to report said hacker because Player Options won't even open. I mean it's completely understandable since it is an Early Access version and bugs would be a common thing, I'm partially a game developer myself so I completely understand these things, but things like I can't even report is where I draw the line in terms of quality of life in both the community and the game itself, ESPECIALLY in an online Multiplayer game. 

I have massive respect for Secret 6 and how I absolutely adore the assets they make for other companies. Project Xandata is such a beautiful game and it truly has lots of potential to be one of the next big thing especially in the Philippines. With a currently developing selection of diverse characters, I really do think it can still be better as to what it currently is now.

In review on the gameplay, here are my honest thoughts:
Characters
Agent - still as broken as ever and definitely needs some balance changes
Marksman - still decent despite being a bit stronger than I remember
Juggernaut - still the underdog of the trio. Honestly for such a tough name, he is pretty fragile and doesn't really scream JUGGERNAUT if it's just gonna die mid fight. HP values felt low too compared to before, maybe increasing the values is what he needs just so it can actually live up to the name. Currently it plays like a glass cannon than someone who goes in hard on the enemy team with his whole kit and how he definitely should be played. Damage on his abilities felt weak as well compared to before and I don't find it sufficient enough for him to stay in a fight either as a skirmisher or a frontliner. 

Weapons
The weapons are the same as ever as far as testing goes. No comments so far other than maybe just going for an infinte ammo for secondary too already instead of just locking it to the Primary. The secondaries are not exactly shining in terms of usage, when you can easily kill players with the Amorsolo when you have god like aim or a hacker.

Abilities
Still the same as ever, except for the new elements, which I admit is really really fun especially Juggernaut's Magwayan well and it really fits the character too with the new ultimate.

Maps
The design is great on all maps and really has a certain charm to it that I really find breathtaking and really unique. 

User Interface
UI could use more work especially in the Main Menu. Personally, I think it looks a bit bland and looks more like an online Mobile game menu than an online PC game. The character showcase is a nice idea and really reminds me a lot of overwatch and apex legends, but the placements of the text in the interface just bothers me a bit.

Overall Aesthetic
Love the design on the whole game and how the color palette on each map and choice of colors on the characters are so well executed. Leave it to Secret 6 to provide quality assets for any game they get involved in. My only wish I guess, especially me as a mega man fan and Project Xandata characters are based on the mega man design aesthetic, maybe add a bit more bulk or sharper edges to the armor of the characters just to make it look as unique as possible than fully embracing the mega man aesthetic. Juggernaut would be the perfect example, make it's default armor template bulkier so it can actually feel like the character is indeed a Juggernaut not just in name but in character as well.

Closing thoughts:
Like I said, the game really has potential to be the next big thing, but at it's current state it definitely feels like it's going to take a while. The game design was well thought of, it just needs lots more polishing in both technical and visual department. I would still come back to this game that's for sure since I'm a big supporter for this game as well as Secret 6, but with the current build, I'm gonna have to pass for now. Looking forward to the future updates and I really hope there is going to be big changes as well.

For being developed by a relatively small independent team, Project Xandata (PXD) strives to bring someting to the fast-paced mobility shooter genre that feels familiar.

(Played CBT, OBT, and EA)
